title: [l70-doctor-jsonl-hardening] ticks_punted probe must tolerate malformed dispatch-log rows
description:
## Problem

The L70 doctor signal is wired, but the live `ticks-punted-probe.sh --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json` path fails when `.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl` contains a non-JSON plaintext row.

Current evidence:

- `.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl:956` contains `[2026-05-04T03:41:49Z] dispatched workforce-lane-a to flywheel:p4`
- `/tmp/flywheel-7lby-ticks-punted-probe.json` shows the probe aborted with `jq: parse error: Invalid numeric literal at line 956, column 15`
- `/tmp/flywheel-7lby-doctor-l70.json` shows doctor still exposes `ticks_punted_count=0`, but `l70_chain_state.warning=ticks_punted_probe_invalid_json`

## Why This Matters

`ticks_punted_count` is the L70 mechanical enforcement surface. A single malformed historical dispatch-log row should not degrade the entire doctor signal.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `.flywheel/scripts/ticks-punted-probe.sh --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json` succeeds even if `.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl` contains malformed historical rows.
2. Probe output includes a machine-readable malformed-row count and line references, or writes those rows to a quarantine evidence path.
3. `flywheel-loop doctor --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json` no longer reports `l70_chain_state.warning=ticks_punted_probe_invalid_json` solely because of one malformed row.
4. A regression fixture covers mixed JSONL plus plaintext dispatch-log rows.
5. The probe still fails when all rows are unreadable or when the log file itself cannot be read.

## Doctrine

- L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT
- L71 VALIDATE-AND-REDISPATCH-DISCIPLINE
- feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface

## Parent

Blocks closure proof for `flywheel-7lby`.
close_reason: VALIDATED/DOCUMENTED/SURFACED: ticks-punted probe now tolerates mixed JSONL/plaintext dispatch logs using jq -R try/fromjson, exposes malformed_row_count and malformed_rows line refs, still fails all-malformed/unreadable logs, and doctor l70_chain_state.warning no longer reports ticks_punted_probe_invalid_json from historical malformed rows. evidence=/tmp/flywheel-7lby1-live-probe.json,/tmp/flywheel-7lby1-doctor-l70.json,.flywheel/receipts/flywheel-7lby1-receipt.md tests=PASS commits=1 beads_updated=flywheel-7lby.1:closed no_bead_reason=no_new_gap fuckups_logged=none

title: [orch-no-punt-chain-fixture-hardening] synthetic tick must survive missing optional helpers
description:
## Problem

`tests/orch-no-punt-chain.sh` does reach the intended L70 chain decision in the synthetic repo, but the full test fails later when `.flywheel/flywheel-loop-tick` serializes optional helper output into the final dispatch-log JSON.

Current evidence:

- `/tmp/flywheel-7lby-orch-no-punt-chain-x.err`
- `/tmp/flywheel-7lby-driver-debug.err`
- `/tmp/orch-no-punt-debug.35126/repo/.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl`

The synthetic run selected `PHASE=BEADS`, `PHASE_REASON=plan_or_docs_without_beads`, and an L70 chain decision with `chain_required=true` and `chained=true`. The failure occurs later when `--argjson frozen_detector` receives invalid or multi-object text after optional pre-tick helper fallback handling.

## Why This Matters

The core L70 branch appears implemented, but the acceptance fixture cannot prove it end-to-end. Missing optional helpers in a synthetic repo should produce one degraded JSON object, not poison the final receipt write.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `bash tests/orch-no-punt-chain.sh` passes in a synthetic repo without copying every optional helper script.
2. `.flywheel/flywheel-loop-tick` normalizes optional pre-tick helper output to a single JSON object before passing it through any `jq --argjson` call.
3. Missing `frozen-pane-detector.sh` yields one structured degraded/warn object.
4. The final synthetic dispatch-log row remains valid JSON.
5. The test asserts the L70 `DISPATCH -> BEADS` same-tick chain path and the callback `next_phase` chain path separately.

## Doctrine

- L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT
- L71 VALIDATE-AND-REDISPATCH-DISCIPLINE
- feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface

## Parent

Blocks fixture proof for `flywheel-7lby`.
close_reason: did=2/2 test_passes_without_optional_helpers=true output_normalized_before_argjson=true tests_passing=true commits=1 — both 7lby fixture-hardening gates met; unblocks 8kvt parent rework

title: [data-backed-deferral-core] implement predicate and question-shape detector
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/data-backed-deferral-guard-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-bbts
Plan predecessor: flywheel-28v

## Goal

Implement the reusable data-backed-deferral predicate and question-shape detector so the flywheel tick can decide when telemetry already implies action and a Joshua-facing question is a violation.

## Scope

- Add or complete `flywheel-loop data-backed-deferral-check --signals <file> --json` and/or the backing helper under `.flywheel/scripts/`.
- Parse the seven signal families from plan lines 34-48: ntm health, ready beads, recent tick history, doctor, PageRank, pane scrollback, and dispatch log.
- Implement the base condition and `data_alignment_score >= 3` predicate from plan lines 50-85.
- Implement question-shape detection against only the last five non-empty lines using the regex set from plan lines 210-236.
- Implement safe-question exceptions for headings, quoted user questions followed by action, and blocked callbacks with `human_question`.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `bash tests/data-backed-deferral-guard.sh` passes for existing fixtures.
2. `flywheel-loop data-backed-deferral-check --signals tests/fixtures/data-backed-deferral/row185-catch.json --json | jq -e '.violation == true and .code == "orch_question_when_data_clear" and .suggested_action != null'` passes.
3. `flywheel-loop data-backed-deferral-check --signals tests/fixtures/data-backed-deferral/ntm116-catch.json --json | jq -e '.violation == true and (.signals_aligned | length >= 3)'` passes.
4. `flywheel-loop data-backed-deferral-check --signals tests/fixtures/data-backed-deferral/missing-datum-allow.json --json | jq -e '.violation == false and .override_reason == "evidence_missing"'` passes.
5. A regex fixture or test assertion covers all 11 minimum question-shape patterns from the plan and proves the broad final-line question rule does not scan entire documentation bodies.
6. `rg -n "data_alignment_score|orch_question_when_data_clear|DATA_BACKED_DEFERRAL_DETECTED" ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop .flywheel/scripts tests` shows the shipped predicate and test coverage.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: fixture tests prove positive catches, ambiguity allows, and regex boundaries.
- DOCUMENTED: help output or inline command description names the plan path and predicate inputs.
- SURFACED: command JSON includes stable `violation`, `code`, `signals_aligned`, `suggested_action`, and `override_reason` fields for downstream doctor/tick consumers.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L66 reinforcement via plan lines 20 and 32.
- AGENTS.md L70, L71, L80 for chain-forward, validate-and-redispatch, and DID/DIDNT/GAPS receipt discipline.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.

## Out Of Scope

- No hard pre-output hook; Option A remains future-only until a real interception substrate exists.
- No source mutation outside the flywheel repo.
- No dispatch-template changes; those belong to the dispatch lint bead.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented data-backed-deferral predicate/check command; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-core]`.
close_reason: field-name aligned: emit code=orch_question_when_data_clear per bead spec; tests=5/5 passed; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-core]

title: [data-backed-deferral-doctor] surface saves and violations in doctor JSON
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/data-backed-deferral-guard-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-bbts
Plan predecessor: flywheel-28v

## Goal

Surface data-backed-deferral state in doctor/tick receipts so the system can track saves, violations, and allowed questions over time.

## Scope

- Add a doctor/tick JSON section for data-backed-deferral counts.
- Count recent saves from `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-saves.jsonl`.
- Count recent overrides from `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-overrides.jsonl`.
- Report current tick violations and last suggested action without echoing sensitive pane text beyond necessary redacted/summary fields.
- Preserve existing doctor JSON compatibility.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `flywheel-loop doctor --repo /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json | jq -e '.data_backed_deferral'` passes.
2. Doctor JSON exposes numeric `saves_count`, `overrides_count`, and `violations_count` fields.
3. Doctor JSON reports `status` as `ok|warn|fail` and uses `warn` when recent `orch_question_when_data_clear` violations exist.
4. `bash tests/flywheel-loop-core.sh` or a dedicated doctor test proves the field exists with empty/missing receipt files and with fixture receipt files.
5. `rg -n "data_backed_deferral|saves_count|overrides_count|violations_count" ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop .flywheel/scripts tests README.md .flywheel/canonical-paths.txt` finds surfaced field, tests, and registry/docs entry.
6. The field does not echo raw secrets, pane scrollback, or full draft output; tests or code review proof show only counts and redacted summaries.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: doctor tests cover empty files and fixture rows.
- DOCUMENTED: README or canonical paths identifies the doctor field and receipt files.
- SURFACED: `flywheel-loop doctor --json` provides a stable machine-readable signal.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L71: validation discipline.
- AGENTS.md L80: DID/DIDNT/GAPS callback reporting can reference this field as evidence.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.

## Out Of Scope

- No implementation of the predicate itself.
- No dispatch-template lint.
- No promotion to hard-block status.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `surfaced data-backed-deferral doctor signal; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-doctor]`.
close_reason: DID: surfaced data-backed-deferral doctor signal with saves_count, overrides_count, violations_count, ok/warn/fail status ladder, and redacted last_suggested_action; wired normal doctor JSON and added doctrine note plus four regression tests. DIDNT: touch dispatch.md, dispatch-template.md, lint scripts, root docs, .github, ecosystem-polish-scaffold, or bin/flywheel-loop dispatcher. GAPS: test fixtures call the invariant directly for threshold speed; live doctor JSON probe verified normal wiring. Evidence: tests/test_doctor_data_backed_deferral_clean_state.sh,tests/test_doctor_data_backed_deferral_warn_threshold.sh,tests/test_doctor_data_backed_deferral_fail_threshold.sh,tests/test_doctor_data_backed_deferral_no_raw_pane_text.sh; commits: 5726bf8, 7c3efb5.

title: [doctrine] callback-evidence-discrepancy trauma class — worker advertises evidence path but doesnt write it
description:
## Source
skillos:1 caught flywheel-kvt8v callback (CloudyMill) advertised evidence=.flywheel/receipts/flywheel-kvt8v/evidence.md but that path does not exist on disk. Only history JSONs landed (skillos 611KB/1330 entries, mobile-eats 980KB, alpsinsurance 0B empty).

Pattern: worker callback evidence field claims a file path; path is not materialized; close still proceeds because validate-callback doesn't probe disk for the path.

## Why this matters
- Callback evidence is a CONTRACT, not a label
- Joshua + skillos rely on evidence.md files for re-audit and verification
- Silent path-non-existence creates trust drift
- skillos called this out by ACTUALLY checking disk — flywheel-side validate-callback should do same

## Acceptance gates
AG1: Audit the last 50 closed beads — count how many advertised evidence paths that don't exist on disk. Quantify drift.
AG2: Patch validate-callback.py: when callback contains `evidence=<path>` field, validate the path exists. Reject close if missing (with override flag for path-is-tmp-dir cases).
AG3: Patch dispatch-template.md callback contract: explicitly require evidence file written BEFORE br close, not advertised then forgotten.
AG4: Audit kvt8v specifically — is the work actually done despite missing evidence file?
AG5: Cross-link with worker-close-requires-git-commit (L143-class) — same family of close-discipline gaps.

## Cross-references
- skillos:1 callback-evidence-discrepancy ACK 2026-05-09
- L143 WORKER-CLOSE-REQUIRES-GIT-COMMIT
- Phase 4 evidence_redacted contract (flywheel-dwavb)
close_reason: Implementation complete (5/5 ACs per pane 2 worker). validate-callback.py + tests + dispatch-template all updated for disk-check on advertised evidence paths. Commits 958aaae + 9ca6461. evidence_redacted=yes (Phase 4 contract self-applied). Close was blocked on .beads contention with pane 3 gb54d.1. Closing from orch per L70.

title: [ntm-audit] verify 86 USE-row callsites — close the audit-claim-vs-reality gap
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07
joshua_directive=2026-05-07T~22Z "audit and address ALL NTM surfaces"

## Why
NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md classifies 108 surfaces: 86 USE / 8 ISSUE / 8 WRAP / 6 EXCLUDED. The 38 wire-in beads addressed the gap rows; the 86 USE rows were classified "already wired or queued" as a CLAIM, not verified by callsite count. This is the same evidence-pack discipline x6ok8 just shipped for callbacks: classifications without proof are upper-bound signals.

The /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/plans/ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07/06-GAP-AUDIT-SPREADSHEET.md from earlier today partially addressed this but flagged unverified-USE rows without resolving. This bead closes that audit gap.

## Acceptance
1. For each of 86 USE rows in NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md, run K=10 socraticode + grep against /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel for actual ntm <surface> callsites
2. Three-way classify each:
   - VERIFIED-USE: ≥3 callsites in flywheel scripts (real wire-in)
   - CLAIMED-USE-NOT-WIRED: 0 callsites in flywheel (classification was aspirational)
   - LATENT-USE: 1-2 callsites (wired-but-thin; flag for monitoring)
3. For each CLAIMED-USE-NOT-WIRED row, propose follow-up bead: either wire it or reclassify
4. For each LATENT-USE row, document the single use-case + why it doesn't yet need fleet-wide wiring
5. Output: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/plans/ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07/11-USE-ROW-VERIFICATION.md`
6. Cross-update NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md with verification status column (added per row)
7. Compliance pack on close per post-x6ok8 contract

## Hard rules
- socraticode K≥10 per surface (NEVER fewer; the inventory classification was already prose-driven, this audit must be data-driven)
- callsite counts measured via `grep -rn "ntm <surface>" /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/` + socraticode codebase_search
- NO patches to scripts; this is audit-only
- NO new beads created here; recommendations only (orchestrator files them)
- No L107 conflicts: write to 11-* path only after lsof check

## Cross-refs
- NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md (the canonical 108-row classification)
- 06-GAP-AUDIT-SPREADSHEET.md (today's earlier partial audit, surfaced this gap)
- L126 evidence-pack-replaces-self-grade (mirror discipline: claims need evidence)

title: [autoloop-p3-controlled-executor] add whitelisted tiny actions and no-op ladder
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/autonomous-loop-2026-04-30.md`

## Scope

Implement Phase 3 controlled execution expansion: `auto_spawn=true` for score
`>=100` and tiny whitelisted local actions.

## Required Behavior

- Keep `auto_spawn=false` by default until preceding Phase 2/3 gates are green.
- Add config-gated `auto_spawn=true` for score `>=100`.
- Add tiny action executor with only the plan's whitelisted actions:
  `run_baseline_validation` and `summarize_dirty_worktree`.
- Enforce cost classes L0-L4 and require explicit Joshua approval for L4.
- Implement no-op escalation ladder: 2 warn, 3 force replan artifact, 4 halt.
- Implement the 10-step "nothing to do" decision tree for executor selection.

## Acceptance Gates

1. Config default keeps `auto_spawn=false`.
2. Score `>=100` fixture with `auto_spawn=true` produces an auto-spawn plan but
   never exceeds one action per run.
3. Non-whitelisted action fixture fails closed with reason code.
4. L4 action fixture requires explicit approval and does not execute.
5. No-op streak fixtures produce warn, force-replan artifact, and halt states at
   streaks 2, 3, and 4.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Implemented Phase 3 controlled executor; tests=<cmd>; whitelisted_actions=<list>`.

## Out Of Scope

Arbitrary source edits, deploy/publish actions, model API calls from launchd, or
multi-worker swarms without a separate bead/mission lane.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: whitelist, cost-class, no-op, and auto-spawn fixtures.
- DOCUMENTED: allowed actions and approval rules are documented.
- SURFACED: executor outcomes appear in status/doctor/digest surfaces.
close_reason: Phase 3 controlled executor shipped: lib/autoloop-executor.sh + bin/flywheel-autoloop extension + 8 tests covering default no-spawn, high-score one-action cap, non-whitelist fail-closed, L4 approval-required, 3 no-op streak states (warn/force-replan/halt), 10-step decision tree. whitelist_count=2 (run_baseline_validation, summarize_dirty_worktree), cost_class_levels=5 (L0-L4). socraticode K=5, 2 commits.

title: /flywheel:recover-pane <session> <pane> — recovery ladder slash command
description:
## Goal
Wrap the recovery ladder (probe → interrupt → respawn+relaunch) in a single slash command operators can invoke when a pane is detected as FROZEN_SUSPECT.

## Recovery ladder (verified today on skillos:0.1)
1. PROBE: 'ntm activity <session> --json' → confirm state=THINKING|GENERATING AND velocity=0 AND duration > 120s
2. CAPTURE: tmux capture-pane -t <session>:0.<pane> -p -S -3000 > /tmp/<session>_pane<N>_pre_recover_<ts>.txt
3. SOFT: tmux send-keys -t <session>:0.<pane> Escape (try first; codex spinner says 'esc to interrupt')
4. INTERRUPT: tmux send-keys -t <session>:0.<pane> C-c (if Escape fails)
5. RE-PROBE after 5s: if still THINKING+velocity=0, escalate
6. RESPAWN: ntm respawn <session> --panes=<pane> --force (kills the agent, drops to shell)
7. RELAUNCH: tmux send-keys -t <session>:0.<pane> '<agent-launch-command>' Enter
   - Codex default: codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -m gpt-5.5
   - cc default: read from ~/.config/ntm/config.toml [agents.commands.claude]
8. VERIFY: ntm activity --json shows state=WAITING within 10s

## Acceptance criteria
- /flywheel:recover-pane <session> <pane> exists and prints each ladder step + outcome
- Ladder halts on first success
- Pane history captured before any destructive step
- Agent type detected via ntm health (cc vs cod) → correct relaunch command chosen
- Codex relaunch uses --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox + correct model from ntm config defaults
- After recover, runs ntm activity --json one more time and reports new state
- Logs each invocation to .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl with action=recover_pane

## Cross-references
- Sister bead flywheel-terc (frozen-spinner detector that triggers this command)
- Today's recovery proof: skillos:0.1 wedged 4m+ on 'Working' spinner with queued '› Summarize recent commits' — recovered via respawn+relaunch
- Memory: feedback_pane_state_ntm_health.md (correction: ntm activity is primary)
close_reason: /flywheel:recover-pane slash command spec done. /tmp/recov_pane_command_design.md + /tmp/recov_pane_command.md (the spec). 8-step ladder, 6 modes, 5 tests, canonical-cli-scoping all 6 PASS, ladder=yes.

title: [learn-bead-quality-mining] worker did/didnt/gaps callback contract + auto-gap-bead creation + audit mining baked into /flywheel:learn
description:
[learn-bead-quality-mining] worker did/didnt/gaps callback contract + auto-gap-bead creation + audit mining baked into /flywheel:learn

## Trauma class
closed-bead-quality-drift — workers ship "DONE evidence=... tests=PASS" but acceptance gates not validated; today flywheel-15dg closed with 66GB corpus RED (Phase 4 prod compaction never ran), flywheel-2uin closed with AG6 NOT_WIRED, flywheel-useh stayed P0 OPEN while 4 newer beads filed on top. Joshua flagged: "i'm still not seeing much energy placed into flagging and beading large scripts for breakup", "we cannot let unfinished work sit there", "this needs to be a repeatable mining process baked into our systems - /flywheel:learn".

## Meadows leverage
- #5 RULES — change closure rule from "worker says DONE" to "worker says DID/DIDNT/GAPS, gaps become beads, then close"
- #6 INFORMATION FLOWS — promote the latent comparison "AG list vs evidence artifacts" into a doctor signal that fires every tick

## Goal
Three-part discipline change:

### Part A: Worker callback contract (NEW)
Every worker `/flywheel:worker-tick` callback MUST include:
- DONE: list of completed acceptance gates with evidence path
- DIDNT: list of acceptance gates not addressed + reason (out_of_scope|deferred|blocked|misread)
- GAPS: list of newly-discovered work items not in original AG list
- For each entry in DIDNT and GAPS, worker creates a fix-bead via `br create` BEFORE sending callback (and includes those bead IDs in the callback envelope)

Updated callback envelope shape:
```
DONE <bead-id> task_id=<id>
  did=<n_completed>/<n_total_ags>
  didnt=<bead-id-list>          # gap-beads worker filed for unaddressed AGs
  gaps=<bead-id-list>            # gap-beads worker filed for newly-discovered work
  evidence=<path>
  tests=PASS|FAIL|SKIPPED
  callback_delivery_verified=true
```

### Part B: /flywheel:learn --bead-quality-mining mode
New mode runs every tick. For each closed bead in last 48h:
1. Parse "Acceptance gates" / "AG1..AGn" from bead description
2. For each AG, derive expected artifact (file path, doctor signal name, test name) using regex + skill cross-ref
3. Probe: artifact exists? test passes? doctor signal present?
4. If any AG unverified → file fix-bead `<orig-id>.audit-gap` automatically (P0 if orig was P0, else P1)
5. Mark orig with audit_status (full|partial|gap_pending) via br update --notes
6. doctor signal `closed_bead_audit_pending_count` → fail readiness if >2

### Part C: Doctor signal closed_bead_audit_status
- closed_bead_audit_pending_count: count of recently-closed beads with no audit verdict yet
- closed_bead_audit_gap_count: count of closed beads where audit found unverified AGs
- audit_gap_top_classes: top trauma classes (e.g. "doctor_signal_not_wired", "phase_b_unfinished", "tests_skipped")

## Acceptance gates

AG1: ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/_shared/dispatch-template.md updated with did/didnt/gaps contract — workers MUST include all 3 sections in callback
AG2: ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/worker-tick.md (or skill SKILL.md) updated with worker self-audit checklist:
  - "Before sending callback, for each AG in bead description, write 1-line evidence."
  - "For unaddressed AGs, file fix-bead via br create with parent_bead=<orig-id>"
  - "For newly-discovered work, file gap-bead via br create with parent_bead=<orig-id> tag=gap"
AG3: .flywheel/scripts/bead-quality-mining.sh exists — runs over last 48h closures, parses AGs, verifies, files fix-beads
AG4: doctor signals wired: closed_bead_audit_pending_count, closed_bead_audit_gap_count, audit_gap_top_classes
AG5: tests/bead-quality-mining.sh covers fixtures: closure-with-full-evidence (audit pass) / closure-with-missing-AG (auto-files fix-bead) / closure-with-doctor-not-wired (auto-files fix-bead) / closure-with-tests-skipped (auto-files fix-bead) / worker-already-filed-gaps (idempotent — doesn't double-file)
AG6: /flywheel:learn skill SKILL.md gains --bead-quality-mining flag + cron-on-tick documentation
AG7: orchestrator-validation-discipline skill cross-references new contract
AG8: AGENTS.md L## entry — "L## CLOSED-BEAD-AUDIT-MINING" sibling of L71 (validate-and-redispatch)
AG9: ft04 propagation — sync-canonical-doctrine.sh covers new probe + skill update fleet-wide
AG10: BACK-MINE the last 48h: run bead-quality-mining.sh against current closed beads, file all gap-beads found (this proves the mining works AND clears today's specific debt)

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: tests pass; back-mine produces N gap-beads where N matches manual audit count
- DOCUMENTED: SKILL.md + AGENTS.md L## + dispatch-template + worker-tick + canonical-paths
- SURFACED: 3 doctor signals + flywheel:status reference + auto-bead promotion

## Skills cited
- orchestrator-validation-discipline (primary extension)
- testing-conformance-harnesses (4-fixture discipline)
- flywheel-doctor-author (3 doctor signals)
- canonical-cli-scoping (--bead-quality-mining flag pattern)
- gate-truth-separation (audit verdict ≠ closure)

## Why P0
- Compounding debt: every tick that closes beads without audit adds drift
- Joshua-side detection delay = days; mining surfaces it within minutes
- Today's flywheel-useh / flywheel-15dg-compaction / flywheel-2uin gaps would have been auto-flagged by this system
- Companion to L70 (no-punt) and L71 (validate-and-redispatch) — completes the validation envelope

## Memory siblings
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_validate_redispatch_foundational_discipline.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_orchestrator_validates_callbacks.md

## Priority: P0
## Estimate: 4 dispatches (~10 worker-min)
notes: audit_status=partial; did=10/10 reported but AG9 (AGENTS.md L-rule) NOT WIRED — gap-bead 7yic.1 carries forward. Back-mine produced 22 gap-beads on prior closures: cwov.1, g9mi.1, 2zsj.1, o7dq.1-3, f589.1-2, ft04.1, m5kg.1-2, susm.1, vso8.1-2, dw5w.1-2, kscr.1-2, hf58.1, zgo3.1, syef.1-2. doctor signals working (closed_bead_audit_pending_count=106, closed_bead_audit_gap_count=21, top_class=doctor_signal_not_wired x8). Mining system functional and self-catching.

title: [autoloop-p2-dispatch] add bounded ntm dispatch and callback reaping
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/autonomous-loop-2026-04-30.md`

## Scope

Implement Phase 2 `dispatch` subcommand for bounded autonomous worker dispatch.

## Required Behavior

- Pick top queue item with score `>=80`.
- Write task packet to `/tmp/flywheel-autoloop-<id>.md`.
- Send via `ntm send` using canonical NTM transport only.
- Append to dispatch log with repo, bead/task, score, packet path, pane/session,
  and idempotency key.
- Enforce `max_autonomous_ticks_per_run=1`, `auto_spawn=false`, and budget gates.
- Require closeout receipt validation for dispatched agents.
- Add callback reaping for pending autoloop dispatches.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `flywheel-autoloop dispatch --dry-run --json` reports intended queue item and
   packet path without sending.
2. Score `<80` fixture does not dispatch and writes a cooldown/skip receipt.
3. Score `>=80` fixture emits a task packet with repo, acceptance gates, and
   callback envelope.
4. Applied dispatch appends a dispatch-log row and uses `ntm send`, not direct
   terminal transport.
5. Callback reaper validates a fixture closeout receipt and marks dispatch
   complete or failed.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Implemented Phase 2 dispatch; tests=<cmd>; dispatch_log=<path>; packet_fixture=<path>`.

## Out Of Scope

Auto-spawn, multi-worker swarms, L4 side effects, external deploy/publish actions.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: dry-run, score threshold, packet, send, and reaper fixtures.
- DOCUMENTED: packet schema and callback contract are stable.
- SURFACED: dispatch-log and reaper outcomes feed doctor/status in sibling bead.
close_reason: Implemented Phase 2 dispatch; tests=set -o pipefail; bash tests/flywheel-autoloop-canonical-cli.sh | tee /tmp/flywheel-8ehk-tests.txt; dispatch_log=/tmp/flywheel-8ehk-acceptance/state/dispatch-log.jsonl; packet_fixture=/tmp/flywheel-autoloop-flywheel-8ehk-packet-fixture.md

title: Enforce dispatch topology and callback contract
description:
## Goal
Patch the flywheel dispatch path so worker prompts cannot bypass topology lookup, canonical callback contracts, L50/L51 checks, or NTM-only transport.

## Context
Origins: fuckup rows dispatch-bypasses-flywheel-dispatch-skill, callback-pane-wrong-no-topology-read, tmux-capture-bypasses-ntm-health, dispatch-file-reservation-skipped, dispatch-socraticode-unavailable, dispatch-callback-indexed-chunks-unknown, convergence-audit-bypass-codex-workers, and orch-hardcoded-pane-zero-for-picoz.

## Dependencies
Related doctrine: L29, L50, L51, L60, L61.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/dispatch.md, session-topology.jsonl, dispatch-log.jsonl, worker callback templates.
OUTPUTS: dispatcher preflight that always resolves callback_pane from topology, includes worker-tick parity, requires numeric socraticode/indexed_chunks fields, and refuses raw multiplexer capture/send paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dispatch generation reads latest session-topology row and writes callback_pane from it.
- Worker packet includes L50 numeric fields and L51 reservation instructions when edits are requested.
- Raw non-NTM pane operations in dispatch flow trip a soft violation.
- Regression fixtures cover picoz pane0-vs-pane1 and missing indexed_chunks_observed.

## Testing Obligations
- Fixture dispatch to picoz uses pane 1 from topology.
- Fixture callback missing indexed_chunks_observed is rejected.
- Fixture hand-rolled dispatch emits violation.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: fix(flywheel): enforce dispatch topology and callback contract [bead]
- Tests green
- AUTONOMY: autonomous

Hook gates + doctor invariants + skill body changes spec'd for: dispatch-bypass, callback-pane-wrong, tmux-bypass-ntm-health, file-reservation-skipped, socraticode-unavailable, callback-indexed-chunks-unknown, convergence-bypass-codex, hardcoded-pane-zero. title: [jeff-local-beads-rust-clone] repair local clone fetch/version-bump path
description:
Parent bead: flywheel-8no1
Upstream issue: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/issues/269
Gap observed during Jeff response absorption.

## Goal

Repair or replace the local `~/Developer/beads_rust` clone so future Jeff response version bumps can use a normal `git pull` / rebuild path instead of bypassing the dirty worktree with `cargo install --git`.

## Evidence

- `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust status --short` showed many modified source and test files plus untracked files.
- `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust fetch origin` failed with `fatal: bad object refs/remotes/origin/dependabot/cargo/rust-minor-f24ad7c61a` and `did not send all necessary objects`.
- For `beads_rust#269`, the worker installed v0.2.5 from Git tag directly to avoid touching or overwriting the dirty clone.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust status --short` is reviewed and every dirty file is classified as user work, generated output, or discardable cache.
2. No destructive reset or checkout is run without explicit Joshua approval.
3. `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust fetch origin --prune` succeeds after the repair strategy.
4. `git -C ~/Developer/beads_rust rev-parse origin/main` succeeds.
5. A documented local rebuild command for `br` is available after repair, or the receipt records `use_cargo_install_git_tag=true` as the durable version-bump path.
6. Final receipt records whether the local clone was repaired, replaced, or intentionally left dirty.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: fetch and rev-parse prove the clone can see upstream refs.
- DOCUMENTED: receipt explains the safe local version-bump path.
- SURFACED: future Jeff issue triage sees this bead instead of rediscovering the dirty/fetch-bad clone.

## Out Of Scope

- No upstream push.
- No destructive cleanup without Joshua approval.
- No attempt to preserve or discard unknown dirty work inside this bead without explicit classification.

## Rollback / Dry-Run Posture

Start with read-only classification and `git fetch --dry-run`/fresh clone alternatives. If replacing the clone, move the current directory aside rather than deleting it.

## DOD

Close with reason: `repaired beads_rust local clone version-bump path; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=flywheel-8no1-local-clone`.

title: [vc-relive] full vc revival — pull Jeff fixes, restart daemon, validate full mapping into vc
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03 ~09:00Z: 'an can we get everything with vc livened up? all fixes should be ready so we can get full mapping into vc right?'

CONTEXT: vc has been partially-broken across multiple gaps tracked in memory. Tonight's jeff-fixes-puller (in flight pane 2) will pull Jeff's fix for vc#4 (daemon empty-loop regression). With that landed, we can fully restart vc and get full ecosystem mapping into it.

KNOWN vc GAPS (per reference_upstream_issues.md):
1. vibe_cockpit#4 — daemon empty-loop regression (CLOSED 2026-05-02, fix-commit pending pull) — TARGETED BY: jeff-fixes-puller this turn
2. PATH shadowing — March-10 binary symlinked from .local/bin to .cargo/bin (status: should hold, verify)
3. DuckDB single-writer lock — blocks vc mcp while daemon runs (gates fsqlite migration upstream)
4. Beads collector 'No databases found' — ru collector returns github org/repo IDs not local paths (held back pending /planning-workflow → /jeff-convergence-audit synthesis)

PHASES (must serialize):

PHASE 1: Pull Jeff fixes
  Depends-on: jeff-fixes-puller (IN FLIGHT pane 2 this turn)
  Acceptance: vc binary at HEAD with vc#4 fix landed
  Verify: vc daemon log shows 'collecting at' string (per memory line 117)

PHASE 2: PATH + symlink verify
  - which vc → must resolve to ~/.cargo/bin/vc (not Vercel, not March binary)
  - vc --version → must report HEAD commit, not 0.1.0/c097d8b
  - ~/.local/bin/vc → must be symlink to ~/.cargo/bin/vc

PHASE 3: Daemon restart + collector verify
  - kill running vc daemon (graceful via SIGTERM)
  - launch new vc daemon (background, with proper logging)
  - wait 60s for first cycle
  - verify collector invocations in logs (target: 11+/16 collectors succeeding per memory)
  - verify rows-per-cycle delta (target: ~225/cycle per memory baseline)

PHASE 4: Full ecosystem mapping into vc
  - confirm beads collector finds local .beads/ dirs (was returning empty per memory)
  - confirm github collector pulls all 11 Joshua-authored Jeff issues + responses
  - confirm ru collector maps repo paths correctly
  - confirm vc has data on: flywheel, mobile-eats, skillos, alps, terra-title, blackfoot, zesttube, zeststream-infra
  - run vc dashboard (if CLI surface) and verify all 8 fleet repos visible

PHASE 5: Wire vc into tick.md Step 4i full surface
  - Currently Step 4i probes vc-observability — graceful degrade when unavailable
  - After revival: probe should report vc_status=ok with alerts and digest
  - Add new probe fields: vc_collector_health (X/16 succeeding), vc_repo_coverage (N/8 fleet repos visible)

PHASE 6: Cross-reference into other tonight beads
  - flywheel-vhl5.1 (fleet-onboarding) Tier 4 health probes should query vc for HEALTHY signal
  - flywheel-1rmp.1 (value-gap-hunter) dimension #1 'cross-repo failure-mode harvester' should consume vc data

ACCEPTANCE GATES (full epic):
[ ] Phase 1 callback DONE from jeff-fixes-puller
[ ] vc binary at HEAD with vc#4 fix
[ ] vc daemon running + collectors reporting
[ ] All 8 fleet repos visible in vc
[ ] tick.md Step 4i upgraded with collector_health + repo_coverage fields
[ ] Beads collector 'No databases found' issue filed if still extant after fix
[ ] Memory entry reference_upstream_issues.md updated with vc-relive timestamp + status

DEPENDENCIES (HARD blocking):
- jeff-fixes-puller IN FLIGHT (pane 2 this turn) — must complete first
- flywheel-vnsw (cli-canonicalization) — phase 5 wires into that

WHEN TO ACTIVATE: as soon as jeff-fixes-puller callback DONE.

ANTI-PATTERN AVOIDED: Trying to revive vc BEFORE pulling Jeff's fix would re-encounter the daemon empty-loop regression. Phase 1 is mandatory blocker.

title: recov-ledger: Recovery ledger + claim/stale semantics
description:
## Problem (Lane E AUDIT finding)
Ladder steps 4 (HARD_INTERRUPT), 6 (RESPAWN), 7 (RELAUNCH) FAIL or PARTIAL on idempotency without persistent state. 6 race conditions named (tick/tick, tick/manual, stale-claim-after-crash, etc).

## Spec
- Path: `~/.local/state/flywheel/recovery-ledger-<session>.jsonl` (append-only JSONL)
- Row fields: ts, session, pane, recover_id (uuid), step (1-8), state (started|done|failed|skipped|abandoned), actor (tick|manual|drill), idempotency_key
- Idempotency key shape: `<session>:<pane>:<minute_bucket>` (1-min buckets coalesce concurrent attempts)
- Reader function in pane-state.sh: returns `in_flight=<recover_id>` if open ledger entry exists for pane

## Claim/stale semantics
- pane-state.sh recover_claim <session> <pane> returns: idempotency_key OR claimed_by=<actor>
- Claim TTL: 60s (auto-stale)
- Stale-claim handling: tick that finds stale claim marks ledger row state=abandoned, starts fresh recover

## AC
- Concurrent tick+manual recover within same minute coalesces to single recover_id
- Crashed-mid-recover: re-read shows stale claim; new recover starts cleanly
- Synthetic test: 2 concurrent invocations on same pane → only 1 actual respawn happens
- recover_claim --json output validates against schema

## Cross-references
- Lane E §6 races 1-6 + Lane E §11 idempotency taxonomy
- Sister bead flywheel-7xxs (consumes ledger reader)
- Sister bead flywheel-7p0q (tick-5 RECOVER_PANE consumes claim function)
- L48 reinforcement: ledger writes are not L48 (state file, not source/binary)
close_reason: Recovery ledger design done. /tmp/recov_ledger_design.md (465 lines) + /tmp/recov_ledger.diff (177 lines). 6 races addressed, 4 tests, ladder=yes. Implementation = patch pane-state.sh in Wave 2.

title: [auto-fix-bead] auto-open or update fix beads when validation gates fail
description:
### Title

`[auto-fix-bead] auto-open or update fix beads when validation gates fail`

### Goal

When validation fails with actionable evidence, automatically create or update a repo-local fix bead. This bead prevents validation failures from becoming scrollback-only observations and closes the L52 gap for callback validation.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation`, `silent-finding-loss`, and `documented-bug-not-actioned-self-recursion`. `flywheel-1z65` says failed gates must file fix beads or reopen audited beads before fuckup logging and summary.

### Acceptance gates

1. Failed validation receipt with high-confidence actionable repair produces a `br create` dry-run payload containing title, priority, type, evidence, parent, and dependency refs. Proof receipt: `B06_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Duplicate detection updates an existing fix bead candidate instead of creating a duplicate. Proof receipt: `B06_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Mutating mode is gated by explicit apply path, idempotency key, and audit receipt. Proof receipt: `B06_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Fix bead body includes the failed validation receipt, original dispatch id, callback ref, trauma class, artifact path/command, and parent dependency. Proof receipt: `B06_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Low-confidence failure can record explicit `no_bead_reason`, but high-criticality Lane A classes cannot silently no-op. Proof receipt: `B06_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Auto-open path never writes to global Beads; `br where` or equivalent must prove repo-local `.beads`. Proof receipt: `B06_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Tests cover missing artifact, missing callback field, duplicate bead, and explicit no-bead reason. Proof receipt: `B06_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `auto_fix_cmd=<cmd> dry_run_payload=<path> idempotency_test=<cmd>:PASS repo_local_probe=<cmd>:PASS commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[auto-fix-bead]`.

### Doctrine references

L52, L53, L56, L60, L70; `flywheel-1z65`; `beads-workflow`; `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md`.

### Out of scope

No automatic reopen of closed beads, no learn promotion, no doctor threshold changes.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B03.  
Blocks: B04, B05, B09, B12.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings if Joshua limits auto-open to P0/P1 or high-confidence failures only.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B06_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B06_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B06_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B06_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B06_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B06_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B06_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [library-ingestion] complete socraticode indexing of all 177 Jeff repos + extract accretive learnings
description:
[library-ingestion] complete socraticode indexing of all 177 Jeff repos + extract accretive learnings

## Trauma class
`incomplete-library-ingestion` — Joshua flagged 2026-05-03T23:43Z: "have we ingested and indexed his entire library to look for accretive learnings?"

Answer: PARTIAL. 177 repos cloned to ~/Developer/, 79 socraticode-indexed, 98 budget-skipped (not failed), 379,400 chunks. Lane B-prime in the validate-everything plan researched 166/177 narratively but not exhaustively. No accretive-learning extraction pipeline.

## Goal
Two-phase work:
**Phase A — finish indexing.** Complete socraticode indexing of the 98 skipped repos. Concurrency 2, idempotent, persistent state.
**Phase B — extract accretive learnings.** Run cross-collection-fanout queries against the full corpus to surface patterns the flywheel doesn't yet have, codify them as beads or memory entries.

## Acceptance gates
1. `~/.local/state/jeff-intel/repos.jsonl` shows `indexed_at` for all 177 repos
2. `mcp__socraticode__codebase_list_projects` returns 177 entries
3. Indexing script handles resume-after-interrupt cleanly (idempotent)
4. Phase B query battery: at least 10 cross-collection queries against the corpus exploring categories: error-handling patterns, callback verification, sub-process orchestration, sqlite/db patterns, agent-mail integration, doctor-signal patterns, cross-runtime parity, secrets handling, test fixture patterns, cli-canonical-scoping
5. Each query produces a `~/.local/state/jeff-intel/learnings/<query>.md` artifact with: what we found, gap in our system, recommended bead/memory action
6. Top 5 highest-value learnings → file as P0/P1 beads with `jeff-corpus-derived` label
7. Doctor signal `jeff_corpus_indexed_count` (currently 79, target 177); flags if <170
8. canonical-paths.txt entry for jeff-intel state dir + learnings dir
9. Tests verify resume behavior + at least 3 fixture queries

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: all 177 indexed; 10 query artifacts present; 5 beads filed
- DOCUMENTED: jeff-intel/README explains pipeline; SKILL.md if extracted
- SURFACED: doctor signal; daily-report (companion bead) summarizes new learnings

## Why P1 (not P0)
This unlocks accretive learning velocity but doesn't block today's mission. P0 is daily-report (synthesis layer) — without it, even with full ingestion the value doesn't surface.

## Doctrine refs
- reference_jeff_substrate_inventory.md
- bead flywheel-1lpv jeff-intel-network (parent — daily monitor)
- bead flywheel-k5yp jeff-philosophy-study (companion)

- /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/plans/validate-and-redispatch-foundational-2026-05-03/01-RESEARCH-B-PRIME.md (existing 166-repo narrative survey)

## Skills cited

- cross-collection-fanout
- canonical-cli-scoping
- accretive-cron-orchestration

title: recov-dispatch-update: dispatch.md consume new pane-class taxonomy
description:
## Problem (Lane D AUDIT highest-risk finding)
Today /flywheel:dispatch validates pane via _shared/pane-state.sh which trusts ntm health activity (the broken probe). After flywheel-terc lands the new contract, dispatch.md MUST also be updated or the pane-state contract change does not propagate to the dispatch infrastructure → silent dispatch into wedged.

## Spec
- Update ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/dispatch.md Step 1 (validate pane)
- Replace activity-string check with pane-class taxonomy check (IDLE_READY only dispatchable; refuse FROZEN_SUSPECT, WORKING, QUARANTINED, UNKNOWN)
- Output the rejection reason in dispatch's abort message ("pane class=FROZEN_SUSPECT, suggest /flywheel:recover-pane <session> <N>")
- Backward-compat: if pane-state.sh returns old format (pre-terc contract), fall back to current behavior + log disagreement

## AC
- /flywheel:dispatch refuses FROZEN_SUSPECT pane with class-specific message
- /flywheel:dispatch abort exit code distinguishes pane-class-reject (4) vs other failures (1)
- Synthetic frozen pane: dispatch refuses; status surface offers /flywheel:recover-pane
- Backward-compat verified: dispatch on non-upgraded pane-state.sh still works
- canonical-cli-scoping check: --json mode of dispatch reports pane_class field

## Cross-references
- Lane D §4 + §9 (highest-risk integration finding)
- Highest-leverage sub-bead because dispatch.md is what TODAY pushes work into wedged workers (the original fleet-idle problem)
- Depends: flywheel-terc (new pane-state.sh contract must exist first)
close_reason: dispatch.md update design done. /tmp/recov_dispatch_update_design.md (284 lines) + /tmp/recov_dispatch_update.diff (75 lines). 5 classes handled, 4 tests, ladder=yes. Exit code 4 introduced. Implementation = patch dispatch.md in Wave 2.

title: [ntm-audit] triage 50 CLAIMED-USE-NOT-WIRED rows: wire-it / reclassify-excluded / reclassify-wrap / rename
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07
parent=flywheel-7p45b

## Why
flywheel-7p45b just shipped: 86 USE rows verified by callsite count; 50 are CLAIMED-USE-NOT-WIRED (0 callsites). That's 58% of USE classifications were aspirational. Each of the 50 needs a disposition decision. Without this triage, the inventory's USE column remains untrustworthy and "audit ALL NTM surfaces" is structurally incomplete.

## Acceptance
For each of the 50 CLAIMED-USE-NOT-WIRED rows in 11-USE-ROW-VERIFICATION.md, decide one of four dispositions:

1. **wire-it** — the surface IS valuable for flywheel, just not yet wired. File a follow-up wire-in bead. (LOC delta: typically negative, replacing hand-rolled with native)
2. **reclassify-excluded** — the surface genuinely doesn't fit flywheel's use-cases. Update NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md to mark EXCLUDED + cite the no-fit reason.
3. **reclassify-wrap** — flywheel has a wrapper that consumes this surface but the audit's grep missed it (different name, indirect call, etc.). Locate the wrapper, document, update inventory.
4. **rename-the-row** — the surface name in the inventory was ambiguous and the audit measured the wrong identifier. Re-grep with corrected name; reclassify per actual count.

Each decision must cite evidence (file:line for wrappers/calls, or explicit "no-fit because X" reasoning).

Output: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/plans/ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07/12-USE-NOT-WIRED-TRIAGE.md`

Sections:
1. Per-row triage table (50 rows × 4-disposition decision + evidence)
2. Disposition counts (wire-it=N, reclassify-excluded=N, reclassify-wrap=N, rename=N)
3. Proposed wire-it beads (one per wire-it row, recommendation only — orchestrator files)
4. NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md diff proposal (the column updates)
5. Updated headline numbers: VERIFIED-USE / LATENT-USE / VERIFIED-WRAP / EXCLUDED counts after triage

## Hard rules
- For each row: socraticode K=10 with surface-name variants (handle ambiguity)
- For each WRAP claim: cite the wrapper script + line where it consumes the native surface
- For each EXCLUDED: cite specific flywheel use-case mismatch
- NO new wire-in beads created here — recommendations only
- L107: write 12-* doc only after lsof check
- Compliance pack on close per post-x6ok8 contract

## Cross-refs
- 11-USE-ROW-VERIFICATION.md (the 50 rows to triage)
- 06-GAP-AUDIT-SPREADSHEET.md (earlier partial audit)
- NTM-SURFACE-INVENTORY.md (canonical 108-row classification, will be updated)

Generated from: flywheel-og4v plan decomposition.

## Goal
Implement the recovery baseline snapshot, deterministic nightly helper, retention policy, restore dry-run/apply harness, bootstrap readiness gate, orphan reconciliation, and drill ledger.

## Acceptance
- Milestone 1 baseline: latest verified NTM checkpoint exists for each approved session and manifest is written temp-to-final.

- Milestone 4 drills: D1 fleet dry-run, D2 disposable kill/restore, D3 interrupted snapshot, D4 boot-like bootstrap drill all produce evidence before full readiness is claimed.
- Restore replay is idempotency-keyed and emits orphan_candidate rows before redispatch; it never blind-sends callbacks.
- Bootstrap waits for home, binary, config, repo paths, expected sessions, watcher status, and health verify.
- Manifest records CLI versions, resume policy, provider transcript pointers, /tmp artifact promotion list, size estimates, and retention decisions.
- Source plan path appears in receipts and manifest.

## Out of scope
Changing protected-session policy without Joshua decision or depending on CASR as the v1 backup substrate.

Depends on B04-B11.

title: [jeff-stack J7] daily-diff-script-build
description:
Build the daily diff + report script: ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-daily-diff.sh

DEPENDS ON: J3 (canonical clones), J1 (x-cli fix).

PURPOSE: Once daily, fetch updates on all 177 Jeff repos, compute diffs since last run, capture tweets and blog posts, produce a structured daily report.

PROCEDURE OUTLINE:
1. Discover repos: `find ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -type d -name '!.*'`
2. State file: ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/last-diff-run.json (records last successful run ts per repo)
3. For each repo:
   - `git -C <path> fetch --all --tags --prune` (suppress output, log errors)
   - Compute new commits: `git log --oneline <last_seen_sha>..HEAD`
   - If new commits exist:
     - Capture: `git log --stat --since=<last_run_ts> -- '*'` to /tmp/jeff-diff/<repo>.txt
     - Append to repos-with-changes list
     - Mark repo for re-index: queue mcp__socraticode__codebase_index call
4. Tweets: call working x-cli command (per J1), capture last 24h from doodlestein
5. Blog: snapshot-diff jeffreyemanuel.com/rss.xml against prior snapshot
6. Aggregate report: /tmp/jeff-report-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md with sections:
   - **New Commits** (per repo): commit messages, file stats
   - **New Releases**: from atom feeds
   - **New Tweets** (doodlestein, last 24h)
   - **New Blog Posts** (jeffreyemanuel.com RSS delta)
   - **Re-indexed**: list of repos pushed back into socraticode
   - **What to learn** (placeholder for L64 layer 2 — fill in v2)
7. Write the report to ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/reports/<date>.md (durable history) AND /tmp/ (convenience)
8. Update state file with new last-seen-shas + last_run_ts
9. Emit JSON receipt to ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/daily-runs.jsonl

CALLBACK: When finished, send completion to orchestrator with link to report.

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. Script is idempotent (run twice, second run finds zero diffs)
2. Resumable on partial failure (state file tracks per-repo progress)
3. Test run produces a non-empty report covering at least the existing changes
4. Re-indexes only repos with actual new commits (not all 177 every day)
5. Robust to network failure on individual repos (logged, not fatal)
6. Total runtime under 30 minutes for typical day

OUT OF SCOPE (for J9 to fill in):
- L64 layer 2 "what can we learn" verdict logic per artifact
- Pattern catalog generation

title: [cm-upgrade-break] 0.4.x breaks cm context workspace contract
description:
## Goal
Restore an upgrade path for cm beyond 0.2.3 without breaking the local cass-memory contract.

## Context
flywheel-z7b8 live-tested Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search v0.4.1 and v0.4.2. Both binaries report cass 0.4.x and reject the existing documented command: cm context <task> --workspace /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json. Local 0.2.3 accepts that command. Per feedback_no_push_ntm_br, do not patch or push upstream; track local upgrade/pin decision here and file upstream issue/PR path separately if needed.

## Dependencies
Blocked by flywheel-z7b8 pin receipt landing.

## Inputs / Outputs
Inputs: .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-z7b8-cm-upgrade.md, /Users/josh/.codex/skills/cass-memory/SKILL.md, upstream v0.4.1/v0.4.2 release assets.
Outputs: either local compatibility wrapper/pin policy, updated cass-memory skill contract, or upstream issue reference.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Decide whether cm should remain 0.2.3, move to a wrapper around cass 0.4.x, or update all local callers to the new cass CLI.
- Preserve a working machine-readable pre-task memory command for flywheel workers.
- Record upstream issue or local no-upstream rationale.

## Testing Obligations
- cm context "smoke" --workspace /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel --json returns success, or the documented replacement command passes with all call sites updated.
- cm doctor --json or replacement health command has a bounded runtime and machine-readable result.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: fix(cm): resolve 0.4.x context compatibility [flywheel-<id>]
- Tests green
- Receipt updated
- AUTONOMY: autonomous

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The artifact, command, or doctrine surface named in `[cm-upgrade-break] 0.4.x breaks cm context workspace contract` is updated with close evidence.
AG2: A targeted test, dry-run, or validator command passes and is named in the close receipt.
AG3: `br show flywheel-anqp1` remains open or in_progress until the evidence artifact exists.
close_reason: Resolved cm 0.4.x context compatibility decision: kept cm 0.2.3 pinned, updated Codex cass-memory skill to workspace-scoped pre-task command, verified cm context smoke and cm doctor --json, tested fresh cass 0.4.2 asset still rejects --workspace and times out doctor bound. Evidence: .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-anqp1-cm-compatibility.md; commit 45253cf.

title: [gap-hunter] add loop-integrity 9th gap class — detect launchd-active-but-loop-dead
description:
Joshua-flagged gap in gap-hunter classifier 2026-05-03 ~07:25Z. Current 8 gap classes don't detect a load-bearing failure mode: a flywheel-managed session has an active launchd driver and active=true loop state file, but the loop is functionally dead (no ledger writes, no pane state changes, no receipts, no callbacks).

PROOF OF GAP CLASS: tonight's session evidence
- mobile-eats: launchd active + ledger writes every 5min + pane state changing + receipts + callbacks → HEALTHY autonomous loop
- skillos: launchd active + active=true + idle gaps Joshua flagged twice tonight → DEAD loop despite all driver markers green
- gap-hunter saw 0 gaps for skillos because it only checks driver-existence markers, not driver-output markers

LOOP-INTEGRITY DEFINITION (5 observable signals per loop, AND-not-OR):
1. ledger_writes_since_last_tick > 0  (~/.local/state/flywheel-loop/ OR product-specific)
2. pane_state_changed_since_last_tick (any worker pane state-since age < tick interval)
3. receipt_files_written_since_last_tick (.flywheel/ticks/ OR last_tick_<project>.json)
4. callback_received_in_last_2_ticks (cross-pane via dispatch-log.jsonl OR ntm send)
5. fuckup_log_decisions_made_since_last_tick (per Step 2c — substrate is being processed)

Loop is HEALTHY iff all 5 signals fire within last interval. Loop is DEAD if launchd-active=true but ≥3 signals zero. Loop is LIMPING if 1-2 signals zero.

ARCHITECTURAL NOTE (Joshua correction 2026-05-03): codex-only sessions CAN run autonomous loops fine — mobile-eats proves it with 30 ticks. Loop-integrity is NOT 'has CC orchestrator' — it's 'driver output is happening'. Initial framing of 'add CC orchestrator pane to skillos' was wrong; right framing is 'why aren't skillos's codex panes doing work between ticks like mobile-eats's are?'

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
- [ ] Add 9th class to ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/gap-hunt-probe.sh
- [ ] Class function checks 5 signals per loop-state file
- [ ] Returns gaps for {DEAD, LIMPING} states with which-signals-failed evidence
- [ ] Validate against tonight's evidence: mobile-eats=HEALTHY, skillos=LIMPING|DEAD
- [ ] shellcheck passes
- [ ] Update gap-hunter version comment
- [ ] Add to canonical-cli-scoping --info output
- [ ] Wire into substrate-bleed-triage skill being sharpened in flywheel:4 RIGHT NOW
- [ ] Wire into tick.md Step 4n gap-hunt receipt as additional class

DEPENDENCIES: pane 3 in-flight gap-hunter classifier-refine work (don't conflict)

DOCTRINE LINKS:
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_loop_state_without_driver.md (loop state file alone is not a driver — sibling pattern)
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/project_loop_state_doctrine_promoted_2026_05_02.md (already promoted to AGENTS.md L57 — extend that doctrine with loop-integrity output checks)

WHEN TO DISPATCH: after pane 3 callback (gap-hunter classifier refine completes) — they edit same file, must serialize.

PARENT: covers gap Joshua identified in 'why doesn't skillos have its own perpetual loop' conversation 2026-05-03.
close_reason: loop-integrity 9th gap class shipped in gap-hunt-probe.sh; live validation: flywheel=HEALTHY, mobile-eats=HEALTHY, skillos=LIMPING, alpsinsurance=DEAD; shellcheck pass; gap-hunt normal run appended ledger and surfaced loop-integrity gaps

title: flywheel-loop doctor --json non-idempotent due to dynamic tentacle probe paths
description:
Trauma class: doctor-full-json-non-idempotent-dynamic-paths

Discovered 2026-05-02 by pane 2 L57 dispatch. flywheel-loop doctor --json embeds dynamic /tmp/tentacle_probe_*_<random>.log evidence paths in tentacle probe output, so JSON differs across consecutive runs even when underlying state unchanged. Breaks canonical-cli-scoping idempotency gate.

Independent of new L57 loop_driver block (which IS idempotent — proven by pane 2).

Fix: stable evidence paths (e.g., /tmp/tentacle_probe_<probe-name>.log without random suffix) OR exclude evidence paths from idempotency hash comparison.

Acceptance:
1. Two consecutive 'flywheel-loop doctor --json' runs produce identical JSON (when state unchanged)
2. canonical-cli-scoping idempotency gate passes for full doctor JSON
3. Evidence paths still recoverable for forensics

Evidence: /tmp/ntm_rebuild_l57_findings.md:182-184
Filed: ~/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl 2026-05-02T~23:55Z

title: [skillos-mission] expand from 'ship sharpened skill per N ticks' to 'daily jsm sync + zeststream jsmesque layer'
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03 ~07:50Z: 'skillos's job is to keep us in sync with jsm every day with latest & greatest, while saving/preserving/building our own jsmesque jsm layers for zeststream skill management. They are supposed to be doing way more than they are right now.'

CURRENT SKILLOS GOAL (v2 set 2026-05-03): 'engine ships ≥1 sharpened skill per N ticks'
- This is parameter-tier (Meadows #12): incremental output count
- It missed the GOAL-tier framing (Meadows #3): WHAT skillos serves

MEADOWS-CORRECTED GOAL (v3, this bead):
1. **Daily jsm sync** — pull latest from jeffreyemanuel/skills daily; ingest new skills; flag updates to existing skills; route to agent-fleet-management for adoption decisions
2. **Zeststream jsmesque layer** — preserve, version, and 'publish' Zeststream-internal skills using same patterns as jsm; never lose a Zeststream skill to drift
3. **Sharpening** — sub-goal of the above, not the headline goal

EVIDENCE FOR THE GAP:
- skillos-limping-diagnostic 2026-05-03 found 2/5 signals alive (callback flywheel-loe1 sibling)
- Dead signals: ledger, callbacks, fuckup_decisions
- Jeff shipped 4 new SaaS skills in last week (seo-for-saas, user-support-triage v2, user-support-ticketing, simplify-and-refactor) — skillos noticed 0
- Local zeststream skills (~/.claude/skills/zeststream-*) have no version tracking, no update detection, no 'publish' surface
- agent-fleet-management v2-tick-3 was authored but never registered for daily refresh

ACCEPTANCE GATES (when this bead activates):
- [ ] skillos GOAL.md v3 rewritten with daily-jsm-sync + zeststream-layer as headline goals
- [ ] daily jsm-pull cron in skillos session
- [ ] new-skill detection routes to skillos-routed.jsonl with classification (adopt | extend | ignore | conflict)
- [ ] zeststream-skill-inventory registry at ~/.local/state/zeststream/skill-inventory.json
- [ ] zeststream-skill-publish wrapper (canonical-cli-scoping compliant) for safe local 'publish'
- [ ] update-detection for existing skills (Jeff version drift like ntm)
- [ ] agent-fleet-management consumes the new daily-sync output
- [ ] skillos loop-integrity 5/5 signals alive within 48h of activation
- [ ] First Joshua-decision filed from auto-detected jsm change within 7d

DEPENDENCIES:
- flywheel-aucl (loop-integrity gap class) — blocks because skillos diagnostic data feeds GOAL.md v3 design
- skillos-limping-diagnostic-2026_05_03 followup beads (3 root causes) — blocks because v3 must address the dead signals

DOCTRINE LINKS:
- /Users/josh/.claude/references/claude-md-jsm.md (jsm hygiene canonical)
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/project_skillos_goal_rotation_v2_2026_05_03.md (parent goal rotation, will be superseded)
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_jeff_substrate_version_drift.md (mentor pattern for daily-sync)
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_use_codex_workers.md (skillos uses codex panes)

WHEN TO ACTIVATE: after wave 1+2 application workers complete (bandwidth available), OR if Joshua promotes this bead to wave 3.

ANTI-PATTERN AVOIDED: This bead replaces a parameter-tier goal with a goals-tier goal. The skillos LIMPING wasn't a parameter problem ('not enough ticks'), it was a goals problem ('shipped goal didn't connect to mission'). Per Meadows: parameter-thrash on a wrong-goal system never works.

title: [skillos-mobile-eats-pattern-apply]
description:
Joshua decision gate for applying the mobile-eats-style skillos architecture from flywheel-b6js.

Architecture summary:
- Mobile-eats works because launchd calls a domain-specific payload every 5m: /Users/josh/.local/bin/mobile-eats-flywheel-loop-tick. That payload writes last_run.json, emits prompt files, dispatches mission-bound work, and relies on callback reaping.
- Skillos currently runs a long-lived generic .flywheel/run-30m-loop.sh every 30m. It sends generic flywheel-loop prompts, writes only repo-local/proof ledgers, and has no JSM-specific work payload or callback reaper.
- Target: add a mission-bound skillos JSM driver parallel to mobile-eats: hourly JSM status/sync detection, daily ZestStream skill inventory, routed decision rows, canonical last_tick mirror, and flywheel notification on new/update detection.

title: [jeff-track-ntm-config-validate] triage remaining live config validation drift
description:
## Goal
Triage the remaining `ntm config validate` failures in Joshua’s live config after ntm#111 and ntm#113 landed.

## Context
During `flywheel-pdwg` response triage, local `ntm` was verified to include `65602811` and the isolated `[coordinator]` repro passed. The live `~/.config/ntm/config.toml` still fails strict validation for a different set of keys: custom `agents.*`, custom `models.*`, `health.researcher_sessions`, `scanner.warning_threshold`, `session_paths.*`, plus recipe agent types `zesty` and `pi`.

Issue #113 already handled the earlier broader-drift set for `context_rotation.recovery.*` and `resilience.rate_limit.auto_rotate`; do not duplicate that upstream issue. This bead is for deciding whether the remaining keys are local config cleanup, legitimate upstream schema gaps, or should be documented as intentionally rejected.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: `/tmp/flywheel-pdwg-config-validate-live.json`, `~/.config/ntm/config.toml`, `~/.config/ntm/recipes.toml`, upstream ntm issues #111 and #113.
OUTPUTS: one of: local config cleanup patch, fresh Jeff issue with source trace, or explicit no-op receipt explaining why strict rejection is correct.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Run `ntm config validate --json` and classify every remaining error into local-only, upstream-schema-gap, or intentionally-rejected.
- If upstream-schema-gap remains, run `gh issue list --repo Dicklesworthstone/ntm --search <keywords>` first and file exactly one non-duplicate issue with file:line citations.
- If local-only, produce a backup-first config cleanup plan; do not mutate `~/.config/ntm/config.toml` without Joshua approval.
- Preserve #113 decisions: `health.researcher_sessions` stays rejected unless a runtime consumer is found.
- Record before/after validation JSON receipts under `/tmp/`.

## Testing Obligations
- `ntm config validate --json | jq .summary` before and after any approved cleanup.
- If an upstream issue is filed, include the GitHub URL and flywheel tracking bead reference.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: none unless an approved local config/doc patch is made. Close reason must include `VALIDATED`, `DOCUMENTED`, `SURFACED`, and the final classification counts.

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The artifact, command, or doctrine surface named in `[jeff-track-ntm-config-validate] triage remaining live config validation drift` is updated with close evidence.
AG2: A targeted test, dry-run, or validator command passes and is named in the close receipt.
AG3: `br show flywheel-b6yu` remains open or in_progress until the evidence artifact exists.

title: [flywheel:learn STATE.md miner] daily mine of all fleet STATE.md files for improvement opportunities
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03 ~09:30Z: 'we should also have part of our fuckup flywheel:learn process a system that mines state.md files across all repos and looks for opportunities to improve things daily.'

GAP: /flywheel:learn currently consumes only fuckup-log.jsonl. STATE.md files across 8 fleet repos contain rich latent signal: 'Next Actions' lists, 'Known Gaps' sections, 'Deferred' rows, 'Resume Context' notes. None of this feeds the learn loop. Joshua manually re-reads STATE.md every few days to find opportunities — many improvements rediscovered repeatedly.

PROPOSED EXTENSION: /flywheel:learn --mine-state [--since=24h]
  Mines all fleet-roster repos' .flywheel/STATE.md (and root STATE.md if present) for:
  
  1. UNRESOLVED items — 'Next Actions' rows that haven't been beaded
  2. STALE items — 'Deferred' rows older than threshold (14d default)
  3. PATTERN items — same gap appearing in 3+ repos = systemic
  4. RECURRING items — gap closed and reopened (drift)
  5. ORPHANED items — 'Known Gaps' entries with no open bead reference
  
  Per discovery: file P2/P3 bead OR add to no-bead-reason ledger (same shape as fuckup decisions).

CANONICAL CLI SHAPE:
  /flywheel:learn --mine-state           — mine all fleet repos
  /flywheel:learn --mine-state --repo X  — single repo
  /flywheel:learn --mine-state --json    — machine-readable digest
  /flywheel:learn --mine-state --dry-run — discovery only, no bead filing

CRON: daily at 06:00 local via launchd (matches morning-review cadence).

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
[ ] /flywheel:learn extended with --mine-state subcommand
[ ] Helper at ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/state-md-miner.sh (canonical-cli-scoping)
[ ] Discovery rules for 5 item classes documented + implemented
[ ] Per-discovery decision: bead OR no-bead-reason with text evidence
[ ] Cap auto-bead at 5/day per repo to prevent flood
[ ] Daily launchd cron @ 06:00
[ ] Wire into /flywheel:tick Step 4q so daily report surfaces in tick receipt
[ ] Test against current 8 fleet repos
[ ] AGENTS.md L62 added per L61 (this directive landed = doctrine)
[ ] README.md Current State updated

DEPENDENCIES:
- flywheel-vnsw (cli-canonicalization) — sibling tile
- flywheel-vhl5 (fleet-onboarding) — provides fleet-roster.json that this consumes

DOCTRINE LINKS:
- AGENTS.md L61 (just landed — every doctrine wires into AGENTS+README; this bead instance triggers L62)
- ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/learn.md (existing skill to extend)
- feedback_flywheel_never_idles (mining is exactly the 'go hunting' pattern)
- This is also Joshua's response to me + part of the value-gap-hunter (flywheel-1rmp) family — cross-link
