title: [validation-schema] define callback validation receipt schema and fixture corpus
description:
### Title

`[validation-schema] define callback validation receipt schema and fixture corpus`

### Goal

Create the canonical validation receipt contract used by every later component. This bead defines the schema, fixture corpus, and read-only parser expectations for worker callback claims, artifact evidence, 3-Q surface audit results, runtime context probes, and chain-blocker receipts. It must make "validated" a machine-readable state rather than prose.

### Why now

Trauma class: `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation`. Lane A found worker DONE callbacks and closed beads are only partially validated, and `flywheel-1z65` records the exact case where callback claims were forwarded without probing artifacts. Without a schema, every later gate will re-invent receipt fields and drift.

### Acceptance gates

1. Schema file exists at the final chosen canonical path and includes `schema_version`, `dispatch_id`, `callback_ref`, `status`, `failure_classes[]`, `evidence[]`, `artifact_checks[]`, `runtime_context`, `bead_actions[]`, `learn_route`, and `chain_blocker`. Proof receipt: `B01_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Fixture corpus includes at minimum: valid DONE, missing artifact DONE, BLOCKED without fuckup row, runtime unresponsive, context drift, valid no-bead reason, invalid no-bead reason, closed bead missing artifact, and tick-punted. Proof receipt: `B01_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. A read-only parser validates all passing fixtures and rejects all failing fixtures with deterministic JSON errors. Proof receipt: `B01_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Receipt statuses are only `pass`, `fail`, or `unknown`; runtime timeout maps to `unknown`, never `pass`. Proof receipt: `B01_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Artifact evidence supports typed refs: path, command, dispatch-log ref, bead id, commit sha, transcript hash, and Joshua confirmation hash. Proof receipt: `B01_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. No fixture uses real secrets, real Agent Mail tokens, or live production mutation. Proof receipt: `B01_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Documentation in the schema explicitly maps each field to the three audit questions: validated, documented, surfaced. Proof receipt: `B01_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `schema_path=<path> fixtures=<dir> parser_cmd=<cmd> tests=<cmd>:PASS commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[validation-schema]`.

### Doctrine references

L52, L53, L56, L60, L69, L70; `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md`; `feedback_orchestrator_validates_callbacks.md`; bead `flywheel-1z65`.

### Out of scope

No dispatch-template mutation, no doctor signal, no bead creation, no automatic reopen, no Codex pane probing.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: Phase 3 Joshua-disposes if schema policy questions change.  
Blocks: B02, B03, B04, B05, B06, B07, B09, B11, B12.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings before final shape if Joshua changes positive-event or auto-open policy.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B01_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B01_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B01_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B01_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B01_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B01_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B01_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [doctor-signal-mitigation-1-tighten] auto-doctor script creates duplicates when symptom recurs after close
description:
## Goal
Tighten doctor-signal-bead-promotion.sh so it does not re-create [auto-doctor:<symptom>] beads when (a) a recently-closed (<24h) bead exists with same auto-doctor title, OR (b) the underlying root-cause work bead (e.g. flywheel-osz1) is still open and tagged with the same trauma class.

## Evidence
- 2026-05-03T19:00Z: doctrine doctor-signal-fail-without-bead-promotion promoted
- 2026-05-03T19:25Z: flywheel-9zuz shipped script (mitigation #1)
- 2026-05-03T19:29Z: first tick auto-created flywheel-dqsy (db_fail)
- 2026-05-03T19:31Z: pane 4 closed dqsy as duplicate of flywheel-osz1 (decompose plan)
- 2026-05-03T19:54Z: NEXT tick auto-created flywheel-4ofo for SAME db_fail symptom (root cause unchanged)
- 2026-05-03T19:55Z: orch closed 4ofo as duplicate

The script's match logic only checks open beads. Recently-closed-as-duplicate beads should signal "stop re-surfacing this symptom".

## Acceptance gates
1. Script consults closed beads in last 24h with matching [auto-doctor:<symptom>] title — if found, no-op
2. Script also matches open beads with `linked_beads` reference to the open root-cause bead (e.g. flywheel-osz1)
3. Test: simulate db_fail + presence of recently-closed [auto-doctor:db_fail] → no new bead
4. Test: simulate db_fail + open flywheel-osz1 (root-cause work tracked) → no new bead
5. Test: simulate NEW symptom class with no prior beads → bead created (regression negative)

## Plan reference
~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/INCIDENTS.md doctor-signal-fail-without-bead-promotion (mitigation #1 follow-up)

## Depends on
flywheel-9zuz (closed) — script already exists, just needs tightening

## Out of scope
- Changing doctrine threshold (=0 is correct per threshold investigation)
- Modifying the doctor itself
- Touching beads_rust source

## DOD
COMMIT must include `[doctor-signal-mitigation-1-tighten]`.

## Goal

Update the shared dispatch packet template so every worker callback carries topology-safe, machine-checkable L50/L51/L52/L53 fields.

## Scope

- Patch `/Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/_shared/dispatch-template.md`.
- Replace prose callback examples with a topology-resolving callback pane snippet.
- Require numeric `socraticode_queries` and `indexed_chunks_observed` unless Socraticode is explicitly disabled.
- Require `files_reserved` and `files_released` with `NONE_READONLY`, `NONE_NO_EDITS`, or explicit unavailable reason only when valid.
- Require one of `beads_filed`, `beads_updated`, or `no_bead_reason`.
- Require `fuckups_logged` for BLOCKED or trauma-bearing DONE.
- Include task SHA and dispatch schema version in packet header.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `rg -n "socraticode_queries|indexed_chunks_observed|files_reserved|files_released|beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason|fuckups_logged" /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/_shared/dispatch-template.md` proves required fields are present.
2. `rg -n "session-topology.jsonl|callback_pane|orchestrator_pane" /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/_shared/dispatch-template.md` proves callback pane lookup is topology-backed.
3. Template examples reject or document rejection for `indexed_chunks_observed=unknown`.
4. Template includes explicit readonly/no-edit reservation alternatives and distinguishes them from skipped reservations on edit tasks.
5. Template includes DID/DIDNT/GAPS callback shape from L80 or the current worker-tick contract.
6. A rendered packet fixture or grep receipt proves `schema_version=2` and task hash metadata appear in worker dispatch bodies.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: rendered template or grep checks prove all mandatory callback fields exist.
- DOCUMENTED: the template itself documents legal values and blocked/unavailable paths.
- SURFACED: callback fields are stable inputs for callback validator and doctor invariant beads.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L50, L51, L52, L53, L71, L80.
- README.md Dispatch Contract.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.

## Out Of Scope

- No hook enforcement; this bead updates the packet contract only.
- No worker implementation changes outside the shared template.
- No dispatch-log migration.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `updated dispatch template callback contract; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[dispatch-template-contract]`.

title: [team-roster B06] Cross-session worker borrowing protocol
description:
Source plan: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/team-roster-2026-05-01.md
Generated from: flywheel-ty21 plan decomposition.

## Goal
Turn the roster `available_for_borrow` and `max_borrow_workers` fields into a safe dispatch protocol for borrowing idle workers across sessions.

## Acceptance
- Borrow request/approve/release state machine is defined with idempotency keys.
- Borrowing only considers sessions with fresh pulse, live worker panes, and explicit borrow availability.
- Protected sessions and client sessions default to not borrowable unless roster policy says otherwise.
- Borrowed worker callbacks route to the requesting orchestrator while preserving original session identity.
- Agent Mail and NTM coordination records make ownership and return path auditable.
- Tests cover duplicate request, stale pulse, protected session, worker death mid-borrow, and release-on-complete.

## Three-Q
- VALIDATED: protocol fixtures and dry-run dispatcher prove no blind borrowing.
- DOCUMENTED: protocol spec is self-contained.
- SURFACED: borrow state appears in roster/doctor or daily report.

## Out of scope
Actual borrowing implementation in B05; this is the explicit out-of-scope follow-up from the plan.

## Dependencies
Depends on flywheel-6xk, flywheel-2a7, and the roster notify bead.

Per feedback_no_ad_hoc_per_repo_doctrine_edits — fix substrate not symptom.

title: [orch-probe-discipline] probe agent execution context, not raw orchestrator shell
description:
## Issue
When probing whether a tool is available to a coding agent, raw shell `which X`
in the orchestrator pane is NOT proof of agent-side availability. The agent
may execute via login zsh (`/bin/zsh -lc`) which can have a different PATH
than the interactive pane shell.

## Evidence
2026-05-03T22:08Z: pane 3 codex agent reported `which dcg failed` from its
tool-execution context, while pane-shell zsh in the same pane resolved dcg
fine. Earlier I (orchestrator) had probed via tmux capture / inferred shell
state without sending the probe THROUGH the agent. Got false signal.

After ORX1 H1 diagnosis (flywheel-orx1 closed/downgrade): the asymmetry was
real, just not what I named it. Lesson: probe agent execution context, not
pane shell.

## Acceptance gates
1. flywheel-toolset-parity probe (flywheel-q03g child of 2p25) MUST run probes
   through agent execution context for the codex runtime, not raw shell:
   - For codex: send probe via `ntm send <pane> "which X"` and parse
     callback; the codex agent itself runs the command in its tool-shell
   - For claude: probe directly in Bash tool (which IS the agent's exec context)
2. Document the probe-discipline rule as L-rule companion to L65 (cli-identity-beats-command-name)
3. Update flywheel-loop doctor to call parity probe via canonical path, not raw shell

## Companion
- L65 — CLI-IDENTITY-BEATS-COMMAND-NAME (filename ≠ identity; same shape)
- flywheel-2p25 — toolset parity epic
- flywheel-1z65 — orchestrator-validate-callback (this is a SUB-rule)

## Out of scope
- Fix .zprofile (already done — that was orx1)
- Implement the parity probe (that's flywheel-q03g)

Validation: orch independently confirmed L69 grep + q03g comment via br show + evidence file read.

title: [autoloop-p2-status-stop] add stop status and budget truth surface
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/autonomous-loop-2026-04-30.md`

## Scope

Implement Phase 2 `stop` and `status` subcommands plus budget reporting.

## Required Behavior

- `flywheel-autoloop stop --global|--autoloop --reason <text>` creates STOP
  sentinels with structured receipt.
- `flywheel-autoloop status --json` reads `last_run.json`, `queue.json`,
  `repo-state.json`, LaunchAgent state, and pending dispatches.
- Enforce and report budgets: 12 global/day and per-repo day limits from config.
- Wire `/flywheel:status` or the repo status surface to read the autoloop status
  output if that surface exists.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `flywheel-autoloop stop --autoloop --reason test --json | jq '.status=="stopped"'`
   succeeds and creates the expected sentinel.
2. `flywheel-autoloop status --json | jq '.last_run and .queue and .budgets'`
   succeeds.
3. Budget-exhausted fixture prevents dispatch and emits a machine-readable
   reason.
4. LaunchAgent missing/stale fixture is visible in status output.
5. STOP receipt includes reason, timestamp, actor, and sentinel path.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Implemented Phase 2 stop/status; tests=<cmd>; status_fields=<list>`.

## Out Of Scope

Auto-spawn, fairness aging, tiny action executor.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: stop/status/budget fixtures.
- DOCUMENTED: status fields and STOP receipt schema documented.
- SURFACED: status output is the operator-facing autoloop truth surface.

title: [jr-capture-wiring-gap] wire documented Josh-request capture substrate
description:
## Goal
Wire the documented Josh-request capture substrate end to end so Joshua-originated requests are captured, triaged, promoted, and visible across Claude and Codex runtimes.

## Context
Audit josh-request-capture wiring on 2026-05-03 found the schema and Claude hook are present, but shipped/documented consumers are not all live. This blocks confidence in the feedback_substrate_watchtower_must_be_wired requirement and blocks flywheel-2p25 parity work.

## Dependencies
Blocks: flywheel-2p25. Related existing beads: flywheel-iaak (CLI), flywheel-a6ax (tick promotion script), flywheel-9wqw (wire promotion script into tick).

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: templates/josh-request-schema.md, ~/.claude/settings.json, ~/.claude/hooks/josh-request-capture.sh, ~/.local/state/flywheel/josh-requests.jsonl, .flywheel/MISSION.md, .flywheel/flywheel-loop-tick.
OUTPUTS: wired CLI, tick consumer, schema-v2-compatible hook/JSONL rows, codex parity path, and validation evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ~/.local/bin/josh-requests exists, is executable, and `josh-requests --help` exits 0.
- .flywheel/scripts/josh-request-tick-promote.sh exists, is executable, reads ~/.local/state/flywheel/josh-requests.jsonl, and emits tick prelude status for open/stale requests.
- .flywheel/flywheel-loop-tick calls .flywheel/scripts/josh-request-tick-promote.sh before dispatch phase selection or prompt emission.
- ~/.claude/hooks/josh-request-capture.sh writes schema v2 JSONL fields from templates/josh-request-schema.md section 4, not legacy v1 fields only.
- .flywheel/MISSION.md entries use schema v2 field names or a documented v2-compatible Markdown mapping.
- Codex parity gap is closed or explicitly routed: Codex-originated Joshua prompts are captured through an equivalent hook/driver/backfill path, not only Claude Code UserPromptSubmit.
- Re-run /tmp/jr-capture-wiring-audit.md checklist and all rows report wired=true, except any explicitly accepted non-applicable rows.

## Testing Obligations
- Run ~/.claude/hooks/josh-request-capture.test.sh and confirm pass.
- Run josh-requests --help plus at least one read-only list/show/stale command against a fixture or dry-run state file.
- Run the tick promotion script in dry-run/fixture mode and confirm deterministic JSON or Markdown prelude output.
- Verify no duplicate request rows are created from a repeated tick prompt.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: fix(josh-requests): wire capture substrate end to end [jr-capture-wiring-gap]
- Evidence attached in bead close reason, including audit path and command outputs.
- flywheel-2p25 dependency remains represented until this bead closes.
- AUTONOMY: autonomous

Forward-only schema v2 hook selected per option b; existing v1 rows left untouched and tolerated by CLI/tick consumers. Closed sub-beads: flywheel-iaak, flywheel-a6ax, flywheel-9wqw. Filed Codex parity follow-up flywheel-xap2, blocked by flywheel-orx1 and attached to flywheel-2p25. Evidence: /tmp/d62z-evidence.md. AUTONOMY: autonomous

title: [fleet-death-rca] codex panes exit clean to bash, no crash trace, 3x today
description:
## Symptom (3rd recurrence 2026-05-03)
Codex panes exit cleanly to bash with NO crash trace, NO stderr, NO panic in
~/.codex/log/codex-tui.log, NO macOS unified log entry. Pattern: agent
completes a tool call (codebase_search, macro_start_session, etc.) successfully,
then the codex TUI process disappears and the parent zsh resumes control.

## Today's instances
1. flywheel:3 — last visible action: socraticode.codebase_search OK; then prompt
2. flywheel:4 — last visible action: macro_start_session returned registration_token; then prompt (also L58 token-echo violation)
3. clutterfreespaces:1 — same clean-exit pattern (no scrollback retained)

Live panes still running codex without dying: flywheel:2, skillos:1, mobile-eats:1.
So this is NOT a global codex/system fault.

## Hypotheses (NONE PROVEN)
- H1: Codex exits voluntarily on certain "turn complete" conditions specific to
  worker dispatch flows (callback emitted → exit). Live panes are orchestrators
  not workers, which is why they survive.
- H2: An MCP server error from agent-mail (token-echo, file_reservateon_paths
  typo seen in pane 4 last call) is treated as fatal by codex MCP client.
- H3: Stale pane_current_command in tmux mis-reports state. Less likely — ps
  confirmed only zsh is alive in the dead panes.

## Cost
Joshua restart load: 3× today, kills entire flywheel orchestrator + multiple
workers each time. This is a workflow-killer, not a nuisance.

## Required next steps (NOT auto-fix)
1. Relaunch one codex worker in dead pane with `2>>/tmp/codex-stderr-$$.log`
   captured separately, wait for it to die, read exit reason. This is the only
   way to convert hypothesis to ground truth.
2. If H1 confirmed: find the agent-side exit hook and gate it on real "session
   over" conditions, not turn-complete.
3. If H2 confirmed: file ntm/agent-mail upstream issue + add MCP-error handling
   to codex launch wrapper.

## Acceptance
- One captured-stderr instance of clean exit
- Root cause classified (H1/H2/H3/other) with evidence
- Mitigation either shipped or filed upstream with reproducer

## Why this is NOT being auto-decided right now
Joshua said this kills flywheel session 3× today. He needs eyes on it. Per
feedback_data_guides_decisions_not_human_judgment.md, the data path is:
capture stderr → classify → fix. That requires one worker reload with logging
on, which is best done as its own dispatched task, not folded into this tick.

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The artifact, command, or doctrine surface named in `[fleet-death-rca] codex panes exit clean to bash, no crash trace, 3x today` is updated with close evidence.

title: [wire-or-explain] dispatch worker-side branch enforcement
description:
## Goal
Enforce worker-side branch discipline in dispatch packets and callbacks so local-main worker artifacts cannot be silently reset away.

## Source refs
- `02-REFINE-r2.md:101` and `:397-405` define B13.
- `03-AUDIT-r2-confirmation.md:56` requires opaque branch refs and identity proof.
- `03-AUDIT-r2-confirmation.md:90` and `:165` mark B13 Joshua-original and not deletable as Jeff prior art.

## Acceptance gates
1. Dispatch packet template requires worker branch/ref identity for implementation tasks that may commit.
2. Callback must report branch/ref proof and writer identity, not just a branch name.
3. Local-main worker commit proof is rejected or marked unresolved in the ledger.
4. Orchestrator merge path consumes branch/ref proof and writes a ledger row.
5. Fixture covers two workers in same repo without branch/ref collision.
6. No raw token or credential content is included in branch proof.

## Three-Q audit
VALIDATED: dispatch/callback fixtures prove reject/accept behavior. DOCUMENTED: worker branch contract is in dispatch template. SURFACED: B2 classifier and B14 reset guard consume branch proof.

## Out of scope
No DCG reset blocker implementation.

## DOD
Close with: `implemented worker side-branch dispatch contract; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[worker-branch-contract]`.
close_reason: implemented worker-side branch dispatch contract; tests=bash tests/wire-or-explain-classifier.sh gate worker_branch_records_ref_and_identity_hash PASS (classifier outputs artifact_class=worker_branch with refs/workers/ prefixed branch_ref and sha256 identity_proof_hash); local-main worker commit marked unresolved in ledger; orchestrator merge path consumes branch_ref proof writes ledger row; two-worker same-repo fixture covers no collision; no raw token in branch proof; commit_tag=[worker-branch-contract]

title: [doctrine-memory-wire] codify validation discipline into L-rules, memory, skill, and README surfaces
description:
### Title

`[doctrine-memory-wire] codify validation discipline into L-rules, memory, skill, and README surfaces`

### Goal

Wire the validation discipline into the ecosystem documentation surfaces after the executable gates exist. This bead converts proven behavior into durable doctrine: L-rule promotion path for L71+, memory references, README updates, and the new `orchestrator-validation-discipline` skill or approved extension.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `canonical_doctrine_drift_local`, `doctrine-orphaning`, and `skill-substrate-validation-drift`. L70 already landed this turn; future validation doctrine needs a disciplined promotion path rather than memory-only notes.

### Acceptance gates

1. New doctrine is added only after executable proof from B03-B09 exists. Proof receipt: `B10_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. AGENTS.md update follows L-rule schema with evidence, forbidden outputs, companion rules, review_due, and trauma_class. Proof receipt: `B10_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. README update is made if command/tick/worker behavior changes operator workflow. Proof receipt: `B10_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Memory note cross-references plan artifact, validation receipt evidence, and implementing beads. Proof receipt: `B10_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. New or extended skill includes triggers, exact usage pattern, anti-patterns, tests/fixtures, and executable command references. Proof receipt: `B10_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Doctrine update cites L60, L69, L70, `flywheel-1z65`, and `flywheel-7lby`. Proof receipt: `B10_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. A drift check verifies canonical AGENTS/README/memory/skill surfaces all mention the same validation primitive name. Proof receipt: `B10_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `agents_line=<line> readme_updated=<yes|no:reason> memory=<path> skill=<path> drift_check=<cmd>:PASS commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[doctrine-memory-wire]`.

### Doctrine references

L56, L60, L61, L69, L70; `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md`; `feedback_orchestrator_validates_callbacks.md`; `feedback_substrate_watchtower_must_be_wired.md`.

### Out of scope

No executable validation logic; no Phase 4 bead creation; no direct edit before Joshua-disposes approves final graph.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B05, B08, B09.  
Blocks: B12.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings and Lane B-prime corpus if Jeff pattern attribution changes wording or surface ownership.

### Phase 3 audit amendments before creation

Audit sources: `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:42`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:70`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:86`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md:118`.

- Doctrine landing must wait for executable proof from B13 and B14 in addition to B03-B09.
- B10 depends on B13 and B14 before adding L71, VALIDATE-CALLBACK, or THREE-Q doctrine as final rules.
- Any earlier doctrine text must be marked candidate/temporary until proof beads close.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B10_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B10_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B10_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B10_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B10_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B10_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B10_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [doctor-signal-mitigation-2] plan-to-bead conversion auto-trigger
description:
## Goal
When .flywheel/PLANS/<file>.md lands and is marked converged OR is older than 24h with no decomposed beads, auto-create [plan-decompose] bead.

## Mechanism
Cron or post-edit hook:
1. Walk .flywheel/PLANS/*.md
2. Read frontmatter for `converged: true` OR check mtime > 24h ago
3. grep beads DB for `tag:[plan-decompose]` referencing this plan path
4. If NO matching bead: br create [plan-decompose] convert <file> at P1, plan path in body, references plan.

## INCIDENTS reference
~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/INCIDENTS.md entry 2026-05-03T19:00Z `doctor-signal-fail-without-bead-promotion` mitigation #2

## Acceptance gates
1. Drop a fake plan with converged frontmatter → bead created within 1 cron cycle
2. Drop a fake plan WITHOUT converged frontmatter, mtime+25h → bead created
3. Plan with existing matching bead → no duplicate (idempotent)
4. flywheel-osz1 (current plan-to-bead conversion bead for bead-isolation) auto-filed if cron had been running

## Depends on
None — independent of mitigation-1

## Plan reference
~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/INCIDENTS.md `doctor-signal-fail-without-bead-promotion`

## DOD
COMMIT must include `[doctor-signal-mitigation-2]`.

title: [ntm-117-capture-provenance-upgrade] adopt Jeff PR #117 capture provenance — kills false-positive ERROR poisoning
description:
[ntm-117-capture-provenance-upgrade] adopt Jeff's per-pane capture provenance (ntm PR #117) into watcher + dispatch logic

## Trauma class
robot-mode-false-positive-poisoning — Jeff just shipped ntm#117 (8cd9301c) adding per-pane capture provenance to --robot-activity and tail. New fields: capture_collected_at, capture_provenance (live|unavailable), capture_error. Resolves our recurring "p2 ERROR but actually idle" false-positive that has poisoned dispatch decisions today (agentmail-mcp-disconnect symptom + multiple wrong-pane sends).

## Goal
Watcher (idle-pane-auto-dispatch.sh) and orchestrator dispatch logic distinguish:
- live + WAITING → dispatch
- live + ERROR/STALLED → skip + escalate
- unavailable + capture_error → infrastructure problem, NOT a worker problem
- live + THINKING → busy

## Acceptance gates

AG1: ntm version probe confirms PR #117 fields present (rebuild ntm if drift)
AG2: /tmp/idle-pane-auto-dispatch.sh updated to filter on `capture_provenance == "live" AND state == "WAITING"`

AG6: tests/pane-capture-provenance.sh covers fixtures: live-waiting, live-error, unavailable-with-error, unavailable-without-error
AG7: reference_jeff_substrate_inventory.md updated noting ntm PR #117 + version

title: [jeff-track-beads_rust-285] br close persists JSONL but not SQLite
description:
Filed: 2026-05-08 by flywheel:1 / LavenderGlen on alpsinsurance:1 intake.

URL: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/issues/285

## Status
OPEN, awaiting Jeffrey response.

## Source intake
xpane from alpsinsurance:1 (CoralRaven), grounded in:
- alps PR #528 (orchestrator-br-substrate-diagnostic-T20260508T205500Z) — diagnostic doc
- alps PR #527 — bulk reconciliation (27-bead session, 6/27 = class-A persistence bug, 21/27 = class-B record absence excluded)
- alps PR #525 — trauma-log

## Local cost
- alpsinsurance: drift 27 beads observed in single session reconciliation
- flywheel substrate: probe shows 24-row gap (1012 JSONL closed events vs 988 DB closed rows)
- Workers self-close via br close, downstream SQLite-reading consumers see status='open' → bead re-dispatched

## Local workaround in active use
Direct SQL with closed_at constraint:
UPDATE issues SET status='closed', closed_at=datetime('now'), updated_at=datetime('now'), close_reason='<reason>' WHERE id='<bead-id>';

Dirty-mark not triggered, so JSONL still drifts. Recovery: br sync --rebuild --json.

## Pre-screen
beads_rust#274 (closure-time policy gates) is a different concern (validation/attestation discipline at close-time, not persistence consistency). Non-duplicate.

## Lifecycle
- OPEN: filed 2026-05-08, awaiting Jeffrey ack
- IN_PROGRESS: when Jeffrey acknowledges with shape (CLOSED-explanation, ACCEPTED-queued, FIXED-shipped, PARTIAL)
- CLOSED: when upstream closes + we've absorbed the lesson + bumped br version (probably 0.2.6+)
notes: UPSTREAM_ACK 2026-05-08T23:34:52Z: Dicklesworthstone acknowledged beads_rust#285, left it OPEN as tracking issue, and requested two diagnostics to accelerate fix: (1) RUST_LOG=br::storage::sqlite=trace,br::cli::commands::close=trace br --lock-timeout 10000 close <id> against precise repro; (2) br doctor --json immediately after divergence. Local lifecycle now IN_PROGRESS; do not close until upstream closes/fix ships and local br version is absorbed.

title: [flywheel-cron-command] implement launchd cron install contract
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/flywheel-loop-tick-contracts-validation-architecture-2026-04-30.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-sgdp

## Goal

Implement `/flywheel:cron` as the deterministic launchd install contract for flywheel-managed recurring jobs.

## Scope

- Add `/Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/cron.md` or canonical script wrapper.
- Support `register`, `remove`, `list`, `status`, `logs`, `pause`, and `resume`.
- Register launchd jobs with explicit PATH, STOP sentinel, max-runtime, and substrate-registry row.
- Verify first fire with `RunAtLoad=true` when applying.
- Separate launchd-class automation from agentic `/flywheel:loop`.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `rg -n "register|remove|list|status|logs|pause|resume|EnvironmentVariables.PATH|STOP|max-runtime" /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/cron.md .flywheel/scripts README.md` proves command/docs coverage.
2. `cron register --dry-run --json` fixture emits planned plist, registry row, STOP sentinel, stdout/stderr paths, and explicit PATH.
3. Apply fixture writes a disabled or temp plist and validates it with `plutil`.
4. Register refuses command paths without executable file and shebang.
5. Remove/pause/resume dry-run fixtures report exact launchctl/plist actions without mutation.
6. Registry fixture proves every applied cron job gets `kind: launchd` with owner and review_due.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: plist, dry-run, apply, and invalid-command fixtures pass.
- DOCUMENTED: help distinguishes cron from loop and names anti-patterns.
- SURFACED: registry and status output make launchd jobs visible to doctor.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L48, L57, L60, L71, L82.
- Plan sections 7, 17, and 18.

## Out Of Scope

- No agentic loop activation.
- No worker tick implementation.
- No arbitrary launchd cleanup.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented /flywheel:cron launchd install contract; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[flywheel-cron-command]`.
close_reason: DID: implemented /flywheel:cron launchd install contract with register/remove/list/status/logs/pause/resume, plutil-validated dry-run/apply, executable path refusals, disabled-by-default apply, and kind=launchd registry rows. DIDNT: touch /flywheel:loop, autoloop, doctor libs, recovery installer scripts, dispatch-deferral lint, or root docs. GAPS: apply writes the plist and registry row but does not load launchd; that stays explicit operator action. Evidence: /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/cron.md,.flywheel/scripts/flywheel-cron.sh,tests/test_flywheel_cron_register_dry_run.sh,tests/test_flywheel_cron_apply_plutil_lint.sh,tests/test_flywheel_cron_refuses_non_executable.sh,tests/test_flywheel_cron_remove_pause_resume_dry_run.sh,tests/test_flywheel_cron_registry_kind_launchd.sh; commits: fb8f3e5,9fa2e31.

title: [wrapper-fix] dispatch-and-verify codex slow-start false-negative
description:
## Problem

`.flywheel/scripts/dispatch-and-verify.sh` reports STUCK / FAIL after 3 retries when codex worker takes >25s (15s initial + 5s × 2 retries) to start emitting output. Wrapper interprets velocity=0 as STUCK and fires empty-Enter retries. When codex finally starts processing (e.g. running socraticode_search or reading a long packet), the velocity is still 0 in the probe window, so wrapper exhausts retries.

Observed 2026-05-08T16:0xZ: dispatched 3 packets to flywheel:2/3/4 via dispatch-and-verify.sh. All 3 wrapper invocations exited 1 with FAIL diagnostic. Pane scrollback inspection confirmed all 3 workers WERE actively coding (sync-canonical-doctrine.sh edits, test fixtures, CLI registry). The wrapper's velocity-from-zero probe is too strict for codex's typical 30-60s warmup.

## Why this matters

L140 mandates dispatch-and-verify for every dispatch. False-negatives lead to:
- Orchestrator believing dispatch failed when work is in flight
- Multiple empty-Enter retries firing into a working pane (potential pollution)
- Spurious 'consider: ntm respawn' hints in the dispatch log

## Acceptance Gates

AG1: Replace velocity-from-zero probe with a multi-signal classifier:
- pane content delta (lines added/changed in the past N seconds)
- ntm changes count (filesystem mutations from the worker)
- pane state with a hysteresis window (require 2 consecutive STUCK reads)

AG2: Extend probe window from 15s+5s+5s = 25s to 30s+15s+15s = 60s OR until first content delta, whichever sooner.

AG3: Add `--probe-mode=permissive|strict` flag; permissive accepts THINKING with zero-velocity if pane content changed since send_ts.

AG4: Update L140 doctrine to cite this fix (the rule itself stays, but the canonical wrapper gets less false-negative).

## References

- L140 DISPATCH-AND-VERIFY-MANDATORY (just promoted 2026-05-08)
- doctrine: `.flywheel/doctrine/loop-non-accretive-trauma-class.md`
- script: `.flywheel/scripts/dispatch-and-verify.sh`
- observation row in dispatch-log.jsonl ts=2026-05-08T~16:0xZ
close_reason: Implemented multi-signal dispatch-and-verify classifier with permissive/strict probe modes, 30/15/15 probe window, two-STUCK hysteresis, L140 three-surface doctrine update, and regression coverage. Tests: bash tests/dispatch-and-verify.sh; bash tests/ntm-surface-changes.sh; bash tests/ntm-surface-conflicts.sh; jq empty .flywheel/scripts/tick-driver-manifest.json. AUTONOMY: autonomous.

title: [orch-capture-parity] define cross-runtime Joshua-input capture parity rule and signal
description:
### Title

`[orch-capture-parity] define cross-runtime Joshua-input capture parity rule and signal`

### Goal

Make every orchestrator runtime, including Claude Code, Codex, and future runtimes, surface Joshua-originated input through canonical capture or explicitly declare itself non-participating. This bead turns capture parity into a fleet participation rule and doctor-visible measurement, while leaving the specific per-runtime capture mechanism to implementation sub-beads.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `codex-feedback-gap`, `runtime-capture-parity-gap`, and `claude-first-orchestrator-paradigm`. The codex feedback probe found `~/.local/state/flywheel/josh-requests.jsonl` was effectively Claude-only while skillos/mobile-eats Codex orchestrators were receiving input through other channels. `flywheel-xap2` is now P0 for the Codex capture mechanism, and `flywheel-zuav` records the L71 candidate rule: every orch must surface Joshua input or be doctor non-conformant. Meadows analysis says the hook is only the #6 mechanism; B13 preserves the #3 goal and #5 rule so the next runtime does not repeat the same gap.

### Acceptance gates

1. A read-only capture parity probe enumerates active orchestrator runtimes from the canonical topology source and emits JSON rows with `session`, `pane`, `runtime`, `participation_state`, `capture_path`, `last_capture_ts`, `last_josh_input_seen_ts`, `gap_reason`, and `evidence_refs[]`. Proof receipt: `B13_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Probe fixtures cover at minimum: Claude hook capture present, Codex capture missing, Codex capture present through wrapper or agent-mail, explicit non-participating runtime, stale capture row, and all-clear fleet. Proof receipt: `B13_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Doctor integration exposes `orchs_with_capture_gap_count` and records per-runtime details; threshold is `>=1` warn during the L71 rollout window and fail after the chosen deadline or immediately if Joshua ratifies hard L71. Proof receipt: `B13_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. The probe never treats raw pane scrollback alone as proof of capture; Codex proof must include captured canonical row or agent-context callback evidence per L69. Proof receipt: `B13_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Dry-run remediation output recommends one of the approved mechanism tracks without mutating config: primary agent-mail/cross-orch route, secondary `ntm send` wrapper capture, or tertiary pane-tail poller only with explicit fragility note. Proof receipt: `B13_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Duplicate capture prevention is specified: repeated dispatch/tick prompts with the same prompt hash do not create duplicate Joshua-request rows. Proof receipt: `B13_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. `flywheel-xap2` integration is explicit: this bead defines the rule/signal contract, while xap2 or its children implement the chosen Codex capture mechanism. Proof receipt: `B13_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
8. Test output includes a fixture where Claude passes and Codex fails, proving `orchs_with_capture_gap_count=1` and a non-zero strict status. Proof receipt: `B13_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `capture_probe=<cmd>:PASS doctor_signal=orchs_with_capture_gap_count fixtures=<dir>:PASS xap2_link=<bead> no_bead_reason=<text-or-none> commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[orch-capture-parity]`.

### Doctrine references

L69, L70, L71-candidate (`flywheel-zuav`), L52, `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md`, `01-RESEARCH-MEADOWS.md`, `flywheel-2p25`, `flywheel-xap2`, `/tmp/codex-feedback-gap-probe.md`.

### Out of scope

No implementation of the mechanism choice itself. Agent-mail routing, `ntm send` wrapper capture, and pane-tail polling each belong in mechanism sub-beads under `flywheel-xap2` or its successors. No ratification of L71 into AGENTS.md; B10 owns doctrine landing after executable proof.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B01.  
Blocks: B04, B09, B10, B11, B12, B14.  
Related parents: `flywheel-2p25`, `flywheel-xap2`, `flywheel-zuav`.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: NONE. `flywheel-xap2` P0 should land first or in parallel, but B13 can define the rule/signal contract without waiting for the final mechanism.

### Wave assignment

Wave 3, before B14/B04 consume capture parity as a doctor producer and before B11 makes runtime parity claims involving Joshua-input capture.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B13_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B13_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B13_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B13_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B13_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B13_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B13_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG8` closes with `B13_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [canonical-cli-repair] flywheel-verdict json schema doctor
description:

Parent epic: flywheel-ntf

Gate(s) closed:
- flywheel-verdict doctor exists/exits cleanly/--json parseable: FAIL -> PASS for verdict DB readiness

- flywheel-verdict schema <command>: FAIL -> PASS for machine-readable output

Why this is top-5 leverage:
Verdict is Joshua-taste substrate. It has a small code surface and currently lacks JSON/schema health, making downstream automation scrape or avoid it. This is the cheapest high-value --json gate closure outside flywheel-loop.

- Existing verdict aliases keep mapping exactly: thumbs_up=keep, thumbs_down=drop, skip=defer.

title: [jeff-stack J5] socraticode-index-all-177
description:
Socraticode-index every one of the 177 Jeff repos at ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/<name>/.

DEPENDS ON: J3 (clones complete).

PROBLEM: daily-jeff-ingest.sh logs `socraticode_indexed: 0` every run because no Jeff repos are in socraticode index. Joshua's L63 doctrine mandates "auto-indexes each clone via mcp__socraticode__codebase_index"; current ledger shows 0/177 indexed.

PROCEDURE (long-running, possibly multi-hour):
1. Read list of all 177 canonical paths from `find ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -type d`
2. For each repo, in serial (socraticode may not be parallel-safe for index ops):
   - Call `mcp__socraticode__codebase_index` with projectPath=<canonical-path>, name=<repo-name>
   - Log result to /tmp/jeff-socraticode-index-progress.jsonl: {repo, status, indexed_chunks, duration, ts}
   - On failure: log error, continue to next (do not halt the queue)
3. After loop: call `mcp__socraticode__codebase_list_projects` and verify all 177 appear
4. Spot-check: `mcp__socraticode__codebase_search` query="agent flywheel" projectPath=<random Jeff repo> returns hits
5. Write summary to /tmp/jeff-socraticode-summary.md

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. mcp__socraticode__codebase_list_projects shows 177 jeff entries
2. /tmp/jeff-socraticode-index-progress.jsonl has 177 success rows OR documented failures
3. Sample search across 5 random repos returns indexed_chunks > 0 each
4. Total indexed_chunks logged in summary (rough size of Jeff knowledge corpus)

CONSTRAINTS:
- Resumable: re-running skips already-indexed repos (check codebase_list_projects first)
- Bounded: if a single repo takes >30min, log and skip (oversized; address separately)
- Memory: socraticode indexing can spike RAM; monitor and back off if needed

This bead may run for 4-8 hours. Worker should report progress via callback heartbeats every 30 min if possible, OR commit progress to ledger so Joshua can monitor.

title: [locked-worker-identities-survive-reboot] persistent (session,pane,role)→identity binding; workers read not mint
description:
[locked-worker-identities-survive-reboot] worker panes register fresh identity per dispatch — need persistent (session,pane,role) binding

## Trauma class
agent-mail-identity-sprawl-recurring — Joshua flagged 2026-05-04 (3rd time): "i'm still seeing workers grab new agentmail identities every time they register. they need to have locked identities that survive reboot"

## Meadows leverage
- #2 PARADIGM: registry was designed for orch (1 identity per session); never pre-allocated worker-pool identities. Each worker self-registers fresh → sprawl.
- #5 RULES: change rule from "worker self-registers fresh" to "worker reads (session,pane) binding from registry, refuses to register if missing"
- #6 INFORMATION FLOWS: doctor signal `worker_identity_registered_count` makes the gap visible per session

## Current state probe
- /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail/sessions/ has rows for: alpsinsurance:0, flywheel:1, mobile-eats:1, picoz:1, skillos:1
- MISSING: flywheel:2, flywheel:3, flywheel:4 (worker panes)
- Tokens dir has 3 files: BrightLake (skillos:1), RoseCliff (mobile-eats:1), RubyCreek (flywheel:1)
- Workers in flywheel session have NO persistent identity → re-register per dispatch

## Goal
Persistent (session, pane, role) → identity binding that survives:
- ntm session restart
- pane respawn
- Mac reboot
- agent-mail MCP server restart
- worker process restart

## Acceptance gates (canonical AG format)

AG1: registry schema extended to support pane roles: schema_version "agent-mail-identity-registry/v2" with role enum [orch, worker, callback, archived]
AG2: pre-allocation script `.flywheel/scripts/agent-mail-pre-allocate-worker-identities.sh` mints stable identities for ALL declared (session,pane) tuples per session-topology.jsonl
AG3: pre-allocation runs on session create AND on first probe if missing (idempotent — never mints duplicates if registry row exists)
AG4: worker dispatch packets include cited identity from registry (no self-register call) — workers READ identity from `~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail/sessions/<session>:<pane>.json`
AG5: doctor signal `worker_identity_registered_count` per session — fails if any pane in topology has no registry row
AG6: doctor signal `agentmail_orphan_session_rows_count` (rows for sessions/panes no longer in topology — needs archival not new mint)
AG7: dispatch-template enforces: workers MUST cite identity_name in callback envelope (proves they read the registry)
AG8: launchd plist (or cron) re-runs pre-allocation on Mac boot to handle reboot survival
AG9: tests/locked-worker-identities.sh covers fixtures: missing-pane (auto-mint) / existing-pane (no-op) / topology-shrunk (archive orphans) / cross-session-reboot (identity preserved)
AG10: AGENTS.md L## entry "L## LOCKED-WORKER-IDENTITIES-CANONICAL"
AG11: ft04 propagates pre-allocation script + L-rule fleet-wide
AG12: BACK-PROVISION existing flywheel session — mint flywheel:2/3/4 identities NOW from this dispatch's first action (clear today's pain immediately, then ship infra to prevent recurrence)

## Long-term fix doctrine
- Identity is a STATE not an EVENT — persists in registry, not minted on dispatch
- (session, pane, role) tuple is canonical key; identity_name is derived stable nickname (deterministic from session+pane+a fleet-wide salt so it survives even if registry is wiped — re-mint produces same name)
- Worker callbacks include identity_name proof; orch validates against registry on receive
- Sibling pattern of L75 (orch-blocker-coordination) and L76 (agentmail-identity-canonical) — completes the identity layer

## Skills to consult
- agent-mail (primary)
- agent-lifecycle
- canonical-owner-runtime-state (sibling — runtime state IS load-bearing)
- safe-migrations (v1 → v2 schema)
- testing-conformance-harnesses
- accretive-cron-orchestration (launchd plist)

## Memory siblings
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_agentmail_identity_canonical.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_agent_mail_token_echo.md (CRITICAL — never echo tokens)
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/reference_lavenderglen_fleet_mail.md

## Dependencies
- depends-on flywheel-g9mi (CLOSED — registry+schema v1)
- depends-on flywheel-2uin (CLOSED — broadcast machinery)
- depends-on flywheel-ca37 (OPEN — orphan archival, related but separate)
- supersedes ad-hoc per-pane registration in dispatches

## Priority: P0 (RECURRING trauma — Joshua flagged 3 times)
## Estimate: 4-5 dispatches (~12 worker-min)

title: [codex-watchtower] systematic surveillance of openai/codex bugs/releases — first-class substrate observability
description:
Codex CLI is first-class infrastructure: every Codex pane in our ntm fleet runs Codex. We are pinned to 0.125.0 because gpt-5.5 was removed in later versions. Today alone we hit openai/codex#12645 (kitty-keyboard+tmux Enter drop) 5+ times = ~30min of stuck panes + Joshua frustration.

We need persistent surveillance on Codex's what/where/how/when, paired with our pinned-version reality.

Five surfaces to track (each with refresh cadence):

1. WHAT — open issues affecting our pinned version (0.125.0) on macOS arm64 + tmux + iTerm2 environment. Daily refresh.
2. WHERE — which Codex internals are in flux (input/Enter/kitty-keyboard, EventBroker, model availability matrix). Weekly refresh.
3. HOW — workarounds + recovery paths the community has found. Append-only knowledge base.
4. WHEN — release cadence + which versions broke/fixed which behavior. Tracks our upgrade-blocked-on-gpt-5.5 constraint.
5. WHY — why we're pinned (gpt-5.5 removal); when does that change (gpt-5.5 deprecation timeline; 5.5+/6 migration plan).

Five deliverables:

A. Skill 'codex-cli-tracker' (new skill in ~/.claude/skills/codex-cli-tracker/):
   - SKILL.md with FOREVER-RULES (e.g., 'Every Codex freeze is openai/codex#XXXX until proven otherwise — search before respawning')
   - references/UPSTREAM-ISSUES.md — curated list of issues affecting our env (12645, 7187, 13666, 5905, 12542, 20580, 20501, 19814, 14048, 7377)
   - references/PINNED-VERSION-CONSTRAINTS.md — why 0.125, when can we upgrade
   - references/RECOVERY-PATTERNS.md — Ctrl-C-relaunch (12645), --disable shell_snapshot (7187), full ~/.codex clear, etc.
   - data/sources.json with refresh schedule
   - scripts/codex-tracker-probe.sh --doctor --json — checks pinned version, fetches recent openai/codex issues, flags new ones in our env

B. Daily ingest script ~/.local/bin/codex-watchtower-daily.sh:
   - cron 09:00Z daily
   - gh api repos/openai/codex/issues --paginate (filter to last 24h)
   - gh api repos/openai/codex/releases (filter to last 24h)
   - Summarize new+relevant matches → fuckup-log entry IF an issue matches our active reproducer pattern
   - Append to ~/.local/state/flywheel/codex-watchtower/daily-YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl
   - L61 dual-channel: NTM ping flywheel:1 + agent-mail durable for any HIGH-relevance hits

C. Tick integration: /flywheel:tick gets new Step 4t 'codex-watchtower-probe':
   - Reads latest daily ingest
   - Surfaces new HIGH-relevance issues in tick receipt
   - Cross-references with frozen-pane-detector strikes
   - Auto-files beads when an issue matches a fuckup class with strike count >=3

D. INCIDENTS entry at ~/Developer/flywheel/INCIDENTS.md:
   - 2026-05-03 5+ strikes of #12645 with our pinned-version constraint
   - Recovery doctrine: Ctrl-C-relaunch (NOT bare-Enter)
   - Detection: velocity=0 + chevron_prompt + state_since>5min in tmux Codex pane

E. Upstream coordination doctrine (L69+ when ratified):
   - When a Codex bug fires 3+ times in a week, file a comment on the upstream issue with our reproducer (passes thankfulness test if it's specific env-match data)
   - DO NOT file new Codex issues until 3-strike rule + dedup vs existing issues (same as Jeff issue gate)
   - Track in skills/codex-cli-tracker/references/UPSTREAM-COMMENTS.md

Acceptance gates:
1. Skill scaffolded with all 5 ref files populated from search results we already have
2. Daily ingest script + launchd plist installed
3. tick.md Step 4t patched + bead validates the probe
4. INCIDENTS entry written
5. First daily ingest run completes successfully
6. Cross-reference with flywheel-3pko (this session's reproducer) wired

Skills cite:
- source-a: codebase-archaeology, dicklesworthstone-stack (mirror Jeff issue surveillance pattern), gh-cli, agent-monitoring
- source-b: research-triad, accretive-cron-orchestration, automating-your-automations
- source-c: skill-builder, agent-fleet-management

Origin: Joshua directive 2026-05-03T17:21Z 'codex is a first class citizen in our environment, we have to have close tabs on the what, where, how, and when of why they keep doing this'
Today's pain: 5+ #12645 strikes, ~30min lost, manual Ctrl-C-relaunch each time.
Pinned version: 0.125.0 (gpt-5.5 dependency).

Parent meta-skill cross-reference (flywheel-1ndw): codex-watchtower is CHILD #1 of ~/.claude/skills/info-source-watchtower/. Keep codex-specific artifacts as a specialization of the parent PATTERN.md four-state gate, SIGNAL-CLI.md subcommands, env-match filter, 3-strike upstream-contact rule, and L66 public-posting gate. dicklesworthstone-stack remains the attributed pattern source, not a replaced artifact.

title: [fleet-skill-discovery-notify] wire skillos NTM notifications and Agent Mail threads
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/fleet-skill-reporting-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-xbuh

## Goal

Wire cross-session notifications and Agent Mail threads so skill discoveries reach skillos and all source sessions can see shared candidate context.

## Scope

- On fresh `sd-*` row, look up skillos coordinator pane from fleet roster/session topology.
- Send a token-safe `ntm send skillos --pane=<orch> ...` notification for fresh discoveries.
- Create or update one Agent Mail thread per `candidate_skill_name`.
- Add source sessions as participants where Agent Mail permissions allow.
- Auto-archive candidate threads when a skill ships or 30 days pass with no new sightings.

## Acceptance Gates

1. Dry-run notification command prints target session, pane, candidate skill, discovery id, and message without sending.
2. Apply-mode notification uses absolute `/Users/josh/.local/bin/ntm send` and never includes raw Agent Mail tokens or secrets.
3. Agent Mail dry-run or fixture creates deterministic thread key `[skill-discovery] <candidate_skill_name>`.
4. Two discoveries for the same candidate reuse the same thread key rather than creating duplicates.
5. Auto-archive fixture marks a thread archived when `skill_shipped=true` or `last_sighting_age_days >= 30`.
6. If skillos topology is missing, command returns a structured `skillos_target_unavailable` result and leaves the `sd-*` row intact for next tick.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: dry-run fixtures cover notify, thread create/reuse, archive, and missing topology.
- DOCUMENTED: notification/thread behavior is described in script help or README.
- SURFACED: skillos receives NTM notification and Agent Mail carries candidate history.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L29, L55, L58, L61, L70, L71.
- Plan lines 133-147 and 176-181.
- Agent Mail token safety doctrine.

## Out Of Scope

- No discovery schema implementation.
- No skill-builder bead creation.
- No quality scoring.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `wired skill-discovery notifications and Agent Mail threads; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[fleet-skill-discovery-notify]`.
close_reason: wired skill-discovery notifications and Agent Mail threads; tests=tests/test_skillos_notify.sh; commit_tag=[fleet-skill-discovery-notify]

title: [worker-verify-callback-delivered] workers must verify their callback ntm send actually delivered before declaring DONE
description:
[worker-verify-callback-delivered] workers must verify their callback ntm send actually delivered before declaring DONE/closing

## Trauma class
`worker-callback-not-delivered` — Joshua flagged 2026-05-03T23:36Z: "periodically seeing workers not properly sending message back to pane 1 (or their orchestrator) and then validating that it sent before closing up shop for the task"

The failure mode: worker completes work, calls `ntm send flywheel --pane=1 ...`, ntm reports "Sent to 1 pane(s)", worker declares DONE — BUT the message either:
1. Failed to land on orchestrator pane (pane busy / queued / lost)
2. Landed but was malformed (FTS5 escape, truncated)
3. ntm reported success but tmux key injection silently failed (Codex queued-not-submitted bug)
4. Worker exited/closed before confirming delivery

Result: orchestrator never validates, bead stays in-flight in dispatch-log, worker thinks it's done. Silent darkness.

## Why this is the worker-side companion to feedback_verify_ntm_send
- `feedback_verify_ntm_send.md` is ORCHESTRATOR-side: verify YOUR sends to workers landed
- This bead is WORKER-side: verify YOUR callback to orchestrator landed before declaring task complete
- Both directions of the same FTS5/queue/silence bug class

## Acceptance gates
1. Worker dispatch-template adds REQUIRED post-callback verification step before "task complete":
   - After `ntm send <orch_session> --pane=<orch_pane> "DONE ..."`
   - Wait 3-5s
   - Verify via ONE OF:
     a. `ntm logs <orch_session> --panes=<orch_pane> | tail -20` — confirm callback text present
     b. `ntm copy <orch_session>:<orch_pane> -l 30 --redact redact --output /tmp/<task_id>-callback-verify.txt` and grep for callback signature
     c. Re-send with `--no-cass-check` if not detected (idempotent, includes task_id for dedupe)
2. Worker emits `callback_delivery_verified=<true|false|unknown>` in their final output
3. If verification fails after 3 retries: worker writes evidence to `/tmp/<task_id>-callback-failed.md` and pauses — does NOT exit clean
4. Doctor signal `worker_callback_not_verified_count` exists and surfaces in flywheel-loop doctor JSON
5. Tests in tests/ verify the wrapper detects: success, queued-not-submitted, FTS5-escape-failure, pane-disappeared
6. Dispatch-template (B02 output at /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/_shared/dispatch-template.md) updated with VERIFY-CALLBACK BLOCK
7. Existing in-flight workers see this in their dispatch packet on next dispatch (no retroactive change for in-flight beads)

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: tests pass; receipt fixtures cover all 4 failure modes
- DOCUMENTED: dispatch-template VERIFY-CALLBACK BLOCK; AGENTS.md L? (probably L71)
- SURFACED: doctor signal `worker_callback_not_verified_count`; canonical-paths.txt entry

## Doctrine refs
- `feedback_verify_ntm_send.md` (orch-side companion)
- `feedback_callback_first_dispatch.md`
- `feedback_orchestrator_validates_callbacks.md`
- AGENTS.md L70 (no-punt — verifying delivery IS the work)

- .flywheel/scripts/validate-callback.py (B03 — orch-side validator, may also probe delivery proof)

## Companion: send-side bug class
ntm has known bugs (FTS5 escape on long prompts, Codex queued-not-submitted). Workers can't fix ntm but CAN verify post-send. This rule is the human-facing-mitigation while Jeff's ntm fixes propagate.

## Why P0
- Joshua periodic observation = recurring trauma
- Silent worker DONE = orchestrator can't validate
- Compounds: if 1/N callbacks lost per cycle, plan progress drops geometrically
- Direct mission threat to validate-and-redispatch foundation

title: [alps-loop-repair] clear doctor blockers for scheduled spin

Clear ALPS loop doctor blockers so the active loop marker can be backed by a healthy scheduled driver.

## Context

## Acceptance Criteria

- Mission lock status has a valid lock-log path or a documented owner-review decision.

- NTM pane health mismatch is resolved or classified as an ntm false-positive with evidence.

## Testing Obligations

title: [dispatch-topology-gate] validate callback and worker panes from topology
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/dispatch-enforcement-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-pnr9
Plan predecessor: flywheel-8i5

## Goal

Implement topology-backed pane validation for dispatches and callbacks so hard-coded panes cannot silently route work to the wrong recipient.

## Scope

- Add helper `_topology_expected_pane session mode` or equivalent reusable function in the dispatch transport hook.
- Read latest row for a session from `/Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/session-topology.jsonl`.
- In callback context, require pane equals `callback_pane // orchestrator_pane`.
- In worker dispatch context, require pane is not human/orchestrator/callback pane and matches dispatch wrapper selection.
- Add `topology_resolved_pane`, `topology_role`, and `topology_row_effective_at` to dispatch receipts.
- Specifically catch historical `picoz --pane=0` mismatch when topology declares pane 1.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `rg -n "_topology_expected_pane|session-topology.jsonl|topology_resolved_pane|topology_role|topology_row_effective_at" /Users/josh/.claude/hooks/flywheel-loop-dispatch-transport-gate.sh /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/dispatch.md /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/_shared/dispatch-template.md` proves topology wiring landed.
2. Hook fixture denies callback-shaped send to a non-callback pane and reports expected vs actual pane.
3. Hook fixture allows callback-shaped send to topology-declared callback pane.
4. Hook fixture denies worker dispatch to human/orchestrator/callback pane.
5. Dispatch-log schema v2 fixture contains topology fields and the effective topology timestamp.
6. Missing topology row is handled explicitly as `topology_unavailable`, not by falling back silently to pane 0.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: hook fixtures cover callback, worker, mismatch, and missing-topology cases.
- DOCUMENTED: dispatch/template docs forbid hard-coded panes and show topology lookup.
- SURFACED: dispatch receipts and hook-block rows expose expected and actual panes.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L29, L57, L67, L71.
- Trauma classes: `callback-pane-wrong-no-topology-read`, `orch-hardcoded-pane-zero-for-picoz`.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.

## Out Of Scope

- No callback field validation beyond pane routing.
- No worker-substrate classification.
- No migration/backfill of legacy rows.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented topology-backed pane validation; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[dispatch-topology-gate]`.

title: [jeff-stack J3] clone-all-177-jeff-repos-canonical
description:
Clone all 177 Jeff repos to canonical location ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/<repo-name>/ as full working trees (not bare mirrors — socraticode needs files).

DEPENDS ON: J0 (gh auth), J2 (sources list with all 177).

PROCEDURE:
1. mkdir -p ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/
2. Read /tmp/jeff-repos.json (from J2)
3. For each non-archived repo, in parallel batches of 8 (don't saturate network):
   - Skip if ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/<name>/ exists with valid .git (this lets J3 be re-runnable)
   - git clone https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/<name>.git ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/<name>
   - On failure: log to /tmp/jeff-clone-failures.jsonl with {repo, error, ts}; continue
4. After loop: dedupe count via `find ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack -maxdepth 2 -name .git | wc -l`
5. Write summary to /tmp/jeff-clone-summary.md: cloned, skipped, failed, total disk usage

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/ contains 177 directories each with .git
2. /tmp/jeff-clone-failures.jsonl is empty OR every failure has a documented reason (private repo, archived, deleted)
3. Total disk under 50GB (warn if approaching, abort if exceeding)
4. README at ~/Developer/dicklesworthstone-stack/README.md documents canonical path policy + last clone ts

CONSTRAINTS:
- Use HTTPS not SSH (gh auth via keychain works for HTTPS)
- DO NOT shallow clone (--depth=1) — daily diff needs full history for git log --since
- DO NOT clone --mirror — socraticode needs working tree files
- Resumable: re-running picks up where it left off
- Network polite: max 8 parallel clones, retry-on-fail with exponential backoff

title: [ntm-wire-in wave-2 P1] wire native ntm agents/analytics/cass/config/extract/get-all-session-text/memory/resume (8 surfaces)
description:

plan_origin=ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07
parent=flywheel-a2lff
disposition_doc=.flywheel/PLANS/ntm-surface-wire-in-USE-ISSUE-WRAP-2026-05-07/12-NOT-WIRED-DISPOSITIONS.md
predecessor=flywheel-opwu8

WAVE-2 P1: wire 8 P1 WIRE-IT surfaces from a2lff disposition. Pattern follows opwu8 (4 P0 wire-ins shipped 51 callsites, -2200 LOC delta, 0 native gaps).

SURFACES (each anchored at #<surface> in disposition doc):

1. agents (#agents) — agent capability metadata; replaces scattered worker identity/capability handling
2. analytics (#analytics) — fleet analytics aggregation; replaces local report aggregation
3. cass (#cass) — CASS memory/context lookup; replaces ad-hoc cass invocations
4. config (#config) — NTM config surface; wire into doctor/validation
5. extract (#extract) — pane code-block extraction; replaces hand-rolled pane-text parsing
6. get-all-session-text (#get-all-session-text) — full-session text capture; replaces per-pane aggregation
7. memory (#memory) — CASS memory native lookup; replaces local memory probes
8. resume (#resume) — handoff/recovery resume surface; wire into handoff closeout

ACCEPTANCE per surface (same contract as opwu8):
- >=3 callsites of native ntm <surface> after wire-in
- LOC delta tracked per surface (sum target: -300 to -500 across all 8)
- existing tests pass; new test per surface in tests/ntm-surface-<name>.sh
- canonical-cli-scoping checklist in commit message per surface

PROCESS (mirror opwu8):
1. socraticode K=10 per surface against /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel
2. ntm <surface> --help to confirm flags
3. Replace hand-rolled with native; preserve fallback only for genuine native gaps
4. Per-surface fixture test
5. Run existing test suite
6. 8 separate commits (one per surface) for revertability

ANTI-PATTERNS (carried from opwu8 wave-1):
- DO NOT delete fallback path until 3 callsites measured AND tests pass
- DO NOT bundle commits
- Stage commits with explicit paths only; the strict_git pack blocks the all-paths flag

CALLBACK:
ntm send flywheel --pane=1 'DONE flywheel-pane4-<bead-id> task_id=<bead-id> did=8/8 evidence=/tmp/flywheel-<bead-id>-evidence.md compliance_pack_path=<path> agents_callsites=<N> analytics_callsites=<N> cass_callsites=<N> config_callsites=<N> extract_callsites=<N> get_all_session_text_callsites=<N> memory_callsites=<N> resume_callsites=<N> commits=<N> tests_pass=yes loc_delta=<N> surfaces_blocked_by_native_gap=<list-or-none>'

title: Fix canonical sync helper timeout on fleet drift checks
description:
## Goal
Make sync-canonical-doctrine.sh complete bounded fleet drift checks without timing out on the current canonical target set.

## Context
During flywheel-g0qv9, the default sync-canonical-doctrine.sh --check --json path and an explicit-root precheck both exceeded useful dispatch bounds while resolving canonical-root drift. A scoped root/snapshot helper over enumerated target repos succeeded and canonical-root-drift-fleet-check passed, so the close-relevant drift is fixed but the default helper still needs performance/scoping repair.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Run canonical-root-drift-fleet-check.sh --json with the default sync helper and get status=pass, timed_out=false.
- Add or update a regression that fails if the helper recursively scans large repo trees when direct repo roots are supplied.
- Preserve --json output fields used by canonical-root-drift-fleet-check.

## Testing Obligations
- bash tests/canonical-root-drift-fleet-check.sh
- A targeted timeout/performance fixture for explicit-root direct .flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md lookup.

## Definition of Done
- Default bounded verifier no longer needs the receipt-local workaround from flywheel-g0qv9.
- Evidence references .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-g0qv9/.
