title: [flywheel-recovery] canonical fleet snapshot + restore command
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03T17:54Z: this is /flywheel:recovery — the canonical fleet snapshot+restore command we discovered we needed mid-session. Tactical pre-cursor restore-fleet.sh exists at ~/Desktop/restore-fleet.sh; this bead is for the canonical SKILL build.

## Problem

Today during a Codex fleet freeze, Joshua needed to reboot. We have:
- /flywheel:handoff — writes per-repo handoff doc (works, used today)
- ntm session save/restore patterns (in vibing-with-ntm + claude-md-ntm.md)
- restore-fleet.sh hand-rolled tactical script

Gap: no canonical command that composes ALL of:
1. Capture pre-reboot fleet state (sessions, panes, agent types, in-flight dispatches, pinned versions)
2. Write resume manifest to disk
3. Generate restore script tied to manifest
4. Post-reboot: idempotent restore that brings up sessions + relaunches agents + verifies health
5. Resume orchestration: dispatch to specific panes per manifest

## Acceptance gates

A. **Skill scaffolded** at ~/.claude/skills/flywheel-recovery/ following info-source-watchtower meta-pattern (just shipped flywheel-1ndw):
   - SKILL.md with FOREVER-RULES (idempotent, fail-open, never lose work)
   - THE EXACT PROMPT for invocation
   - references/MANIFEST-SCHEMA.md (what gets snapshot)
   - references/RESTORE-PATTERN.md (ntm session create + agent relaunch sequence)

B. **Slash command** at .claude/commands/flywheel/recovery.md with subcommands:
   - /flywheel:recovery snapshot — capture current fleet state to ~/.flywheel/recovery/<ts>.json + write restore-fleet-<ts>.sh
   - /flywheel:recovery restore — read latest snapshot + invoke restore script + verify health
   - /flywheel:recovery list — list available snapshots
   - /flywheel:recovery diff <snapshot-id> — show what would change vs current

C. **Composes existing skills** (don't reinvent):
   - vibing-with-ntm for session create/save patterns
   - flywheel:handoff for per-repo state capture (recovery is fleet-wide; handoff is per-repo)
   - flywheel:respawn for pane-level recovery (tactical)
   - canonical-cli-scoping for --doctor/--info/--examples/--schema CLI surface

D. **Manifest schema** captures:
   - For each session: name, cwd, pane count, layout, per-pane (idx, agent_type, last commit/state, working bead, in-flight dispatches)
   - launchd plist references (which loops are running)
   - socraticode index state (which projects active)
   - Watchtower daily ingest state
   - pinned versions of all Jeff binaries (codex, ntm, br, dcg, cass, mcp_agent_mail)

E. **Idempotency**: running restore twice safely no-ops if sessions already up
F. **Fail-open**: if any single pane fails to restore, restore continues; report at end
G. **Test path**: snapshot → reboot → restore → verify pane count + agent types match manifest
H. **Cron hook (optional)**: tick.md Step 4w writes a snapshot every N ticks so recovery has fresh state
I. **INCIDENTS entry** documenting today's discovery: gap surfaced when Codex fleet freeze required reboot mid-session

## Skills cite (source-(a))

- info-source-watchtower (PARENT meta-pattern, just shipped)
- vibing-with-ntm (NTM primitives)
- flywheel:handoff (sibling, per-repo not fleet)
- canonical-cli-scoping (CLI contract)
- skill-builder (canonical scaffolding)

## Constraints

- READ-WRITE on ~/.claude/skills/flywheel-recovery/ (NEW)
- READ-WRITE on .claude/commands/flywheel/recovery.md (NEW)
- READ-WRITE on ~/.flywheel/recovery/ (NEW manifest dir)
- shellcheck pass on all scripts
- DO NOT remove the tactical restore-fleet.sh from ~/Desktop until canonical is verified working
- DO NOT auto-load any cron without Joshua approval

## Origin

Joshua directive 2026-05-03T17:54Z: 'this is the flywheel:recovery process I was talking about wanting'
Pre-cursor: ~/Desktop/restore-fleet.sh + ~/Desktop/RESUME-AFTER-REBOOT-2026-05-03.txt
Trigger event: Codex fleet freeze caused need for reboot; existing /flywheel:handoff captured per-repo state but no fleet-wide restore exists.
close_reason: DONE 9/9 did/didnt/gaps validated, evidence /tmp/flywheel-fpza-evidence.md, tests PASS

title: [doctrine-propagation] sync-canonical-doctrine.sh must propagate to ROOT AGENTS.md, not just .flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md
description:
[doctrine-propagation] sync-canonical-doctrine.sh must propagate to ROOT AGENTS.md, not just .flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md

## Trauma class
`doctrine-canonical-vs-root-drift` — every flywheel-installed repo has BOTH a root AGENTS.md (what agents read first) AND a .flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md (what doctrine sync writes). The current sync script only writes the second. Root AGENTS.md drifts independently in EACH repo. Discovered 2026-05-03 when Joshua flagged "skillos needs full L70 stamp too" — root AGENTS.md in skillos had 0 L70 references while .flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md had 3. Mobile-eats only got L70 because flywheel orch sent explicit cross-orch ntm send.

## Root cause
`/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/sync-canonical-doctrine.sh` writes target `.flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md` but never touches root AGENTS.md. Codex orchs (and arguably most agents) follow the pattern of reading root AGENTS.md first. Result: doctrine lands in canonical-snapshot files but not in the file agents actually read.

## Why P0
- Joshua flagged this exact gap: "this needs to be system wide not per repo ad hoc"
- L70 was specifically added to fix orch-punt-trauma but the rule is invisible in skillos+mobile-eats roots
- The codex-feedback-gap probe (2026-05-03) confirmed: doctrine propagation is the load-bearing path for codex-orch behavior since codex has no UserPromptSubmit hook
- Recurring fleet-killer: every new L-rule repeats this drift unless mechanism is fixed

## Acceptance gates
1. `sync-canonical-doctrine.sh --apply` either:
   - (Option A) writes a marker section in target root AGENTS.md (e.g. delimited block "## CANONICAL FLYWHEEL DOCTRINE — auto-synced, do not edit") replacing the prior block atomically, OR
   - (Option B) refuses to exit 0 if target root AGENTS.md drifts from canonical (lint-mode) AND `flywheel-loop doctor` surfaces it as a doctor signal
2. `flywheel-loop doctor --json | jq .canonical_root_drift` exists and reports per-repo drift state
3. doctor-signal-bead-promotion.sh promotes drift to a bead automatically
4. backup-before-write semantics preserved (existing `.bak.<ts>` pattern)
5. mobile-eats, skillos, alpsinsurance, flywheel all show L70 + L69 + three-question audit umbrella in root AGENTS.md after first apply
6. Test added to test-sync-canonical-doctrine.sh covering root-AGENTS sync mode

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: tests pass, all 4 repos show synced root AGENTS post-apply
- DOCUMENTED: sync-canonical-doctrine.sh --help describes new mode; AGENTS.md L? records the canonical-block protocol
- SURFACED: doctor signal landing as bead automatically; new doctor field in JSON

## Doctrine refs
- AGENTS.md L61 (ecosystem-wire-in)
- AGENTS.md L70 (ORCH-NO-PUNT — this is what failed to propagate)
- feedback_substrate_watchtower_must_be_wired.md
- feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md (umbrella)
- feedback_wire_into_ecosystem.md

## Companion: cross-orch directive
While this bead is in flight, the orchestrator should still send L70 cross-orch directives to skillos:1 and any other live codex orchs whose root AGENTS.md is stale, citing this bead as the systemic fix.

title: [doctrine] skill-autoresearch python-vs-shell tooling preference mismatch class
description:
## Source

Three consecutive skill-enhance / skillos-new-skill-request beads closed scope-pass with four_lens scores below 7:

- flywheel-spdu (skill-enhance-beads-br) — brand:4,sniff:4,jeff:5,public:3, before:3.54 → after:5.25
- flywheel-2gvl (skillos-new-skill-request mutation-safety-contract) — 4/5/5/4
- flywheel-njzi (skillos-new-skill-request ipc-transport-contract) — 4/5/5/4

ALL THREE workers cited the SAME root cause:
`skill_autoresearch_operational_tooling_python_preference_mismatch`

Diagnostic: skill-autoresearch substrate prefers Python operational tooling but the canonical flywheel surface (canonical-cli-scoping, jsm, beads-br, agent-orchestration) is shell-first. Workers correctly bound scope (no out-of-scope mutations) but the underlying tooling-preference mismatch produces low brand/sniff/public scores because the resulting skill files don't match flywheel surface conventions.

Workers in flywheel-werb (agent-memory) and flywheel-w304 (accretive-cron-orchestration) and flywheel-puwl (agent-orchestration) and flywheel-raq3 (validation-fixture-contract) and flywheel-cmf4 (failure-taxonomy-receipts) all scored 8-9 on the same patterns — so it's not a worker-skill issue, it's a target-skill-class issue.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Audit skill-autoresearch SKILL.md + scripts/ to identify python-vs-shell tooling preference. Document explicitly which substrates skill-autoresearch is suited for vs not.

AG2: Author doctrine page `.flywheel/doctrine/skill-autoresearch-tooling-preference-class.md` capturing: the three failure beads, the root cause, the working pattern (5 beads scored 8-9), the failing pattern (3 beads scored 4-5), and the routing rule (which skill-enhance bead-class needs which substrate).

AG3: Update dispatch packet contract: when bead targets a shell-first skill (canonical-cli-scoping, jsm, beads-br, agent-orchestration), DON'T route through skill-autoresearch. When bead targets a python-friendly skill (skill-builder-managed), skill-autoresearch is fine.

AG4: Re-route the three failed beads (spdu, 2gvl, njzi) — do NOT re-dispatch as-is. Re-author with explicit shell-first tooling guidance OR park as known-pattern-mismatch.

AG5: Propagate doctrine via doctrine-sync 6h cadence to peer orchs (alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, skillos) so they don't repeat the same pattern.
close_reason: landed L147 skill-autoresearch tooling substrate routing doctrine; packet guard and regression test added; failed beads parked known-pattern-mismatch; doctrine-sync dry-run receipts for alpsinsurance/mobile-eats/skillos; tests=PASS

title: [C] register asupersync tentacle substrate
description:
## Goal
Add an asupersync tentacle substrate probe for async-runtime source freshness and orphan-task discipline.

## Context
asupersync underlies vc daemon runtime and future Rust daemons; runtime drift should be visible.

## Dependencies
Depends on B2 for current checkout/fetch state.

## Inputs / Outputs
Files in scope: ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/data/substrate-registry.json; ~/Developer/asupersync; consumer Cargo pins
Output artifact: committed implementation and/or evidence artifact named in acceptance criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria
- probe emits JSON with source recency, consumer pin evidence, and orphan-task test status or explicit not-run reason

## Testing Obligations
- Run git/source checks and a bounded test/probe if available; validate JSON shape.

## Origin
00-MASTER-PLAN.md §IV.5 lines 312-319; 05-doctrine-comparison async runtime notes.

## Out of Scope
Do not refactor Rust runtime consumers.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: include [C10] in the commit message or close reason.

## Goal
Bash helper that takes a skill name + version, sends fleet-mail to skillos, logs dispatch entry.

## Path
`~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/handoff-skill-to-skillos.sh`

## Contract (canonical-cli-scoping)
handoff-skill-to-skillos.sh [--dry-run] [--batch] <skill-name> [<version>]

Exit codes: 0 sent, 1 fleet-mail send failed, 2 skill not found, 3 ownership=forbidden (no handoff needed), 4 already-handed-off-this-version

JSON stdout: {"action":"sent|skipped|forbidden|duplicate","message_id":<int|null>,"skill":"<name>","version":"<X.Y.Z>","ownership":"local|upstream"}

## Audit findings addressed
- F1 (idempotency): check skillos receipts at `~/Developer/skillos/state/<skill>-v<X.Y>-*.json` before sending
- F3 (information-leak): consult `jsm show <name>`; short-circuit exit 3 if upstream_owned

## Acceptance gates
1. --dry-run prints expected message, sends nothing
2. Re-running for same skill+version exits 4 (no duplicate)
3. Jeff-owned skill (e.g. vibing-with-ntm) exits 3 (forbidden)
4. Successful send appends entry to `.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl` with `event=skillos_handoff_sent`
5. Uses fleet-mail-project at `/Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/fleet-mail-project`

title: [frankenterm-adoption] migrate fleet after v0.1 release
description:
## Goal
Adopt FrankenTerm for the fleet only after a public v0.1+ release exists and a migration canary passes.

## Context
Follow-up from flywheel-ubrb5. Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm is public, indexed in the Jeff corpus, and watchtower-monitored, but latestRelease is currently null as of 2026-05-08.

## Dependencies
Depends on flywheel-ubrb5 watchtower/lifecycle close. Operational trigger is a future FrankenTerm v0.1+ release announcement.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm release metadata, /Users/josh/Developer/jeff-corpus/frankenterm Socraticode index, active fleet sessions flywheel/alpsinsurance/mobile-eats/skillos.
OUTPUTS: migration canary receipt, per-session rollout/rollback status, any blocking bug beads.

## Acceptance Criteria
AG1: gh repo view Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm shows latestRelease.tagName matching v0.1.0 or higher.
AG2: FrankenTerm install/build smoke passes in an isolated path without changing live fleet terminal state.
AG3: One flywheel worker-pane canary runs for at least 24h with pane capture, dispatch, recovery, and memory telemetry green.
AG4: Rollback to WezTerm is documented and tested before any client session is migrated.
AG5: alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, and skillos migrate only after flywheel canary is green and each session has a per-session receipt.

## Testing Obligations
- gh release/list or API evidence for the triggering release.
- ft/FrankenTerm smoke command evidence.
- ntm/flywheel pane-work-signal evidence before and after canary.
- Memory/RSS comparison against current WezTerm baseline.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: chore(frankenterm): adopt v0.1 fleet canary [frankenterm-adoption]
- AUTONOMY: autonomous
- No client session migrated before rollback proof.

title: [agentmail-identity-canonical] canonical identity registry surviving reboots — orchs+workers MUST resolve own identity from durable source
description:
[agentmail-identity-canonical] canonical identity registry surviving reboots — orchs+workers MUST resolve own identity from durable source ## Trauma class `agentmail-identity-sprawl` — Joshua flagged 2026-05-04T01:00Z: "I am constantly seeing orchestrators and workers using different agentmail identities and are losing tokens - we're getting agent sprawl in our agentmail system - this needs to be beaded out and planned how to let agents know across the system what their identity is in a way that survives reboots." ## Evidence (this session, multiple occurrences) - skillos transitioned FoggyBear → BrightLake 2026-05-03T23:31Z (reason: "FoggyBear registration token unavailable after compaction/reboot-like context loss; registered new identity and persisted local token env pointer"). New identity = new token = lost mailbox continuity. - 6 token files in ~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail-tokens/: foggybear.json, lavenderglen.json, rubycreek.json + .token sidecars + README. No machine-readable per-session identity registry. - Coord ledger has 50+ events tagged with various identity names (RubyCreek, FoggyBear, BrightLake, LavenderGlen, CoralRaven, HazyFox, GreenHill, picoz-pane0/1) — no canonical mapping session:pane → current_identity → token_path - DOCTOR signal `orchs_with_capture_gap_count=4` (alps, mobile-eats, picoz, skillos all show capture_gap because their identity isn't canonically resolvable) ## Meadows leverage point **#5 RULES** — sibling to L73 auth-marker/v1 ratification (yesterday), L70 ORCH-NO-PUNT, L71 VALIDATE-AND-REDISPATCH-DISCIPLINE, L75 ORCH-BLOCKER-COORDINATION. Paradigm "each pane has an identity" is correct; producing-loop missing — there's no canonical contract specifying: - WHERE identity is stored (canonical path convention) - HOW identity is resolved at session start (lookup mechanism) - WHAT survives reboot/compaction (durable persistence) - WHO owns the identity-token mapping (orch ratifies; flywheel:1 propagates) NOT #2 paradigm (paradigm is correct). NOT #6 info-flow alone (doctor signal `orchs_with_capture_gap_count` exists but doesn't help PRODUCE identity). #5 RULES — formalize the contract. ## Goal Canonical agentmail-identity-registry/v1 contract: 1. Per-session-pane identity registry: ~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail/sessions/:.json 2. Schema: session, pane, identity_name, token_path, registered_ts, last_used_ts, fleet_mail_project_key, predecessor_identity (if rotated), rotation_reason 3. Resolve mechanism: every orch+worker on session start runs canonical resolver: `flywheel-loop identity --session --pane

--json` 4. Resolver returns: existing identity (if registered) OR proposes new (if first-time) with explicit Joshua-disposes prompt 5. NO ad-hoc agent_mail register_agent calls — must go through resolver to prevent sprawl 6. Reboot survival: all state under ~/.local/state/ (durable across compaction); never in pane scrollback or env vars alone 7. Token vault canonical path: ~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail/tokens/.token (mode 600) 8. Deprecation: old foggybear.json + LavenderGlen.token files migrated to canonical path 9. Doctor signal: identity_registry_drift (sessions in topology with no registry entry) + identity_token_orphan (token files with no session pointing at them) 10. Auto-promote bead when identity_registry_drift > 0 ## Acceptance gates 1. Schema at `.flywheel/validation-schema/v1/agent-mail-identity-registry.schema.json` (extends auth-marker/v1 family) 2. Registry directory `~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail/sessions/` populated for all 5 known orchs (flywheel:1, mobile-eats:1, skillos:1, alps:1 if exists, picoz:1 if exists) 3. New CLI `flywheel-loop identity --session --pane

[--json] [--register --identity NAME]` 4. flywheel-loop doctor JSON gains `identity_registry` field with: total_registered, drift_count, orphan_token_count, recent_rotations 5. Migration script imports existing 3 tokens (RubyCreek, FoggyBear, LavenderGlen) into canonical layout 6. Tests in tests/agent-mail-identity-registry.sh: register, resolve, rotation, drift detection, orphan detection 7. Doctor signal `agentmail_identity_drift` auto-promotes bead when orchs_in_topology - identities_registered > 0 8. ft04 propagation: registry contract goes into all 17 flywheel repos via sync-canonical-doctrine 9. AGENTS.md L76 candidate AGENTMAIL-IDENTITY-CANONICAL ratifies the contract 10. Cross-orch directive template extended to include "identity_resolved=" field so peer orchs verify identity at handshake 11. Daily-report (flywheel-o7dq) Identities section: total registered, recent rotations, drift count 12. Skill `agent-mail` SKILL.md updated to point at canonical registry as ONLY identity source 13. canonical-paths.txt entries for registry dir, schema, resolver CLI ## Three-Q audit - VALIDATED: tests pass; doctor field active; live registry has all 5 orchs - DOCUMENTED: schema + resolver CLI --help + AGENTS.md L76 + skill updated - SURFACED: doctor signal + daily-report + auto-bead-promotion + cross-orch handshake field ## Skills cited - agent-mail (primary — extend, codify) - canonical-cli-scoping (resolver CLI design) - canonical-owner-runtime-state (durability pattern) - agent-fungibility-philosophy (identity continuity across worker swaps) - accretive-cron-orchestration (registration timing) - system-health (doctor signal pattern) - mcp-agent-mail server (sibling) ## Why P0 - Real cost: skillos lost FoggyBear mailbox continuity + token (already documented this session) - Multiple identities accumulating in ~/.local/state/ — sprawl Joshua explicitly flagged - No durability guarantee = every reboot/compaction = potential identity loss - 4 of 5 orchs already in capture_gap (orchs_with_capture_gap_count doctor warning) - Sibling fleet rules (L70-L75) all already ratified; this is the missing #5 RULES leverage point in the same family - Fleet-wide via ft04 propagation pattern (already proven 4× this session) ## Doctrine refs - bead flywheel-ft04 CLOSED (root-AGENTS sync mechanism — propagation pipe, sibling) - bead flywheel-2zsj CLOSED (storage-discipline — sibling fleet rule, #5 RULES) - bead flywheel-vc3e (orch-blocker-coordination L75 — sibling) - bead flywheel-3ck3 (headless-chrome-leak-doctor — sibling resource leak) - bead flywheel-xap2 (orch-capture-parity gap — companion; this bead provides the canonical capture key per session) - bead skillos-1kc (auth-marker/v1 — sibling pattern, also #5 RULES leverage) - ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_meadows_rules_unblock_paradigm_intact.md - /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail-tokens/ (existing pre-canonical token files; migration source) - ~/.local/state/flywheel/cross-orch-coordination.jsonl (event log of identity transitions) ## Current sprawl evidence (probe at flag time) - 6 files in ~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail-tokens/: foggybear.json, lavenderglen.json, rubycreek.json + .token sidecars + README - 1 file in ~/.local/state/flywheel/agent-mail/skillos-current.env (post-rotation) - 0 files in canonical session-pane registry layout (this bead's deliverable) - Coord ledger: at least 8 distinct identity names referenced (RubyCreek, FoggyBear, BrightLake, LavenderGlen, CoralRaven, HazyFox, GreenHill, picoz-pane0/1) — no canonical mapping

title: [skill-uplift] canonical-cli-scoping 681→745 via agent-ergonomics audit recommendations
description:
## Source

Agent-ergonomics audit fork 2026-05-08 ran 11-dim methodology against canonical-cli-scoping. Result: 681/1000 weighted avg.

**Joshua directive 2026-05-08T22:14Z: target 1000 if anywhere possible.**

Path requires applying ALL 10 recommendations + iterative re-audit + addressing each weak-dimension structurally.

## Current scorecard

- Strongest: output_parseability 810, self_documentation 770, safety_with_recovery 750
- Weakest: intent_inference 510, regression_resistance 545
- Worst surfaces: S016/S017/S018/S019 (own scripts, 495-645) — meta-paradox

## Path to 1000 — 4-phase plan

### Phase 1 — Quick wins (681 → 745, low blast)
- R-001: Add --json + capabilities + exit-code dictionary to check-cli-scoping.sh
- R-003: Concrete "state-handling" examples for triad trigger S002 (3-5 worked examples + corpus)
- AG: re-audit, confirm ≥ 745

### Phase 2 — Subagent validator (745 → 820, Joshua-review HIGH blast)
- R-002: doctor/health/repair contract validator subagent — reads source + binary + emits per-rule scorecard
- R-005, R-006, R-008 from recommendations.jsonl: catches partial-implementation gaps that static probes miss
- AG: re-audit, confirm ≥ 820

### Phase 3 — Regression resistance ladder (820 → 920)
- Snapshot/golden tests for every contract rule (15 rules × test pair = 30 tests)
- Wrong-but-legible invocation corpus (deliberately malformed dispatches that should still be parseable + actionable)
- Mutation testing: introduce single-char rule changes, confirm validator catches each
- AG: re-audit, confirm ≥ 920

### Phase 4 — Asymptote (920 → 1000)
- Intent_inference structural fix: fuzzy matching, partial-implementation tolerance with graceful degradation
- Self-application: every rule the skill enforces, the skill itself implements as exemplar
- Cross-skill consistency: align with agent-ergonomics + jeff-issue-chain + dispatch-template idioms
- Fresh-agent-from-scratch test: subagent with zero context successfully invokes canonical-cli-scoping correctly first try, scoring all 11 dims at 90+
- AG: re-audit, confirm 1000 (or 990+ — last 10 may be irreducible — accept floor at 990)

## Acceptance gates

AG1-Phase1: 745+
AG2-Phase2: 820+ (Joshua review subagent infra change)
AG3-Phase3: 920+
AG4-Phase4: 1000 (or 990+ if irreducible asymptote)
AG5: Re-run audit on top-10 fleet skills using same 4-phase template once canonical-cli-scoping hits 990+
AG6: Doctrine page `.flywheel/doctrine/skill-self-application-1000-pattern.md` — the 4-phase ladder as reusable methodology

## Cross-references

- Audit artifacts: /tmp/canonical-cli-scoping-agent-ergonomics-audit/
- flywheel-rzgqc: agent-ergonomics name-length codex bug
- Memory: `feedback_canonical_cli_at_dispatch`
- Skill: agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools
close_reason: Phase 1 quick wins complete: R-001 checker JSON/capabilities/exit-code dictionary and R-003 state-handling examples/corpus landed; remaining 990+ ladder split to flywheel-gb54d.1 and flywheel-gb54d.2.

## Out Of Scope

title: [skillos-proposal] codex-stuck-detector bg-terminal class + caam-rotate-and-respawn primitive
description:
## Source

skillos:1 / BrightLake cross-orch coordination 2026-05-08T18:35Z. Severity: high — silent 30+ min freeze observed at skillos:2 today (post-completion, child process hung).

## Two gaps in current detector

**Gap 1: detector doesn't catch background-terminal hangs**
Current `codex-template-stuck-detector.sh` v2 classifies: model_at_capacity_halt, post_completion, input_deaf/buffer_stuck. Missing: pattern `Waiting for background terminal (Xm Ys • esc to interrupt)`. The timer increments so hash-stability check returns alive.

**Gap 2: caam-rotate-and-respawn primitive doesn't exist**
`test_caam_auto_rotate_on_usage_limit.sh` exists but detector's `recover()` doesn't invoke `caam activate <next-profile>`.

## Proposed acceptance gates

AG1: Add `background_terminal_stuck` subclass to classify() with `Waiting for background terminal \(([0-9]+)m` pattern + 5min threshold; strip timer text from t0/t1 before sha comparison

AG2: New script `.flywheel/scripts/caam-rotate-and-respawn.sh` orchestrating 6-step rotation:
  1. Read current active CAAM profile (`caam profile current`)
  2. Pick next from rotation roster
  3. `caam activate <next>`
  4. ntm-send Ctrl-C, wait clean prompt
  5. `/flywheel:respawn` on new profile
  6. Re-dispatch last in-flight bead from dispatch-log.jsonl

AG3: Wire `caam-rotate-and-respawn` into detector's `recover()` for both `model_at_capacity_halt` and new `background_terminal_stuck` subclasses

AG4: Doctor extension — add 5 metrics: codex_freeze_recovery_attempted_24h, codex_freeze_recovery_succeeded_24h, codex_freeze_recovery_success_pct_24h, caam_rotation_count_24h, caam_active_profile, caam_profiles_available; WARN if success_pct < 0.5; FAIL if profiles_available < 1

AG5: Tests — fixtures for new subclass + e2e test for rotate-and-respawn

## Effort estimate

- bg-terminal class: ~1 hr
- caam-rotate-and-respawn primitive: ~2-3 hrs
- recover() wire-in: ~30 min
- doctor metrics: ~30 min
- tests: ~1.5 hrs

Total ~5-6 hrs flywheel-side work.

## Real-world impact

skillos:2 freeze observed 2026-05-08T18:30Z: post-completion (PR #69 merged at 0af88c35), spawned bg terminal task, hung 30+ min undetected, Joshua manually respawned. Every active codex worker pane is at risk.

## Cross-references

- `.flywheel/scripts/codex-template-stuck-detector.sh` v2 (extend)
- `.flywheel/tests/test_caam_auto_rotate_on_usage_limit.sh` (already exists)
- `.flywheel/scripts/worker-auto-respawn-watchdog.sh` (sibling)
- `~/.claude/skills/caam/SKILL.md`
- `/Users/josh/.local/bin/caam` (rotation CLI)
- AGENTS.md L91, L115 (siblings)
- INCIDENTS fire-and-forget-dispatch 2026-05-08 (same trauma family)
close_reason: Implemented background-terminal detector class, CAAM rotate-and-respawn primitive, recovery wire-in, doctor metrics, and focused tests. Evidence: .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-ggld7/evidence.md. Commit: c4a284c

title: [git-stash-janitor] fix empty untracked-tree and macOS polish verifier gaps
description:
Observed during flywheel-x5f0e ALPS stash-janitor run. The git-stash-janitor verifier and polish-bar check treat empty third-parent stash trees as missing untracked payloads on macOS. Official verify-bundle.sh reported 50 UNTRACKED failures, while supplemental reconciliation proved every failure was an empty third-parent tree with an empty materialized directory. polish-bar-check.sh also reported diffs=0 because its find -regextype expression is not BSD-compatible.

Evidence:
- /Users/josh/Developer/alpsinsurance/.stash_janitor_workspace/bundle_verification.log
- /Users/josh/Developer/alpsinsurance/.stash_janitor_workspace/empty-untracked-tree-reconciliation.tsv
- /Users/josh/Developer/alpsinsurance-stash-archive-2026-05-08/

## Acceptance gates
AG1: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/git-stash-janitor/scripts/verify-bundle.sh treats an empty third-parent tree plus an empty materialized directory as byte-equal, not missing.
AG2: /Users/josh/.claude/skills/git-stash-janitor/scripts/polish-bar-check.sh counts canonical diff files on macOS/BSD without GNU find -regextype.
AG3: A fixture with an empty third-parent stash tree passes verify-bundle.sh and polish-bar-check.sh on macOS.
close_reason: Fixed git-stash-janitor empty third-parent untracked verification and macOS diff counting; verified with fixture and ALPS run

title: [coord-wire-in T3B] canonical dispatch-packet materializer (one stock, two flows)
description:
Mission: continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet. Donella #4 (self-organization) + #3 (goal) fix: ONE canonical 'dispatch packet' stock that BOTH /flywheel:dispatch (operator-fired) AND ntm-coordinator-pinned daemon (auto-fired) materialize from the SAME primitive — eliminates the current 2-stock divergence (canonical 636-line _shared/dispatch-template.md vs minimal 16-line ~/.config/ntm/templates/flywheel-worker-tick.txt).

WHY THIS SUPERSEDES T3A (flywheel-of8k): T3A was 'persist template into repo' — but the template itself is a parameter (Meadows #12). The real fix is a materializer primitive so the coordinator's minimal template is a vehicle, not a substitute, for the canonical contract.

DELIVERABLE: .flywheel/scripts/build-dispatch-packet.sh
- Canonical-cli-scoping: --dry-run|--apply|--json|--explain|--info|--examples|--schema|--help
- INPUT: --bead-id <id> --target-pane <N> --target-session <session> [--task-id <id>] [--dispatch-channel auto|operator]
- OUTPUT: writes /tmp/dispatch_<task-id>.md (canonical packet) + emits JSON with {packet_path, packet_sha256, validation_status, fields_resolved[]}
- LOGIC: 
  1. Read _shared/dispatch-template.md (contract source-of-truth)
  2. Resolve dynamic fields:
     - mission_anchor + mission_fitness_claim/class (from .flywheel/MISSION.md)
     - josh_request_id (lookup from josh-requests.jsonl by bead linkage; null if none)
     - identity_name + worker_identity_status (from orch-worker-identity manifest)
     - callback_pane (from session-topology.jsonl latest-wins)
     - skill auto-routes (from bead title/body keywords matched against ~/.claude/skills/)
     - memory hits (via _shared/inject-memory-hits.sh)
     - BEAD body (via br show --json + br dep tree)
     - file discipline (PICOZ_WORKER_FILES from bead scope)
     - acceptance criteria (from bead body)
     - verification command (from bead acceptance section)
  3. Write packet, emit JSON

INTEGRATION:
- /flywheel:dispatch is REFACTORED to call build-dispatch-packet.sh (replaces inline synthesis logic)
- ntm coordinator template degrades to: 'Read /tmp/dispatch_{BEAD_ID}.md — that is your canonical contract.'
- Daemon hook: pre-dispatch trigger fires build-dispatch-packet.sh for next assignment; daemon's minimal prompt becomes a pointer

ACCEPTANCE:
- script ships with full canonical-cli-scoping
- packet output passes _shared/dispatch-template.md schema (every contract block present + filled)
- bash -n + shellcheck clean
- e2e test: /flywheel:dispatch and coordinator-daemon-fired dispatch produce IDENTICAL packets for same bead
- existing flywheel-olhg-style callbacks continue to validate
- mission_fitness_claim, josh_request_id, identity_name, callback_pane, skill_auto_routes, memory_hits, bead_body, file_discipline, acceptance, verification — ALL present in materialized packet

DEPENDS: existing _shared/dispatch-template.md + br show + topology + orch-worker-identity manifest + josh-requests.jsonl

CLOSES: T3A (flywheel-of8k) — supersedes the 'persist template' framing with the correct architecture

title: [validate-tick-phase] insert VALIDATE phase between DISPATCH and INTEGRATE
description:
### Title

`[validate-tick-phase] insert VALIDATE phase between DISPATCH and INTEGRATE`

### Goal

Add a first-class VALIDATE phase to the tick state machine. The tick must validate all in-flight worker callbacks before integration, summary, or new dispatch work can treat their claims as true.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation`, `worker_capacity_gate_false_block`, and `meat-puppet-orchestrator-decision-on-partial-state`. The current tick can observe callbacks and still proceed on partial state; Lane C identified VALIDATE as the phase that prevents this.

### Acceptance gates

1. Tick phase order includes VALIDATE after DISPATCH callback reap and before INTEGRATE. Proof receipt: `B05_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Pending callback with no receipt routes to VALIDATE and increments/consumes `callbacks_unvalidated_count`. Proof receipt: `B05_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Failed validation prevents INTEGRATE until fix bead, reopen, or no-bead reason exists. Proof receipt: `B05_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Unknown/runtime-unresponsive validation creates a bounded blocker state, not raw-shell fallback. Proof receipt: `B05_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Clean validation proceeds to INTEGRATE and preserves receipt ref in tick receipt. Proof receipt: `B05_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Tick dry-run shows planned VALIDATE actions without mutating state. Proof receipt: `B05_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Tick receipt includes validation summary fields for doctor and `/flywheel:learn`. Proof receipt: `B05_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `tick_cmd=<cmd> receipt=<path> fixtures=<cmd>:PASS validate_phase_seen=yes commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[validate-tick-phase]`.

### Doctrine references

L50, L52, L53, L60, L67, L69, L70; `flywheel-1z65`; `feedback_data_guides_decisions_not_human_judgment.md`.

### Out of scope

No new doctor signals beyond consuming existing ones; no dispatch block rendering; no parity binary.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B03, B04, B06, B07.  
Blocks: B08, B09, B10, B12.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings if validation should run in background instead of blocking INTEGRATE.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B05_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B05_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B05_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B05_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B05_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B05_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B05_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [skillos-orchestrator-apply] choose and apply skillos HEALTHY loop architecture
description:
## Goal
Joshua chooses an architecture for skillos orchestration, then an apply worker implements the selected path to move skillos from LIMPING to HEALTHY under L60.

## Context
Parent bead `flywheel-668a` found that skillos has a working JSM payload but no sufficient orchestrator/reaper/fuckup-decision receiver. The rolled-back JSM apply proved the payload could write ledger, pane activity, and receipts, but loop-integrity still failed callback and fuckup-decision signals.

Design artifacts are in `/tmp/skillos-orchestrator-DESIGN/`:
- `option-a-cc-orchestrator-pane.md`
- `option-b-orchestrator-helper-script.md`
- `option-c-cross-session-l61.md`
- `README.md`

## Options

- Selected option explicitly states how all 5 L60 signals fire.

title: [jeff-doctor-repair-contract] require doctor/health/repair triad for new operational substrates
description:
## Why

Jeff repos repeatedly expose `doctor`, `health/status`, and `repair --dry-run` as a triad. Flywheel has many doctor signals, but new operational substrates can still land as one-off scripts without a repair posture.

## Scope

Codify the doctor/health/repair triad as a flywheel substrate contract:

- Add a reusable checklist for new operational scripts and CLIs.
- Require `doctor --json`, non-mutating health/status, and repair preview for mutating repair surfaces.
- Add dry-run/apply separation and backup-before-write expectations where repair mutates state.
- Wire the checklist into canonical CLI scoping or a sibling flywheel substrate skill.

## Source Evidence

- `mcp_agent_mail/README.md:726` documents `am doctor`.
- `mcp_agent_mail/README.md:793` documents `doctor repair --dry-run`.
- `flywheel_connectors/AGENTS.md:17` lists `am doctor fix`, `am doctor repair`, and `am doctor reconstruct`.
- `coding_agent_session_search/README.md:90` makes health/status JSON the readiness truth.
- `/tmp/flywheel-avlj-evidence.md` P01.

## Acceptance Gates

1. A documented checklist exists for operational substrate triad compliance.
2. At least one existing flywheel script is evaluated against the checklist in a fixture or receipt.
3. Missing repair/dry-run posture produces a machine-readable warning.
4. Mutating repair examples include backup-before-write or explicit no-backup rationale.
5. Tests validate PASS/WARN/FAIL examples.

## DOD

Close reason shape: `Codified doctor/health/repair contract; tests=<cmd>; examples=<path>`.

## Doctrine

L60, L71, L82, `system-health`, `canonical-cli-scoping`.

title: wide-skill-enhancement-scan-jeff-patterns
description:
# /flywheel:worker-tick — wide skill enhancement scan vs Jeff's mined patterns

## Goal
Cross-reference EVERY skill in the library (~284 skills under ~/.claude/skills/) against the 8 doctrine clusters + 8 code patterns mined from Jeff's 177-repo corpus (.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/). Produce a per-skill enhancement matrix flagging where Jeff's methods could ADOPT into / EXTEND / replace patterns inside our skill body text.

## Scope
- Scan ALL skills, not just flywheel-owned. Includes: agent-mail, gsd, postgres-best-practices, generic dev skills (python-best-practices, rust-best-practices, testing-conformance-harnesses, etc.), domain skills (medical-coding, payroll-processing, etc.).
- For each skill: read SKILL.md (or first 200 lines if longer)
- Cross-reference against /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/01-doctrine-cluster.md and 02-code-patterns.md

## Pattern reference (8 clusters + 8 code patterns)

**Doctrine clusters** (from w3pr Phase 1):
1. testing-patterns (executable verification)
2. doctor-health-repair-triad
3. idempotency-and-dry-run
4. ipc-and-transport-contracts
5. error-handling-and-recovery
6. schema-versioning-and-migrations
7. callback-and-receipt-envelope
8. append-only-audit-and-lineage

**Code patterns** (from w3pr Phase 2, with verdicts):
- ADOPT: idempotency-key-fail-closed (43 repos), testing-fixture-conventions (110), lock-file-convention (123), frontmatter-validation (160)
- EXTEND: doctor-health-repair-triad (121), schema-version-migration (134), append-only-audit-log (140)
- DIVERGE: callback-envelope-shape (151)

## Acceptance gates (canonical AG numbering)

AG1: scan touches ALL ~284 skills (count from `ls ~/.claude/skills/ | wc -l` minus archives + directory entries); no sampling
AG2: per-skill enhancement matrix written to `.flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/06-skill-enhancement-matrix.md` with columns: skill_name | applicable_clusters | applicable_patterns | verdict (ENHANCE/SKIP/REPLACE/NEW-COMPANION) | concrete_diff_summary
AG3: top-20 highest-leverage enhancements identified with rationale (skills where ≥3 patterns apply AND skill is high-traffic)
AG4: separate "needs new sibling skill" list — patterns that have NO existing skill home (e.g., if "frontmatter-validation" maps to no existing skill, flag for skillos handoff)
AG5: NO direct skill mutations — this is a SCAN bead. Output is the matrix; per-skill enhancement work is filed as follow-up beads downstream
AG6: For top-20 enhancement candidates, file individual P1 beads with format `[skill-enhance-<skill-name>] adopt Jeff <pattern> into <skill>`
AG7: For each "new sibling skill" candidate, file ONE skillos-handoff bead per candidate with format `[skillos-new-skill-request] <skill-name>: justification + sample SKILL.md outline`
AG8: receipt at /tmp/flywheel-skill-enhance-scan-evidence.md cites the matrix path + counts (skills_scanned, skills_with_enhancement_op, skills_skip, top20_files, new_sibling_count)
AG9: cross-reference w3pr.4 (Phase 4 ADOPT/EXTEND/AVOID synthesis already filed) — coordinate, don't duplicate scope

## Skills to consult
- skill-builder (skill authoring)
- skill-autoresearch (cross-skill scan patterns)
- codebase-audit (matrix-style audit deliverable)
- jeff-convergence-audit (Phase 1 broad-sweep discipline)

## Method
1. `ls ~/.claude/skills/ | grep -vE '^\.|\.bak\.' > /tmp/skill-roster.txt` — get authoritative skill list
2. Read .flywheel/jeff-corpus/v1/learnings/01-doctrine-cluster.md + 02-code-patterns.md once into worker context
3. Iterate roster: for each skill, read first 200 lines of SKILL.md, classify against clusters + patterns
4. Append row to matrix
5. Sort by leverage score = (cluster_match_count + pattern_match_count) × usage_proxy
6. Top 20 → file P1 enhancement beads via br create
7. Patterns with no skill home → file skillos-handoff beads

## Per the new contract: callback MUST report did/didnt/gaps
- DID: each AG with citation
- DIDNT: AGs you can't meet (file gap-bead before callback)
- GAPS: newly-discovered patterns NOT in w3pr's 8 (file gap-beads as `w3pr-skill-scan.<N>`)

## Callback envelope
/Users/josh/.local/bin/ntm send flywheel --pane=1 "DONE flywheel-jeffenhance task_id=<id> did=<n>/9 didnt=<bead-ids-or-none> gaps=<bead-ids-or-none> evidence=/tmp/flywheel-skill-enhance-scan-evidence.md tests=PASS skills_scanned=<N> top20_beads_filed=<N> skillos_handoff_beads=<N> callback_delivery_verified=true"

## Constraints
- DO NOT mutate any skill SKILL.md — output is matrix + bead filings
- DO NOT push anything upstream
- For skillos-handoff beads, do NOT xpane to skillos directly — file the bead in flywheel; orch handles xpane after review
- Time-budget: this is wide scan, expect ~15-20 worker-min. Skip non-applicable skills fast (e.g., domain billing skills probably don't have testing-fixture-conventions to enhance)

## Memory
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_meadows_jeff_mentors.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Developer-flywheel/memory/feedback_accretive_corpus_ingestion.md

title: [L-rule] PRE-COMMIT-GITLEAKS-MANDATORY + rank-3 secret-handle convention (Phase 1)
description:
Cross-orch from skillos:1 (BrightLake), Joshua green-lit. Mobile-eats had TWO leaks today:
- 1st: Nango credential commit 63c9d58 (caught by CI gitleaks 2 days later)
- 2nd: DATABASE_URL via `head -c 200 /tmp/safe-sink.json` readback (~6h after 1st, vector-6 readback class)

Joshua directive: "I will rotate all secrets later — once we can prove our systems can stop leaking our damn secrets." No rotation until structural fix lands.

## Phase 1 scope (this bead, EXPANDED with vector-6)

L-rule canonical authoring covers MULTIPLE vectors of the same rank-3 root (agent never holds raw secret values):

title: [auto-reopen-bead] detect and reopen falsely closed beads with missing shipped artifacts
description:
### Title

`[auto-reopen-bead] detect and reopen falsely closed beads with missing shipped artifacts`

### Goal

Scan closed beads whose close reason claims shipped artifacts or command surfaces and reopen or candidate them when mechanical probes fail. This bead specifically targets the "closed bead != shipped substrate" failure class.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation`, `documented-bug-not-actioned-self-recursion`, and `closed-bead-artifact-missing`. The `flywheel-1z65` evidence cited josh-request beads closed as shipped while canonical artifacts were missing.

### Acceptance gates

1. Scanner reads closed bead close reasons and extracts typed artifact refs from known patterns. Proof receipt: `B07_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Missing file, non-executable file, failed smoke command, or invalid schema produces `reopen_candidate`. Proof receipt: `B07_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Valid artifact with passing smoke remains closed and records no action. Proof receipt: `B07_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Ambiguous prose-only close reason produces `unknown` with no automatic reopen. Proof receipt: `B07_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Reopen mutation, if enabled, uses repo-local `br reopen` plus comment with validation receipt and audit reference. Proof receipt: `B07_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Dry-run mode is default and emits JSON candidate list. Proof receipt: `B07_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Tests cover missing file, command smoke fail, ambiguous close reason, and valid artifact. Proof receipt: `B07_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `scanner_cmd=<cmd> candidates_fixture=<cmd>:PASS dry_run_json=<path> reopen_mode=<candidate|apply> commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[auto-reopen-bead]`.

### Doctrine references

L52, L56, L60, L61, L70; `flywheel-1z65`; `feedback_substrate_watchtower_must_be_wired.md`.

### Out of scope

No broad historical backport outside the current plan policy; no reopening beads based only on human suspicion; no global Beads vault mutation.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B03.  
Blocks: B04, B05, B09, B12.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings if Joshua chooses candidate-only rather than automatic reopen.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B07_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B07_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B07_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B07_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B07_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B07_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B07_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [jeff-pattern-import-contract] enforce file-line evidence before doctrine/skill adoption
description:
## Why

Jeff pattern mining is now canonical doctrine (L64), but the enforcement is still mostly social. Agents can cite "Jeff inspired" without repo/file evidence, which violates the corpus value proposition.

## Scope

Implement a Jeff-pattern import contract for doctrine, skills, and plan artifacts:

- Require `Source: Jeff <repo>:<file>:<line> + ZestStream adaptation` for every Jeff-originated doctrine or skill claim.
- Add a lightweight validator over AGENTS.md, skill drafts, and plan outputs that mention Jeff/Dicklesworthstone.
- Add fixtures with one valid citation, one missing file:line citation, and one vague "inspired by Jeff" citation.
- Add a doctor signal or learning-surface count for uncited Jeff claims.

## Source Evidence

- Flywheel L64 requires file-line evidence for Jeff-originated patterns.
- `~/.claude/skills/dicklesworthstone-stack/SKILL.md` requires every Jeff signal to transit `seen -> noted -> strike-evidence|archived|extracted`.
- `/tmp/flywheel-avlj-evidence.md` P01-P12 gives examples of compliant file-line citations.

## Acceptance Gates

1. A validator command exists and returns non-zero for missing Jeff file-line citations.
2. Fixtures cover valid, vague, and missing-citation cases.
3. `flywheel-loop doctor --json` or an equivalent probe exposes `jeff_pattern_uncited_count`.
4. README/AGENTS docs describe the citation shape.
5. Tests pass and include the validator fixtures.

## DOD

Close reason shape: `Implemented Jeff pattern import contract; tests=<cmd>; doctor_field=<field-or-none>`.

## Doctrine

L64, L56, L71, `dicklesworthstone-stack`, `beads-br`.

title: [jeff-philosophy-study] daily 'what is Jeff up to / what can we learn' snapshot + deep socraticode mining + pattern internalization
description:
Joshua directive 2026-05-03 ~10:10Z: 'as we get this jeff repo process locked down I want to do a deep socraticode search through all of his work. I also want to have a daily snapshot - what is jeff up to - what can we learn from him today type of thing - I want to embody his philosophies and methods of working into our ecosystem'

PARADIGM (Meadows #2): not just consume Jeff's substrate (L63 jeff-intel-network), but STUDY HIS METHOD. Internalize his patterns. Become his student, not his consumer.

DEPENDENCIES (must land first):
- flywheel-1lpv jeff-intel Phase 1 IN FLIGHT (clones + indexes all repos)
- flywheel-vnsw cli-canonicalization IN FLIGHT (provides /flywheel:jeff-* command surface)

THIS BEAD adds 3 NEW LAYERS on top of L63 substrate:

LAYER 1: Deep socraticode mining (one-time + monthly refresh)
  - Cross-repo pattern extraction across all 9+ Jeff repos
  - Topics: state machine design, error handling, callback contracts, schema evolution, doctrine surfaces, README/AGENTS shape, test pyramid, telemetry/observability, dispatch patterns, idempotency
  - Output: ~/.local/state/jeff-philosophy/patterns.jsonl with {pattern_class, repos_using_it, file:line citations, our_adoption_status}
  - Deliverable: /tmp/jeff-philosophy-deep-mine_findings.md (~500 lines, comprehensive)
  - Cadence: full mine on first run, incremental monthly

LAYER 2: Daily 'What is Jeff up to / what can we learn' snapshot
  - Cron daily 06:00 (matches morning-review window)
  - Pulls (uses L63 jeff-intel-network outputs):
    - Last 24h commits across all Jeff repos (titles + 1-line diff summary per commit)
    - Last 24h X posts (if Jeff X handle discovered)
    - Last 24h website diffs (if blog active)
    - Last 24h GH issues opened/closed (incl ones not authored by us)
    - Last 24h release tags
  - For EACH artifact: 'what can we learn?' analysis:
    - Pattern class identification
    - Cross-link to existing flywheel doctrine
    - 'Could we adopt this?' verdict (YES_ADOPT | YES_ADAPT | NO_NOT_OUR_DOMAIN | NEED_RESEARCH)
  - Output: ~/.local/state/jeff-philosophy/daily-snapshots/<date>.md
  - Surfaces in /flywheel:status morning section

LAYER 3: Philosophy internalization → flywheel doctrine
  - When LAYER 1 mining finds a pattern Jeff uses everywhere that we use inconsistently:
    - File P3 bead 'adopt-jeff-pattern-<name>'
    - Cross-reference Jeff's exemplars by file:line
    - Once adopted: cite in AGENTS.md as L-rule with 'Source: Jeff <repo>:<file>:<line> + ZestStream adaptation'
  - Examples likely to surface (hypothesis):
    - Jeff's '7-axis rubric' shape → already adopted as our jeff-issue process
    - Jeff's 'doctor surface' shape → already inspired flywheel-loop doctor
    - Jeff's 'fuckup-log' philosophy → already adopted (Jeff originated this concept per memory)
    - Jeff's L-rule numbering convention → already inherited
  - Likely NEW patterns to discover via deep mine:
    - Schema migration shape across his 9 repos (frankensqlite, beads_rust, vibe_cockpit, cass each have one)
    - Worker callback shape across ntm + agent-mail + dcg
    - Doctrine surface shape across all his READMEs
    - Test pyramid shape (his 57+ tests vs our test surface)

CANONICAL CLI:
  ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-philosophy-mine.sh (canonical-cli-scoping)
    --deep-mine        # full one-time pattern extraction across all repos
    --daily-snapshot   # cron daily, 24h delta + learning analysis
    --pattern <name>   # focused mine for specific pattern class
    --doctor           # health probe (snapshots fresh? mining current?)
    --json             # machine-readable
  /flywheel:jeff-philosophy slash command wrapper

INTEGRATION:
  - Cron: daily 06:00 for snapshot
  - Cron: monthly 1st-of-month 04:00 for deep-mine refresh
  - tick.md Step 4w wires daily-snapshot into tick receipt
  - /flywheel:status morning section surfaces 'today's Jeff insight'
  - /flywheel:learn --review includes Jeff-pattern-adoption opportunities
  - Long-term: build ZestStream's own L-rule pack derived from Jeff's pattern catalog

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
[ ] L63 substrate (flywheel-1lpv) DONE — all Jeff repos cloned + socraticode-indexed
[ ] Deep-mine helper script shipped + canonical-cli-scoping pass
[ ] First deep-mine produces ≥10 pattern classes with ≥3-repo evidence each
[ ] Daily-snapshot cron active
[ ] /flywheel:jeff-philosophy slash command shipped
[ ] tick.md Step 4w wired
[ ] First daily snapshot proves end-to-end (delta detected, 'what can we learn' analysis present)
[ ] First pattern internalization → AGENTS.md L-rule with Jeff exemplar cited
[ ] AGENTS.md L64 added per L61 (this directive landed = doctrine)
[ ] README.md Current State updated per L61

DOCTRINE LINKS:
- L63 (jeff-intel-network — provides substrate for this study layer)
- L62 (latent-signal-substrate paradigm — same shape applied to Jeff's work)
- L61 (ecosystem wire-in — this rule itself wires)
- feedback_jeff_substrate_version_drift (META-RULE source)
- feedback_meadows_jeff_mentors (CRITICAL — memory cites Jeff as one of 2 explicit mentors for the flywheel)

WHEN TO ACTIVATE: after flywheel-1lpv (jeff-intel-network Phase 1) callback DONE.

title: [orchestrator-tick-phase-a] implement Phase A orchestrator tick checks
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/flywheel-loop-tick-contracts-validation-architecture-2026-04-30.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-sgdp

## Goal

Implement Phase A `/flywheel:tick` orchestrator checks and receipt writing in SOFT mode.

## Scope

- Add/update `/Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/tick.md` and `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop tick`.
- Implement Phase A checks: autoloop receipts read, NTM dispatch discipline, learn-review cadence when >=3 unprocessed fuckup events.
- Read callbacks and write tick receipt at the repo-local or session state path.
- Dispatch one ready bead or hold idle with a machine-readable reason.
- Log violations to fuckup-log with failure classes from plan lines 130-139.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `bash tests/validate-tick-phase.sh` passes.
2. Fixture with unread autoloop receipts emits `orch_skipped_substrate_read` SOFT violation and writes a tick receipt.
3. Fixture with >=3 unprocessed fuckup events and no learn review emits `orch_skipped_learn_review`.
4. Fixture with raw transport evidence emits `orch_used_raw_tmux` or current positive-only NTM violation class without breaking L29 language.
5. Tick receipt includes phase, checks_run, violations, mode, dispatch_status, and hold_reason when no work dispatches.
6. Tick stays within bounded runtime in fixture or reports `tick_budget_exceeded` with handoff receipt.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: tick fixtures cover violation and no-work paths.
- DOCUMENTED: tick command names Phase A check list and receipt path.
- SURFACED: receipts and fuckup-log rows expose every violation for L56 promotion.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L29, L48, L50, L56, L70, L71.
- Plan sections 3, 10 Phase A, and 13.

## Out Of Scope

- No worker-tick checks.
- No deep-audit twice-daily flow.
- No launchd cron implementation.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented Phase A orchestrator tick checks; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[orchestrator-tick-phase-a]`.
close_reason: DONE pane 2 (post-respawn): Phase A orch-tick. did=6/6, tests 25/25 pass. Evidence: flywheel-loop-tick + tick-receipt.schema.json + validate-tick-phase.sh + tick.md. socraticode K=3, beads-workflow consulted.

title: [convergence-fleet-lock-repair-tool] implement lock hash repair primitive
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/convergence-bead-plan-2026-05-01.md`

## Scope

Create the fleet lock repair primitive as either `flywheel-loop lock-repair --repo <path> --json` or standalone `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-lock-repair`.

## Required Behavior

- Read `.flywheel/MISSION.md`, `.flywheel/GOAL.md`, and `.flywheel/STATE.md`.

- Add or update `lock_hash` in frontmatter.
- Append per-file rows to `.flywheel/lock-log.jsonl`.

4. Backups exist for each changed file.
5. Running `flywheel-loop doctor --strict --repo <fixture> --json` after repair returns `status=="ok"` or explicit non-lock blocker.

## DOD

`Implemented fleet lock repair; tests=<cmd>; command=<path>; lock_log=<path>`.

No implementation dependency. Related to `flywheel-64kr`; do not duplicate any canonical CLI checklist work already covered there.

## Out Of Scope

Bulk fleet application; that is owned by the fleet-state-docs repair bead.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: dry-run/apply/doctor fixtures.
- DOCUMENTED: command help describes backup, dry-run, and body-hash semantics.
- SURFACED: lock-log rows create durable repair evidence.

title: [learn-validation-routing] route validation events through /flywheel:learn without double-processing
description:
### Title

`[learn-validation-routing] route validation events through /flywheel:learn without double-processing`

### Goal

Integrate validation failures and positive validation receipts with `/flywheel:learn` so every finding is surfaced exactly once, promoted through L56 when appropriate, and never misrouted into the fuckup-log when it is a positive event.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `positive-event-misrouted-to-fuckup-log`, `trauma-amnesia`, `doctrine-orphaning`, and `orchestrator-skipped-callback-validation`. Lane A found learn loop surfacing is partial, and 00-INTENT requires every worker callback ingested to produce validation receipts before LEARN advances.

### Acceptance gates

1. Validation receipt includes `learn_route=ignore|review|promote|skill_extend`. Proof receipt: `B09_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Failed validation with trauma class creates exactly one learn/fuckup event or links to an existing one. Proof receipt: `B09_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Positive validation receipt is routed to a positive outcome substrate or ignored with explicit reason; it is not written as a fuckup. Proof receipt: `B09_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Recurring failure class follows L56: fuckup-log -> INCIDENTS -> L-rule, not direct L-rule. Proof receipt: `B09_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Duplicate scan of same receipt does not create duplicate learn events. Proof receipt: `B09_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. `/flywheel:learn --review` or equivalent can list unrouted validation events. Proof receipt: `B09_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. Tests cover failed validation, duplicate failed validation, positive validation, and skill-extension routing. Proof receipt: `B09_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `learn_cmd=<cmd> routing_ledger=<path> duplicate_test=<cmd>:PASS positive_route=<path|none_reason> commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[learn-validation-routing]`.

### Doctrine references

L53, L56, L61, L62, L70; `/flywheel:learn`; `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md`.

### Out of scope

No authoring of new skills, no direct INCIDENTS.md or AGENTS.md promotion unless the learn command already supports it.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B04, B06, B07.  
Blocks: B10, B12.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings on positive-event substrate location.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B09_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B09_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B09_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B09_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B09_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B09_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B09_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: [jeff-upstream] mcp-agent-mail create_agent_identity returns registration_token unconditionally — caller cannot satisfy 'no-token-echo' contract
description:
Filed 2026-05-03 ~08:25Z by orchestrator-cc following fuckup-cluster flywheel-dy0h gap #1.

UPSTREAM REPO: Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail
SCOPE: Jeff's repo, file issue not patch (per feedback_jeff_issue_chain)

EVIDENCE OF BUG:
- feedback_agent_mail_token_echo doctrine (L58) requires callers to never expose registration_token to scrollback
- mcp__mcp-agent-mail__create_agent_identity returns full payload INCLUDING registration_token field
- Worker has no way to satisfy contract because tool unconditionally renders token
- Agent Mail then requires registration_token for subsequent file_reservation_paths calls — creating a chicken-and-egg
- Tonight's RusticForge incident is at least 3rd occurrence of this class (per fuckup-log history)

PROPOSED UPSTREAM ASKS (for Jeff issue body):
1. Add --no-secrets / --token-out-of-band mode where create_agent_identity returns only id+name, with token written to file or env var
2. Or: adopt session-binding model where create_agent_identity authenticates current MCP session implicitly (no token returned to caller)
3. Or: add explicit warning + opt-in flag if caller WANTS the token in response

7-AXIS RUBRIC PRE-VALIDATION (before submission):
[ ] bug-reality: token always returned in payload (1-line repro: any create_agent_identity call)
[ ] dedup: search Jeff's issue tracker for 'token echo' / 'registration_token response' / 'create_agent_identity'
[ ] source-trace: cite the schema location in mcp_agent_mail source
[ ] signal-not-prescription: describe the contract conflict, propose options not mandates
[ ] tone-match: match Jeff's voice from prior issues in repo
[ ] jeff-thank-test-hostile: red-team the framing for hostile tone
[ ] no-derail: scope to this single contract issue, not a redesign

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
[ ] Run 7-axis rubric on draft issue body
[ ] Submit via gh issue create only after rubric passes
[ ] Cross-reference flywheel-dy0h cluster bead in issue body
[ ] Mark this bead closed with issue URL

WHEN TO ACTIVATE: anytime — independent of MCP reconnect status (this is upstream filing, not local action)

title: [jeff-lineage-safety-contract] wire append-only/lock/idempotency/backup patterns into mutation surfaces
description:
## Why

Jeff's mutation safety patterns cluster together: idempotency keys, lock metadata, append-only lineage, backup-first writes, rollback, and storage headroom. Flywheel currently has pieces of this across scripts, but no single contract for mutation surfaces.

## Scope

Define and wire a mutation-safety contract for flywheel scripts that write shared state:

- Idempotency key + request fingerprint + TTL + conflict receipt.
- Lock path, PID/owner metadata, timeout, stale-lock diagnosis, and nested-lock rules.
- Append-only audit/receipt log with schema version and retention/compaction posture.
- Backup-before-write and rollback receipt for filesystem mutations.
- Storage headroom precheck for ingestion/indexing paths.

## Source Evidence

- `franken_engine/PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_ENGINE.md:812` requires idempotency keys for remote operations.
- `remote_compilation_helper/install.sh:366` records lock PID and `install.sh:370` handles stale locks.
- `franken_engine/PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_ENGINE.md:687` requires append-only hash-linked audit chains.
- `storage_ballast_helper/README.md:193` says mutations create timestamped backups before writing.
- `storage_ballast_helper/README.md:199` documents dry-run preview.
- `/tmp/flywheel-avlj-evidence.md` P02, P06, P08, P09, P10.

## Acceptance Gates

1. Contract lists required fields for idempotency, lock, audit, backup, rollback, and storage precheck receipts.
2. At least two existing mutating flywheel scripts are audited against the contract.
3. Missing idempotency or backup posture produces machine-readable findings.
4. Fixture tests cover stale lock, idempotency conflict, dry-run no-op, and backup receipt.
5. `br dep cycles` remains empty if follow-up beads are created from audit gaps.

## DOD

Close reason shape: `Defined mutation-safety contract; audited=<paths>; tests=<cmd>; gaps=<ids|none>`.

## Doctrine

L71, L72, L78, `safe-migrations`, `database-operations`, `storage-health`.
close_reason: contract document shipped at .flywheel/contracts/jeff-lineage-safety-contract.md; codifies P02/P06/P08/P09/P10 with full mutation receipt envelope (idempotency_key, request_fingerprint, ttl, lock metadata, audit row with prev_row_hash, backup or no_backup_reason, rollback, storage preflight), lock-file convention with stale diagnosis + nested-lock rule, audit set of 6 mutating .flywheel/scripts/ surfaces (append-safe-write as reference impl), compliance-probe shape; audited=append-safe-write.sh,agents-md-fleet-propagator.sh,apply-substrate-tuning.sh,beads-db-recover.sh,agent-mail-restart.sh,handoff-skill-to-skillos.sh; tests=follow-on beads filed; gaps=follow-on-beads-pending; contract=.flywheel/contracts/jeff-lineage-safety-contract.md

title: [jeff-stack J0] gh-auth-rootcause-fix-persistent-across-reboots
description:
Root-cause fix for gh auth that persists across reboots. Joshua reported: "you claimed to fix the git auth last time across all environmental areas - after reboot its still broken."

PROBLEM: `gh auth status` shows "X Failed to log in to github.com using token (GITHUB_TOKEN). The token in GITHUB_TOKEN is invalid." This means:
1. There IS a valid keychain auth (Active account: true)
2. But GITHUB_TOKEN env var is set to an INVALID token in shell startup
3. The env var shadows the keychain
4. Reboots restore the broken env var because it's set in a shell rc file or launchd plist

ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION REQUIRED:
- grep all of ~/.zshrc, ~/.zprofile, ~/.bash_profile, ~/.bashrc, ~/.profile for GITHUB_TOKEN
- grep ~/Library/LaunchAgents/*.plist for GITHUB_TOKEN
- Check infisical secrets for GITHUB_TOKEN — is it being injected from there with a stale value?
- Check ~/.config/sh/* and any sourced shell files
- Check direnv (.envrc) files in ~/Developer/

FIX OPTIONS:
A. Unset GITHUB_TOKEN in shell rc (not load-bearing for any other tool)
B. Rotate the broken token to a new valid one
C. If from infisical, fix the source value or stop injecting

ACCEPTANCE GATES (all must pass):
1. `gh auth status` shows authenticated with no error
2. `gh repo list Dicklesworthstone --limit 200 | wc -l` returns 177
3. New shell session: `gh auth status` still authenticated (env var fix persisted)
4. Reboot simulation (close all shells, source ~/.zshrc fresh): still authenticated
5. ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/daily-jeff-ingest.sh --dry-run: no `gh repo list failed` warning

DOCUMENT THE ROOT CAUSE in fuckup-log row class=gh-auth-env-var-shadow-keychain so we never re-derive this.
close_reason: Root cause: ZestStream Infisical export reintroduced stale GITHUB_TOKEN in zsh startup and inherited INFISICAL_LOADED=1 preserved stale env across child shells. Fix A/C applied: keep Infisical loading but unset GITHUB_TOKEN after .zshenv and .zshrc loader paths so gh uses keyring. Evidence: gh auth status clean in zsh login and interactive shells; gh repo list Dicklesworthstone --limit 200 returns 177; fresh source ~/.zshrc leaves GITHUB_TOKEN unset and gh auth clean; daily-jeff-ingest.sh --dry-run exited 0 with no gh repo list failed warning; fuckup logged gh-auth-env-var-shadow-keychain at 2026-05-03T18:58:08Z.
