title: [doctrine] flywheel-cleanup-scratch helper — DCG-allowlisted scratch-dir cleanup primitive
description:
## Source

Recurring fuckup class observed 10+ times in last 3 hours (2026-05-08T23:52Z onward):
- flywheel-glrlb rm -rf absolute path blocked
- flywheel-olp9b rm-rf, find-delete, mv-to-Trash all blocked
- flywheel-z6lk3 rm-rf + mv from /var/folders blocked
- flywheel-mjrly reservation+cleanup combo blocked
- flywheel-wire-worker-close-requires-git-commit-a1912c82 unlinks blocked
- flywheel-rdqc7.1 recursive cleanup blocked
- flywheel-03uki overbroad scratch cleanup blocked
- flywheel-ftj0m rm -rf dispatch scratch blocked
- flywheel-wire-dispatch-to-lib-not-bin redirect-truncate blocked
- ... and counting

## Pattern

Workers create scratch via `mktemp -d /var/folders/.../T/flywheel-<bead-id>.XXXXXX.*`, do work, attempt `rm -rf <absolute_path>` to clean up. DCG PreToolUse hook blocks `rm -rf` regardless of path scope. Workers recover via fallback (rmdir, explicit unlinks, mv-to-Trash) which is less reliable + leaves orphans in `/var/folders/.../T/`.

## Fix: canonical helper

Author `~/.local/bin/flywheel-cleanup-scratch` (or `.flywheel/scripts/cleanup-scratch.sh`).
- Single argument: absolute path
- Validates path matches `^/var/folders/.*/T/(flywheel|josh|wave)-.*` OR `^/tmp/(flywheel|dispatch_)-.*`
- If validates: emits `rm -rf` via internal logic
- DCG allowlist: permit this binary by full path (already proven pattern with tmp-aggressive-prune-cron-wrapper)

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Author `.flywheel/scripts/cleanup-scratch.sh` with path validation + JSON output (--dry-run / --apply / --json shape).
AG2: Add to DCG allowlist by full path (per `dcg` skill canonical-by-full-path pattern).
AG3: Update worker-tick template to call `flywheel-cleanup-scratch $TMPDIR_PATH` instead of raw `rm -rf` at end of bead.
AG4: Test fixtures: valid paths (canonical scratch), invalid paths (refused with reason), non-existent paths (no-op).
AG5: Doctrine: `.flywheel/doctrine/scratch-cleanup-canonical-pattern.md` capturing the pattern + reference to fuckup history (10+ occurrences).
AG6: Propagate via doctrine-sync 6h cadence — all 57 fleet repos pick up cleanup helper + worker-tick template change.

## Cross-references

- Memory: `feedback_dcg_prose_trigger_strip_dangerous_substrings`
- Skill: `dcg` (canonical destructive-command-guard)
- Existing canonical-by-full-path pattern: `tmp-aggressive-prune-cron-wrapper`, `codex-rollout-permission-janitor`
- Fuckup-log evidence at ~/.local/state/flywheel/fuckup-log.jsonl (10+ entries 2026-05-08T23:52Z+)
close_reason: Implemented canonical scratch cleanup helper; tests, DCG allowlist, doctrine sync local propagation, L112 probe, and evidence receipt pass. Commit 9492552.

title: [CRITICAL] JSM-managed skills will overwrite our worker edits — audit + jsm-push-ready-patch discipline needed
description:
## Joshua observation (2026-05-08T18:55Z)

"When we update our jsm skills - since some of these are jsm skills - they will overwrite the edits we're making here."

## Risk

Workers across waves 13-14 have been directly editing skill files at \`~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md\`. Some of those skills are JSM-managed. The next \`jsm push\` or \`jsm pull --update\` will OVERWRITE the worker's edits with the canonical JSM version.

## What we've seen

Worker discipline IS present in some cases:
- flywheel-irm9 (skill-enhance-cass): worker detected JSM management, produced \`jsm_push_ready_patch=yes\` artifact INSTEAD of direct mutation. CORRECT PATTERN.
- flywheel-jf7c, npo8, 96np, nf7x, ewqr, d7lv: worker reported \`jsm_status=not_installed_or_saved_in_jsm_db\` and edited directly. SAFE for now but unprotected against future JSM enrollment.

AG1: AUDIT — generate report of which \`~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md\` files are JSM-managed vs unmanaged. Use \`jsm list\` and check each skill that received worker edits this session (waves 13-14).

AG2: REVERT-OR-PROMOTE — for each JSM-managed skill that received direct worker edits this session: either revert the live skill (jsm pull) and produce a jsm-push-ready patch instead, OR push the worker's edits via jsm push.

AG3: ENFORCEMENT — extend worker dispatch packet for skill-enhance class:
  - Pre-flight: \`jsm status <skill-name>\` MUST run before any direct skill mutation
  - If JSM-managed: worker MUST produce jsm-push-ready patch artifact (not mutate live)
  - If unmanaged: worker MAY mutate directly, but MUST also write the same patch as a jsm-import-ready artifact in case the skill becomes JSM-managed later

AG4: DOCTRINE — promote this pattern to canonical L-rule (or extend an existing one): skill-enhance work MUST honor JSM management state, with jsm-push-ready-patch as the canonical artifact, not direct mutation.

AG5: REGRESSION — fixture test: skill-enhance dispatch packet to JSM-managed skill MUST refuse direct mutation; dispatch packet to unmanaged skill MUST still produce jsm-push-ready patch as artifact.

## Cross-references

title: [autoloop-p1-queue] add scan scoring queue dry-run and cooldowns
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/autonomous-loop-2026-04-30.md`

## Scope

Implement Phase 1 scan/scoring/queue mode for `flywheel-autoloop`.

## Required Behavior

- Add `scan --json` and `scan --dry-run --json`.
- Add deterministic scoring from the plan's 14-signal formula, with thresholds:
  `<50 skip`, `50-79 queue`, `80-99 dispatch-eligible`, `>=100 priority`.
- Write `$FLYWHEEL_AUTOLOOP_STATE_DIR/queue.json` with scored repo candidates.
- Write/update `$FLYWHEEL_AUTOLOOP_STATE_DIR/repo-state.json` for negative cache
  and cooldowns.
- Add allowlist support via `FLYWHEEL_AUTOLOOP_REPOS`.
- Enforce same-repo cooldown: max once per 4 hours unless override/high score.
- Keep Phase 1 as queue/scan only; no agent dispatch.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `flywheel-autoloop scan --dry-run --json | jq '.status and .queue'` succeeds.
2. `flywheel-autoloop scan --json` writes `queue.json` with score, reason, repo,
   threshold, and cooldown fields.
3. Fixture with no ready repos yields `status=="idle"` and no tick dispatch.
4. Fixture with one ready repo yields a non-empty queue entry and deterministic
   score across repeated runs.
5. `FLYWHEEL_AUTOLOOP_REPOS=<repo>` limits candidates to the allowlist.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Implemented Phase 1 scan/scoring/queue; tests=<commands>; queue=<path>`.

## Out Of Scope

LaunchAgent interval change, dispatch subcommand, stop/status subcommands.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: dry-run and fixture gates prove deterministic queue behavior.
- DOCUMENTED: queue schema and scoring thresholds documented in help/config.
- SURFACED: later doctor/status bead exposes queue health and age.
close_reason: Implemented Phase 1 scan/scoring/queue; tests=bash tests/flywheel-autoloop-canonical-cli.sh; queue=/Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel-autoloop/queue.json

title: [data-backed-deferral-dispatch-lint] wire Option C and 8i5 integration
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/data-backed-deferral-guard-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-bbts
Plan predecessor: flywheel-28v

## Goal

Wire Option C dispatch-skill lint and flywheel-8i5 integration so selected actions go through the canonical dispatch path instead of becoming question-shaped handoffs.

## Scope

- Update dispatch authoring surfaces or templates to require the reduced predicate before asking Joshua to choose among dispatchable work.
- Reject empty override reasons when `evidence_missing`, `requires_joshua_decision=true`, or `tie_between` is used.
- Test integration flow: 28v selects the action, 8i5 enforces canonical dispatch transport/callback contract.
- Preserve dispatch packet scopes and file-reservation requirements.

## Acceptance Gates

1. Dispatch template or skill text contains a data-backed-deferral lint step referencing the plan path and reduced predicate signals: idle worker, ready work, and PageRank/doctor alignment.
2. A test or scripted fixture fails a dispatch draft that ends with `Want me to ...?` when idle workers and ready work are present without an override.
3. A test or scripted fixture passes a dispatch draft with `evidence_missing=<named datum>` or `tie_between=<a,b>` and rejects blank reasons.
4. Integration test or receipt proves the flow `28v predicate selects action -> 8i5 canonical dispatch enforcement -> ntm dispatch + callback contract`.
5. `rg -n "data-backed-deferral|DATA_BACKED_DEFERRAL_DETECTED|flywheel-8i5|canonical dispatch" ~/.claude/commands ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel .flywheel/scripts tests` finds dispatch lint and coverage.
6. Callback guidance includes DID/DIDNT/GAPS reporting for any dispatch lint correction.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: lint tests prove reject and allow cases.
- DOCUMENTED: dispatch authoring text explains the reduced predicate and override receipt shape.
- SURFACED: lint failures are machine-readable and reference the canonical dispatch enforcement path.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L50, L51, L52, L70, L71, and L80.
- Plan lines 151-180 and 278-304 for Option C and 8i5 relationship.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.

## Out Of Scope

- No hard pre-output hook.
- No changes to ntm itself.
- No new dispatch transport outside the canonical 8i5 path.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `wired data-backed-deferral dispatch lint and 8i5 integration; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-dispatch-lint]`.

title: [three-q-surface-registry] implement 3-Q surface registry and audit runner
description:
### Title

`[three-q-surface-registry] implement 3-Q surface registry and audit runner`

### Goal

Make every flywheel surface finite, enumerable, and mechanically auditable against Joshua's three questions: validated end to end, documented in the right doctrine surface, and surfaced into the learning loop. This bead creates the registry and audit runner that turn the META-RULE into a machine-checkable contract.

### Why now

Trauma classes: `documented-not-wired`, `silent-finding-loss`, `doctrine-orphaning`, and `surface-validation-blindness`. `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md` established the umbrella META-RULE, and Phase 3 found the plan still had B13/B14-shaped holes: lens 1+2 found eight surfaces failing at least one question, while lens 3 found B14 absent even though the final DAG depends on it. B14 is the Meadows #5 rule plus #6 mechanism that prevents "every surface" from staying an unbounded phrase.

### Acceptance gates

1. Registry file shape is defined with `schema_version`, `surface_id`, `category`, `owner`, `repo`, `runtime_scope`, `q1_validated`, `q2_documented`, `q3_surfaced`, `evidence_refs[]`, `last_checked_ts`, `status`, and `gap_reason`. Proof receipt: `B14_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
2. Initial registry seed covers the Lane A surface taxonomy, including L-rules, INCIDENTS.md trauma classes, memory notes, skills, beads, doctor signals, tick phases, dispatch templates, worker callbacks, hooks, launchd plists, CLI surfaces, MCP servers, agent-mail, josh-requests, `/flywheel:learn`, and cross-orch propagation paths. Proof receipt: `B14_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
3. Audit runner command emits deterministic JSON and non-zero strict status when any required surface has a missing or stale Q1/Q2/Q3 evidence ref. Proof receipt: `B14_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
4. Doctor integration emits `surfaces_unwired_count` and includes the top failing surface ids with Q1/Q2/Q3 failure flags. Proof receipt: `B14_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
5. Every registered surface has an automated probe or an explicit `manual_or_external_reason`; `unknown` is allowed during migration but cannot count as `validated`. Proof receipt: `B14_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
6. Fixtures cover documented-only, validated-only, surfaced-only, complete, stale-evidence, missing-artifact, and unknown-runtime surfaces. Proof receipt: `B14_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
7. B04 can consume the registry output without parsing prose, and B09 can route newly discovered unwired-surface failures into `/flywheel:learn` exactly once. Proof receipt: `B14_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
8. The audit runner can filter by surface category and by owner bead so Phase 4 workers can prove their own surface without rerunning the entire fleet audit. Proof receipt: `B14_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.
9. The registry explicitly models Codex and Claude separately when a surface is runtime-dependent, so a Claude-only pass cannot satisfy a cross-runtime surface. Proof receipt: `B14_AG9=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` must be present in the implementation callback.

### DOD

Close reason shape: `registry=<path> audit_cmd=<cmd>:PASS doctor_signal=surfaces_unwired_count fixtures=<dir>:PASS categories=<N> commit=<sha>`.  
Commit message tag: `[three-q-surface-registry]`.

### Doctrine references

`feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md`, L60, L61, L70, L56, L69, `02-REFINE-r4.md`, `03-AUDIT-FINDINGS.md`, `01-RESEARCH-A.md`.

### Out of scope

No retroactive repair of every failing surface. This bead defines the registry, probes, fixtures, and signal; B04 consumes the signal, B09 routes learning events, B10 lands doctrine, and later implementation beads remediate specific surface failures.

### Dependencies

Blocks on: B01, B13.  
Blocks: B04, B09, B10, B12.  
Related parent: `flywheel-2p25`.  
References: B11 for runtime-specific parity probes and B04 for doctor signal consumption.

### Pre-flight blockers

BLOCKED BY: Phase 3 audit findings only if Joshua changes the finite surface taxonomy or strictness threshold. No external binary is required for the initial registry/audit runner.

### Wave assignment

Wave 4, before B04 finalizes doctor signals and before B10 lands THREE-Q doctrine.

### Phase 5 polish contract

#### Acceptance gate proof mapping

- `AG1` closes with `B14_AG1=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG2` closes with `B14_AG2=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG3` closes with `B14_AG3=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG4` closes with `B14_AG4=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG5` closes with `B14_AG5=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG6` closes with `B14_AG6=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG7` closes with `B14_AG7=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG8` closes with `B14_AG8=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.
- `AG9` closes with `B14_AG9=<command|file|schema|fixture|validation_receipt>:PASS` and evidence path or stdout/stderr log.

#### Dry-run and rollback posture

- Default implementation posture is read-only or dry-run first for every mutating path; apply mode must be explicit and idempotent.
- Rollback evidence must be named before mutation: either revert the implementing commit, restore from the pre-mutation backup artifact, or mark the bead blocked with `blocked_by=<specific rollback gap>`.
- Shared-state writes must use repo-local paths, Agent Mail file reservation when files are touched, and audit receipts for any Beads, JSONL, or state-db mutation.

#### Implementation callback evidence shape

Worker DONE callback must include: `bead=<id> evidence=<artifact-or-command> artifact_checks=<list> validation_receipt=<path-or-none> tests=<cmd>:PASS files_released=<paths> beads_filed|beads_updated|no_bead_reason=<receipt> fuckups_logged=<classes|none> chain_blocked_reason=<reason|none>`.

#### Phase 5 pre-flight blocker receipt

- `preflight_status=clear_for_implementation` for plan-local blockers resolved by Phase 3 audit and Phase 4 decomposition.
- External blockers named in the original bead body remain explicit `blocked_by=<bead-or-condition>` and must not be silently bypassed.
- If implementation discovers a missing primitive, file or update the minimal repo-local bead and add a dependency before closing the consumer.

#### Phase 5 dependency receipt

Actual Beads graph dependencies after Phase 4 are recorded in the polish ledger. The implementation worker must check `br dep list --json --direction down <id>` and `br dep cycles` before claiming ready/closed.

### Canonical CLI scoping checklist

This bead mentions a command, CLI, flag, executable path, or operator surface. Implementation dispatch must carry this checklist and the worker callback must report PASS/FAIL/N/A for each item:

- `doctor` / `health` / `repair` triad exists or explicit `N/A=<reason>` when the bead only extends a non-CLI data contract.
- `validate` / `audit` / `why` exists for any state-handling command.
- `--info`, `--examples`, `quickstart`, `help <topic>`, and `completion <shell>` are present for any new operator-facing CLI.
- `--json` is available everywhere machine output is consumed; exit codes are stable and documented.
- Mutating commands support `--dry-run`, `--explain`, idempotency key, and audit record.
- `schema <command>` or equivalent schema emission exists for machine-readable outputs.
- Long-running commands expose metrics/logs/trace or an equivalent observable receipt.
- Name-collision precheck is recorded with `which <name>` plus ownership proof before claiming any command name.

title: Refile pi cherry-pick from skipped 95ed40e0 (Jeff-canonical, separate from local cubcode)
description:
Trauma class: bundled-cherry-pick-skipped-with-correction-after.

2026-05-02 Joshua's reconcile of ntm pulled in commit 95ed40e0 'feat(ensemble): add cubcode and pi as first-class agent types'. Worker (pane 2) hit 4 UU files with semantic conflicts and called blocked. Joshua's first call was 'skip', then corrected: pi is Jeff's (Dicklesworthstone/pi_agent_rust), cubcode is Joshua's (CC-mirror/CC-router for local models — agent-mail-aware).

The bundled commit was therefore mixed: Jeff-canonical pi + Joshua-local cubcode. Skipping was right because cubcode isn't yet upstream-canonical, but we lost pi support in the skip.

Fix: refile separately.
1. Pi: cherry-pick the pi-only portions of 95ed40e0 against current HEAD (resolve any conflicts preserving HEAD's broader aliases like cursor/windsurf/AgentOllama). This goes into upstream-track.
2. Cubcode: separate flywheel-local feature commit, never upstreamed (or at minimum gated behind a feature flag until cubcode design stabilizes).

Acceptance:
1. Pi cherry-pick lands cleanly OR upstream issue filed asking Jeff to split 95ed40e0 into a pi-only commit
2. Cubcode integration kept as separate flywheel-local commit on local/bead-isolation-reconciled-* branch
3. ntm rebuild + verify build passes after re-applying pi support
4. memory updated to reflect pi=Jeff, cubcode=Joshua
5. Document doctrine: 'when cherry-picking upstream commits that touch local-only code, split them at the cherry-pick boundary, don't bundle'

Source: /tmp/ntm_worktree_cleanup_findings.md (semantic conflict analysis)
Source: /tmp/ntm_cherrypick_skip_findings.md (skip outcome)

title: [convergence-dispatch-gate-narrow] reduce false-positive transport denials
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/convergence-bead-plan-2026-05-01.md`

## Scope

Narrow `~/.claude/hooks/flywheel-loop-dispatch-transport-gate.sh` so it blocks true raw dispatch attempts without false-positive blocking canonical `ntm send` payloads or control-mode commands.

## Required Behavior

- Split compound commands on command separators before matching.
- Skip control-mode cancellation commands such as `tmux send-keys -X cancel`.
- Match forbidden dispatch tokens only inside the actual raw dispatch segment.
- Keep `ntm send` as canonical transport and explicitly document that it is allowed.
- Preserve true-positive denial for raw pane dispatch attempts and retired `ntm assign --auto --watch`.

## Acceptance Gates

1. Raw pane dispatch fixture is denied.
2. `tmux send-keys -X cancel; ntm send ...` fixture is allowed.
3. `ntm send ... "Body mentions tmux send-keys"` fixture is allowed.
4. `ntm assign picoz --auto --watch` fixture is denied.
5. Existing hook tests still pass, or a new hook-specific test script passes all four fixtures.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Narrowed dispatch transport gate; tests=<cmd>; true_positive=<n>; false_positive=<n>`.

## Dependencies

No implementation dependency.

## Out Of Scope

Changing NTM itself or weakening L29 canonical transport doctrine.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: true-positive and false-positive fixtures.
- DOCUMENTED: hook comment explains canonical `ntm send` allowance.
- SURFACED: repeated denials remain visible through fuckup/incident flow.
close_reason: Narrowed dispatch transport gate; tests=hook gate1 (raw dispatch w/dispatch tokens denied), gate2 (cancel+ntm allowed), gate3 (ntm w/tmux mention in body allowed), gate4 (ntm assign --auto --watch denied); true_positive=2; false_positive=0

title: [doctrine] codex 0.129 background-terminal-wedge freeze class — distinct from 0.125 classes
description:
## Source

Codex 0.129 canary on flywheel-x2okl: TWO freeze occurrences in single session.

## Occurrence 1: 2026-05-08T~21:00Z
- /goal mode pursuing FrankenTerm watchtower bead
- Spawned background terminal cross-repo (codex-canary → flywheel/scripts/)
- Wedged at 6m23s, velocity=0
- Snapshot: /tmp/codex-canary-freeze-snapshot-20260508T210541Z.json

## Occurrence 2: 2026-05-08T~21:30Z
- Joshua confirmed second freeze
- Same goal still in pursuit (or auto-resumed)
- Pattern recurring → not single-event

## Recommendation (DOCTRINE)

**HOLD codex-cli 0.129 fleet rollout** until 0.130 stable cuts. The /goal mode + background-terminal subsystem in 0.129 is unstable for sustained agent-swarm use. Two freezes in one session = unsuitable for production fleet.

Specifically:
- DO NOT rollout 0.129 to alpsinsurance / mobile-eats / skillos worker panes
- DO NOT migrate flywheel canonical pane bin
- KILL codex-canary session — the canary did its job (proved /goal works AND proved the freeze class)
- WAIT for 0.130 stable cut + 24h community soak before re-canarying

## Recovery from canary freeze

ntm respawn codex-canary --panes=0 → relaunch codex-129 → resume /goal pursuit. Survives but freezes again under same goal.

## Workaround for /goal mode value

If Joshua wants /goal mode capability pre-0.130:
- Issue /goal commands MANUALLY in interactive sessions only (operator-driven, not agent-orchestrated)
- Avoid cross-repo goals (limit blast radius of background-terminal wedges)
- Use 0.125 + manual prompt persistence as inferior-but-stable substitute

## Acceptance gates

AG1 (existing): Improve robot-activity classifier — distinguish "TUI says Working" from "process actually working".
AG2 (existing): Document mass-respawn as canonical recovery for stale-tui-counter class.
AG3 (REVISED): Kill codex-canary session today. Don't re-canary until 0.130 stable.
AG4 (existing): Investigate two-binary contamination theory if relevant.
AG5 (REVISED): Add 0.130 cut to jeff-binary-version-watchtower (extends to codex repos too — flywheel-ubrb5-FrankenTerm precedent).

## Cross-references

- flywheel-x2okl (canary parent) — STATUS: implementation done, canary unstable, fleet rollout HOLD
- flywheel-fg2v5 (NTM custom respawn primitive gap) — still relevant
- flywheel-ubrb5 (FrankenTerm watchtower) — could co-track 0.130 cut

title: [INCIDENT-2026-05-08] tmux socket killed by aggressive /tmp prune — 4 fleet sessions orphaned
description:
2026-05-08T23:09Z incident: tmp-aggressive-prune.sh (just shipped 2c21355 for flywheel-2bd2r Layer 2) included /private/tmp/tmux-501/* in its prune scope. Socket inode survived in tmux server FDs but directory entry deleted, preventing new connections. Subsequent `tmux new-session` recreated socket but original server's session table is unreachable from new socket file.

LOST: 4 fleet sessions (flywheel, skillos, mobile-eats, alpsinsurance) and all worker panes including:
- flywheel pane 2: 91m7 (dekp partial→9/9 + ulha) — IN FLIGHT
- flywheel pane 3: icua (viux public lens) — IN FLIGHT
- flywheel pane 4: 2bd2r (P0 /tmp lifecycle Layers 1+3+4) — IN FLIGHT (ironic — the bead defining the doctrine that would prevent this)
- skillos/mobile-eats/alpsinsurance: unknown work-in-flight

LESSON: tmp-aggressive-prune.sh deny-list is incomplete. Must add:
- /private/tmp/tmux-501 (and tmux-* glob)
- /private/tmp/launchd-* (already in deny-list)
- any /private/tmp/<service>-<uid> pattern (IPC sockets)

IMMEDIATE: extend deny-list, ship hotfix to script, re-spawn fleet sessions, recover dispatch state from .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl (callbacks landed, can re-dispatch from last-known state).

JOSHUA-LENS 25yr ops: this is the canonical "the thing that fixes the symptom kills the patient" disaster. The sanctioned cleanup script needs an active-socket-aware deny-list, not just hostname-pattern matches.

This bead's existence proves Layer 4 (doctor invariant) of 2bd2r was load-bearing. Without it, this could happen again at any scale.

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The incident cause named in `[INCIDENT-2026-05-08] tmux socket killed by aggressive /tmp prune — 4 fleet sessions orphaned` is blocked by a script, deny-list, doctor signal, or documented recovery path.
AG2: A reproduction fixture, dry-run, or live-safe probe proves the same incident class is detected before damage.
AG3: The close receipt names the command output, affected artifact path, and residual risk or `no_bead_reason`.
close_reason: Fixed tmp-aggressive-prune IPC deny-list with tmux/service-uid/socket-entry protections; added live-safe fixture and receipts. Commit 12aaaec.

title: [jeff-stack J8] daily-launchd-plist-install
description:
Install daily launchd plist for jeff-daily-diff.sh.

DEPENDS ON: J7 (script exists and validated).

PROCEDURE:
1. Create ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.jeff-daily-stack-ingest.plist with:
   - Label: ai.zeststream.jeff-daily-stack-ingest
   - ProgramArguments: ["/bin/bash", "-lc", "/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/scripts/jeff-daily-diff.sh"]
   - StartCalendarInterval: Hour=6, Minute=0 (06:00 local, matches L63 cadence)
   - RunAtLoad: false
   - StandardOutPath: ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/launchd.stdout.log
   - StandardErrorPath: ~/.local/state/jeff-intel/launchd.stderr.log
   - EnvironmentVariables: PATH including ~/.local/bin and /opt/homebrew/bin
   - LowPriorityIO: true (don't compete with foreground work)
2. `launchctl bootstrap gui/<uid> ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.jeff-daily-stack-ingest.plist`
3. Verify: `launchctl print gui/<uid>/ai.zeststream.jeff-daily-stack-ingest | grep state`
4. Test trigger: `launchctl kickstart gui/<uid>/ai.zeststream.jeff-daily-stack-ingest`
5. Tail logs to confirm successful run

ACCEPTANCE GATES:
1. plist file syntactically valid (`plutil -lint <path>` passes)
2. launchctl list shows the agent loaded
3. Manual kickstart produces a successful run with report written
4. Tomorrow at 06:00 it fires automatically (verify next morning)
5. Plist is idempotent: re-bootstrapping doesn't duplicate

title: agent-mail-launchd-maxfiles-and-doctor-fd-probe
description: Per fd_leak_diagnosis 2026-05-01 (flywheel-0w1): Agent Mail LaunchAgent inherits launchd soft maxfiles=256 and wedges when service leaks lockfile FDs (209 leaked .commit.lock/.archive.lock FDs to unlinked paths). Add SoftResourceLimits/HardResourceLimits NumberOfFiles to ai.zeststream.mcp-agent-mail-local.plist (target: 4096 soft / 65536 hard). Add doctor probe that checks child PID numeric FDs, max FD, lock-FD count, launchctl maxfiles. Doctor warn >128 FDs or >25 lock FDs; fail >220 FDs. Add modern install/restart helper. Acceptance: (1) restart clears descriptor count to baseline ~30; (2) doctor PASS on fresh service; (3) one file_reservation_paths call succeeds; (4) under sustained load (50 reservations), service does not wedge. Source: /tmp/fd_leak_diagnosis.md. Companion: upstream Jeff issue Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail (regression of #116). NO source modifications without Joshua approval per L48.

ntm#111 and A2/A4 validation show the filing shape works and prevents non-bug filings.

## Dependencies
Depends on at least one completed A-stream filing closeout or existing ntm#111/A2 proof.

## Inputs / Outputs
Files in scope: ~/.claude/skills/dicklesworthstone-stack; flywheel slash command surface; reference_upstream_issues.md
Output artifact: committed implementation and/or evidence artifact named in acceptance criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria
- invoke the new workflow on one candidate and produce an 8-section draft, validation ladder artifact, duplicate search evidence, and Monitor plan

## Testing Obligations
- Test command generation without filing; ensure no issue is submitted before Joshua review.

## Origin
00-MASTER-PLAN.md §IV lines 194-204; filing playbook in INCIDENTS.md.

## Out of Scope
Do not automate gh issue create without explicit submission gate.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: include [B8] in the commit message or close reason.
- Acceptance evidence is captured in the bead closeout.
- Dependency graph remains acyclic after close.

title: [skillos-proposal] bake journey-writing into dispatch + loop protocols (4-layer architecture)
description:
## Source

skillos:1 / BrightLake cross-orch proposal 2026-05-08T18:30Z. No action required this tick — informational alignment ask.

## Goal

Convert L61 ECOSYSTEM-TOUCH (AGENTS + README on doctrine landing) and similar META-RULEs from suggestions into mechanical gates by baking journey-writing into dispatch + loop protocols.

## Four-layer architecture

**Layer 1 — per-bead journey entry (dispatch-time)**
- Extend `_shared/dispatch-template.md` callback contract: require `journey_entry_path=<repo>/.flywheel/journal/<bead-id>.md`
- Worker writes 50-200 word prose entry per bead alongside JSON receipt
- Schema: `.flywheel/validation-schema/v1/journey-entry.v1.schema.json`
- Validators refuse close without journey_entry_path

**Layer 2 — post-merge auto-doc**
- `.flywheel/scripts/post-merge-doc-update.sh` invoked by gh-wrapper or launchd PR-event scanner
- Reads PR body + commit + journal entry; auto-bumps README CLI section + CHANGELOG.md
- Doctor field `readme_post_merge_staleness_hours` fails when README un-touched N ticks after PR merge

**Layer 3 — daily-report journal rollup (extend L77)**
- `.flywheel/scripts/daily-report.sh` already exists (flywheel-o7dq) — extend inputs to include `.flywheel/journal/*.md` written that day
- Doctor refuses when daily-report doesn't include yesterday's journal entries

**Layer 4 — session synthesis (operator-disposed)**
- New skill `session-synthesis-writer` OR extend `living-documentation`
- Operator surface: `/flywheel:journal --synthesize --since=<ts>` → bg agent drafts `docs/session-<date>-<theme>.md`

## Estimated effort (per skillos:1)

- flywheel-journey-entry-schema: half day
- flywheel-post-merge-doc-hook: 1 day
- flywheel-daily-report-journal-rollup: 2 hours
- flywheel-session-synthesis-skill: half day

Total ~3 days flywheel-side work.

## Onboarding wiring

`/flywheel:adopt` + `/flywheel:onboard` install:
- `.flywheel/journal/` + .gitkeep
- post-merge launchd plist
- daily-report.sh extended config
- doctor invariants for README + CHANGELOG staleness

Makes journey-writing a property of every flywheel-installed repo at install time, not per-repo retrofit.

## Why it matters

- Rev-8 mission "self-improving capability loops" requires journey signal beyond code state
- Phase 13 (compounding visibility) explicitly needs real trauma-to-skill case studies — today hand-curated
- Phase 19 (Anthropic Skills Curator positioning) requires public narrative without operator overhead

## Cross-references

- `~/.claude/skills/readme-writing/SKILL.md`
- `~/.claude/skills/changelog-md-workmanship/SKILL.md`
- `~/.claude/skills/living-documentation/SKILL.md`
- AGENTS.md L61, L77, L91
- INCIDENTS.md fire-and-forget-dispatch (just landed today, same trauma family)
- skillos/docs/session-2026-05-08-flywheel-spin.md (in progress, Layer 4 prototype)

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Author Layer 1 schema (.flywheel/validation-schema/v1/journey-entry.v1.schema.json)
AG2: Extend dispatch-template.md callback contract with journey_entry_path requirement
AG3: Implement post-merge-doc-update.sh + launchd scanner
AG4: Extend daily-report.sh to consume journal/* files
AG5: Author session-synthesis-writer skill + /flywheel:journal --synthesize command
AG6: Wire all four layers into /flywheel:adopt + /flywheel:onboard install

title: [daily-report] build /flywheel:daily-report producing accretive-learning + Jeff-intel summary
description:
[daily-report] build /flywheel:daily-report producing accretive-learning + Jeff-intel summary

## Trauma class
`no-daily-narrative` — Joshua flagged 2026-05-03T23:43Z: "do we have the daily report process built?"

Answer: NO. There are dailyreflection/weeklyreflection/quarterlyreflection skills but NOTHING flywheel-specific. No reflections directory. No accretive-learning rollup. Result: every day's discoveries (new traumas, new memories, closed beads, Jeff-intel finds) live in scattered logs without synthesis.

## Goal
Build `/flywheel:daily-report` skill that runs once per day (via launchd or manual) and produces a structured rollup answering:

1. **What shipped?** — beads closed today (with class breakdown: validate-everything, jeff-substrate, doctrine, ecosystem)
2. **What did we learn?** — feedback memories added today; new trauma classes named; doctrine promoted; META-RULES codified
3. **What's Jeff up to?** — new Dicklesworthstone repo activity (commits, releases, issues he's responding to); cross-orch reference flywheel-1lpv
4. **What's stuck?** — beads in_flight >24h; stale plans; punted phases (ticks_punted_count); chain_blocked rows
5. **What's next?** — top P0 ready beads; mission-anchor drift signals; gap-hunt-probe top class
6. **Cross-orch state** — mobile-eats / skillos / alpsinsurance roster + recent ACK rate; cross-orch-coordination.jsonl summary

## Acceptance gates
1. `/flywheel:daily-report` skill at ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/ or as command at ~/.claude/commands/flywheel/daily-report.md
2. Output written to `<repo>/.flywheel/reports/daily-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md` (one per repo per day)
3. Aggregate sources: br ready/list, MEMORY.md diffs since yesterday, dispatch-log.jsonl, fuckup-log, doctor JSON, cross-orch-coordination.jsonl, jeff-corpus state, INCIDENTS additions
4. `notify` integration (per feedback_low_bead_threshold_work_hunt): if report flags hard blockers, send Pushover + mac-alert
5. canonical-paths.txt entry registers report directory
6. doctor signal `daily_report_age_hours` exists; flags fail if >36h
7. Tests in tests/ verify report generation against fixtures (zero-bead day, all-stuck day, normal day)
8. `flywheel:status` references most recent daily report
9. launchd plist (or recommend cron) ships report at fixed time daily

## Three-Q audit
- VALIDATED: tests pass; sample reports generated
- DOCUMENTED: skill SKILL.md; canonical-paths entry; AGENTS.md mention
- SURFACED: doctor signal; notify integration; latest report linked from MISSION.md or status

## Doctrine refs
- feedback_low_bead_threshold_work_hunt.md (notify integration)
- feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface.md (umbrella)
- feedback_substrate_watchtower_must_be_wired.md (Jeff-intel section)
- bead flywheel-1lpv (jeff-intel-network — daily monitoring)
- bead flywheel-k5yp (jeff-philosophy-study)
- ~/.local/bin/notify (Pushover + mac-alert)

## Skills cited
- canonical-cli-scoping
- accretive-cron-orchestration
- changelog-md-workmanship (report formatting)
- system-health (doctor signal pattern)

title: [ntm-117-jeff-response] per-pane capture provenance shipped on main 8cd9301c — adopt+dogfood decision needed
description:
## Jeff response on ntm#117
2026-05-03T22:42Z Jeff shipped deferred item #1 (per-pane capture provenance) at commit 8cd9301c on main.

## What landed (relevant to flywheel)
Three new fields on every pane in --robot-activity AND --robot-tail JSON output:
- `capture_collected_at` (stamp-before-call, RFC3339)
- `capture_provenance` ("live" | "unavailable")
- `capture_error` (omitempty; full classifier/tmux stderr verbatim, grep-able)

Per-pane independence: one pane failing doesn't poison the rest.
Backwards-compatible: omitempty tags preserve pre-#117 shape on happy path.

## Status
- Not yet in any release tag (latest tag v1.14.0 is from 2026-04-24)
- 8cd9301c is on main (committed 23 min after our flywheel session triggered the issue)
- Our installed binary v1.14.0-19-gda5627aa pre-dates this commit

## Acceptance gates for this bead
1. Decide: build ntm from main NOW vs wait for next tagged release
   - Build-from-main: gives us per-pane provenance immediately, but risks main-instability
   - Wait-for-tag: stable, but blocks our fleet-watchdog work (B11/B13 in validate-everything plan)
2. If build-from-main: backup current binary + build + verify --robot-activity emits new fields + log to jeff-substrate-upgrades.jsonl
3. If wait-for-tag: file dependency note in validate-everything plan B13 (orch-capture-parity) — ntm pane_pid + capture_collected_at are now load-bearing for L71 implementation
4. Either way: update reference_jeff_substrate_inventory.md memory note with the v1.14.0-→post commit
5. Reply to ntm#117 with our adoption decision per feedback_jeff_issue_chain
6. Update fleet-watchdog implementation plan (B11) to consume capture_provenance == "live" before claiming runtime parity

## Plan integration
- Validate-everything plan B11 (parity probe) was being designed around q03g binary; this Jeff substrate change provides equivalent per-pane provenance via ntm directly. Could simplify B11.
- B13 (orch-capture-parity, L71 candidate) gains a real implementation path via capture_provenance + per-session aggregation
- Joshua-disposes JD-002 (graduated proof level) was approved 23:05Z — this Jeff substrate makes "live runtime probe" cheaper

## Recommendation
Joshua-decision: build from main NOW, dogfood per Jeff's note ("We'll dogfood #1 inside the same tick it lands and report back through this thread").

## Doctrine
- feedback_jeff_substrate_version_drift (probe + decide upgrade)
- feedback_jeff_issue_chain (file response, reply to thread)
- L71-candidate (zuav) — substrate just shipped to enable
- B11/B13 in validate-everything plan integration

title: [stamping-upgrade] extend doctrine-sync to cover .flywheel/doctrine/ scripts/ and launchd/ templates
description:
## Goal

Make the fleet stamping system the most advanced, most accretive in the ecosystem: fast (existing 6h periodic), hyper accurate (content hashes, backup-before-write, append-only ledger), AND comprehensive (covers doctrine docs + scripts + launchd templates, not just AGENTS.md).

## Current state (after 2026-05-08 session)

✅ Working:
- AGENTS.md canonical block sync (sync-canonical-doctrine.sh)
- Full AGENTS.md replace sync (flywheel-doctrine-sync binary, 6h periodic via launchd)
- 57 fleet repos auto-discovered, 56 root + 57 canonical syncs / pass, 0 errors typical
- Backup-before-write per repo (AGENTS.md.bak.<ts>)
- Append-only ledger at ~/.local/state/flywheel/doctrine-sync-ledger.jsonl
- Schemas (storage-override, identity-deferral) and bead-quality-mining.sh sync via sync-canonical-doctrine.sh

❌ Gap (this bead's scope):
- `.flywheel/doctrine/*.md` — 5+ docs in flywheel that don't propagate (loop-non-accretive-trauma-class.md, topology-restoration-after-migrate-strip.md, fleet-doctrine-hr-policy.md, tick-script-skillos-doctor-emission.md, tmp-lifecycle.md, etc.)
- `.flywheel/scripts/*.sh` — only one (bead-quality-mining.sh) propagates today; dispatch-and-verify.sh, tmp-aggressive-prune.sh, topology-tick-refresh.sh, etc. should propagate
- `.flywheel/launchd/*.plist` — plist templates for fleet-installable cron jobs

## Why this matters

L140-L142 (this session) reference doctrine docs by path. Fleet repos got the L-rule summary but NOT the underlying doctrine doc. Cross-references in canonical point to paths that don't exist in the target repo. This is "stamped" but not "fully stamped."

## Acceptance Gates

AG1: Extend `flywheel-doctrine-sync` binary OR `sync-canonical-doctrine.sh` to also walk `.flywheel/doctrine/*.md` from source and copy to each in-scope repo's `.flywheel/doctrine/`. Backup-before-write for each touched file.

AG2: Add `.flywheel/scripts/*.sh` propagation with allowlist (some scripts are repo-local and must NOT propagate; the canonical list explicitly names which ARE fleet-shared).

AG3: Add `.flywheel/launchd/*.plist` propagation as templates (the receiving repo can choose to launchctl bootstrap them or not; sync just makes them available).

AG4: Update doctor probe `fleet_three_surface_drift` to detect drift in doctrine docs / scripts / plists, not just AGENTS.md L-rules.

AG5: Run apply across fleet, verify 0 errors, verify all 5 new doctrine docs from 2026-05-08 land in every fleet repo with content hash matching canonical.

## Constraints

- Repo-local scripts (e.g. `mobile-eats-loop-with-receipt-mirror.sh` for mobile-eats) MUST NOT propagate. Use explicit allowlist in canonical.
- Backup discipline preserved (no in-place writes without .bak).
- Append-only ledger discipline preserved (every write logged).
- 6h periodic cadence preserved (no faster pulse needed; tick-time advisory checks).

## References

- doctrine: `.flywheel/doctrine/fleet-doctrine-hr-policy.md`
- doctrine: `.flywheel/doctrine/loop-non-accretive-trauma-class.md`
- script: `.flywheel/scripts/sync-canonical-doctrine.sh`
- binary: `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-doctrine-sync`
- plist: `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.flywheel-doctrine-sync.plist` (6h periodic)
- canonical L139 (TMP-LIFECYCLE) cross-refs `.flywheel/doctrine/tmp-lifecycle.md` — same gap class as L140-L142
close_reason: Extended doctrine sync to managed docs/scripts/plists, wired doctor drift probe, applied across fleet, and verified zero managed drift/errors.

title: [dispatch-v2-wrapper] implement schema v2 canonical dispatch wrapper
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/dispatch-enforcement-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-pnr9
Plan predecessor: flywheel-8i5

## Goal

Upgrade `/flywheel:dispatch` to emit schema v2 dispatch receipts and prove every dispatch was produced by the canonical wrapper.

## Scope

- Patch `/Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/dispatch.md`.
- Add `dispatch_skill_version=flywheel-dispatch/v2`.
- Require topology resolution before choosing worker and callback panes.
- Require `edits_expected=true|false|unknown` and `socraticode_required=true|false` classification.
- Export `FLYWHEEL_DISPATCH_WRAPPER=1` only around the canonical `ntm send`.
- Write schema v2 `dispatch_sent` rows to `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl` before send and update with send result.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `rg -n "dispatch_skill_version=flywheel-dispatch/v2|schema_version.*2|FLYWHEEL_DISPATCH_WRAPPER|edits_expected|socraticode_required" /Users/josh/.claude/commands/flywheel/dispatch.md` proves the wrapper contract landed.
2. A dry-run or fixture dispatch writes a schema v2 row and `tail -1 .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl | jq -e '.schema_version == 2 and .event == "dispatch_sent" and .dispatch_skill_version == "flywheel-dispatch/v2"'` passes.
3. `tail -1 .flywheel/dispatch-log.jsonl | jq -e '.topology_resolved_pane and .topology_role and .callback_pane and .task_sha256 and .channel == "ntm"'` passes for the fixture row.
4. The wrapper records `agent_type`, `worker_substrate`, `pane_state_source`, `socraticode_required`, and `file_reservation_required` as non-null schema v2 fields.
5. Existing legacy dispatch-log rows remain readable; no migration deletes or rewrites old rows during normal dispatch.
6. The command path uses absolute `/Users/josh/.local/bin/ntm` for sends and does not introduce a new transport.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: fixture dispatch produces machine-readable schema v2 row.
- DOCUMENTED: `/flywheel:dispatch` docs name schema v2 and classification fields.
- SURFACED: dispatch-log row exposes wrapper version, topology, and task hash for doctor consumers.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L29, L50, L51, L52, L53, L70, L71, L80.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.
- Memory: `feedback_three_audit_questions_per_surface`.

## Out Of Scope

- No PreToolUse hook blocking in this bead.
- No callback contract validation beyond writing dispatch rows.
- No retroactive backfill of old dispatch-log rows.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented schema v2 canonical dispatch wrapper; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[dispatch-v2-wrapper]`.

title: [codex-PATH-missing] worker panes lack ~/.local/bin; jeff substrate invisible
description:
## Symptom (2026-05-03 22:08Z)
Live codex pane (flywheel:3) probe returned:
`DCG_PROBE_RESULT: PATH_MISSING; /Users/josh/.local/bin/dcg v0.5.1 (which dcg failed)`

Binary exists on disk + works via absolute path. `which dcg` fails in codex
pane shell = ~/.local/bin not in codex's PATH.

## Implications
Every Jeff substrate binary the agent invokes by bare name fails silently in
codex panes: dcg, br, ntm, cm, jsm. This means:
- Codex agents writing "dcg explain ..." in dispatch templates → command not found
- Codex agents calling `br create` → command not found
- Tool calls that shell out to these tools fail in ways the agent may not surface

## Connection to flywheel-delp (fleet-death-rca)
Strong candidate. Pattern of dead panes = clean exit after tool call success.
Hypothesis: agent shells out → tool not found → MCP/codex layer treats some
class of exec failures as fatal → process exits clean.

## Truth checks needed before claiming RCA
1. Capture exit reason for one death with stderr redirected (already in flywheel-delp scope)
2. Verify orchestrator panes (flywheel:1, flywheel:2) DO have ~/.local/bin in
   PATH while worker panes do NOT — that explains differential survival
3. Find where codex panes' PATH gets stripped — likely in launchd plist
   for ai.zeststream.flywheel-flywheel-loop or in the codex launch wrapper

## Acceptance
- ~/.local/bin in PATH for ALL codex panes, orchestrator AND workers
- Verified by ntm send probe responding `which dcg` → /Users/josh/.local/bin/dcg
- launchd plists audited; whichever wrapper strips PATH is fixed

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The launchd or plist artifact named in `flywheel-orx1` validates with `plutil -lint` or an equivalent dry-run.
AG2: A restart or health probe proves the daemon behavior described in this bead.
AG3: The close receipt names the plist path, validation command, and rollback posture.
notes:
# ORX1 H1 vs H3 Diagnosis

## Verdict

`h=H1`

The earlier `PATH_MISSING` probe came from a different shell context inside the same Codex runtime: Codex tool execution uses non-interactive login zsh (`/bin/zsh -lc`), and `/Users/josh/.zprofile` reset PATH in that context. That made `which dcg` fail even though NTM-launched Codex panes could have the Jeff substrate visible in their pane shell.

`H2` is also true after intervention: PATH was fixed between probes by the `.zprofile`, Codex wrapper, NTM template, and launch PATH-order changes. It is not the primary explanation for why the original observation contradicted pane parity.

`H3` is not supported by the current evidence.

## Evidence

- Pre-fix Codex tool probe in flywheel:3 returned `which dcg` failure while `/Users/josh/.local/bin/dcg --version` returned `0.5.1`.
- `/Users/josh/.zprofile` reset PATH to `/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin`, ran `path_helper`, and did not restore `/Users/josh/.local/bin` until the ORX1 fix added:
  `export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"`.
- The mismatched context was reproducible before the fix: a plain zsh command with PATH injected could resolve `dcg`, while a login zsh command (`/bin/zsh -lc`) lost it through `.zprofile`.
- NTM-launched Codex process evidence showed Codex node processes launched from `/Users/josh/.cargo/bin/codex` with user-bin PATH present in the parent environment. That points away from a universal NTM/Codex pane spawn strip.
- Current parity validation from both pane 1 and pane 3 resolves all five binaries:
  `dcg`, `br`, `ntm`, `cm`, `jsm`.
- `e493cca` frozen-pane-detector v2 added `.flywheel/scripts/frozen-pane-detector.sh`; its recovery command is:
  `CODEX_CMD="${FROZEN_PANE_CODEX_CMD:-codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox}"`.
  It does not modify PATH or introduce a separate stripped environment. It relies on whichever pane shell PATH resolves bare `codex`.

## Recommendation

Downgrade `flywheel-orx1` from P0 to P2 and reclassify it as an `orchestrator-probe-discipline` / login-shell bootstrap hygiene issue.

Keep `flywheel-delp` open as fleet-death RCA. ORX1 restored Codex tool-shell substrate visibility and fixed PATH order, but the evidence no longer supports treating ORX1 as the P0 root cause for fleet death.

Do not close `flywheel-orx1` yet; leave it open for orchestrator decision after this refinement.

title: [doctrine-prop] flywheel:doctrine-sync — version-stamped pull from canonical AGENTS template
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07
joshua_directive=2026-05-07T~21Z "stamp all of this into the rest of the fleet"

## Why
Today shipped: L125 (env-file-sealed-substrate), L126 (evidence-pack-replaces-self-grade), close-handler/dispatch-template/worker-tick/worker-orchestration/dispatch-tool-contracts/orchestrator-validation-discipline rewrites, fuckup-heuristics promotion, jeff-issue-chain v1.1, RESHAPED-OUR-SCOPE 5th shape. All landed in `flywheel/` + canonical surfaces. **Rest of fleet (mobile-eats, alpsinsurance, zesttube, skillos, picoz, cfs) does NOT have these yet.**

The canonical-meta-rules-sync three-surface watchdog already exists for *flywheel's own* root/canonical/template parity. What's missing: the cross-repo pull mechanism so peer fleet repos pick up template updates.

## Acceptance
1. `~/.local/bin/flywheel-doctrine-sync` (or `.flywheel/scripts/doctrine-sync.sh` + symlink) — diffs target repo's AGENTS.md against `templates/flywheel-install/AGENTS.md` from flywheel HEAD; surfaces missing L-rules; --apply mode appends them with provenance citation
2. Doctrine version stamp in every fleet repo's `.flywheel/STATE.json` — `doctrine_version: "2026-05-07.L126"` (semver-like: date + highest-L-rule)
3. Doctor probe `doctrine_version_current` lands in `flywheel doctor` — fails if behind template
4. flywheel:onboard checklist gains `doctrine-sync` step (so new onboards never start stale)
5. Per-repo tick reads doctrine-version + auto-pulls when behind (Donella leverage point #5: continuous-sync invariant)
6. Agent Mail broadcast template at `templates/peer-orch-broadcasts/doctrine-update-template.md` for one-shot announcements
7. Run against the 6 fleet sample repos as a verify pass; emit compliance pack per repo

## Hard rules
- dry-run is the default; --apply requires --idempotency-key (mirrors storage-prune.sh, session-residue-prune.sh contract)
- refuses to write to a repo without `.flywheel/` (not flywheel-onboarded)
- never overwrites local L-rules; only appends missing ones with `# Pulled from flywheel/templates/flywheel-install/AGENTS.md@<sha>` provenance
- per-repo CASCADE not BULK — script runs against ONE repo at a time, fails-closed if anything mismatches schema
- compliance pack on close per post-x6ok8 contract (no four-lens emission)

## Cross-refs
- canonical-meta-rules-sync skill (existing flywheel-internal three-surface watchdog)
- /flywheel:onboard (per-repo install)
- /flywheel:tick step 1a awareness check (per-repo doctrine reading)
- L126 evidence-pack-replaces-self-grade (the load-bearing rule we're propagating)
- session-residue-prune.sh (safety contract template)

title: [jeff-track-ntm-114-followup] robot activity still diverges from work/health gate
description:
## Goal
Track the remaining ntm robot-surface convergence failure after #114: `--robot-activity` reports live Codex panes as `THINKING` while `--robot-is-working` and `--robot-agent-health` report the same panes safe/healthy.

## Context
Jeff fixed the original #114 mis-typing bug in `f4506605` by passing tmux-tracked agent type into `agent.Parser.ParseWithHint`. Local `ntm` includes that commit, and dogfood confirms the type now converges to Codex-ish (`cod`), but the scheduling gate still disagrees on work state.

Live receipts from `flywheel-4d4a`:
- `/tmp/flywheel-4d4a-robot-activity-r2.json`: panes 2/3/4 have `agent_type=codex`, `state=THINKING`, patterns include `codex_esc_interrupt` plus `codex_working` or `codex_waiting_background`.
- `/tmp/flywheel-4d4a-robot-is-working-r2.json`: same panes have `agent_type=cod`, `is_working=false`, `is_idle=true`, `recommendation=SAFE_TO_RESTART`.
- `/tmp/flywheel-4d4a-robot-agent-health-r2.json`: same panes have `local_state.is_working=false`, `local_state.is_idle=true`, `recommendation=HEALTHY`.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: ntm#114 thread, local ntm source at `/Users/josh/Developer/ntm`, receipts under `/tmp/flywheel-4d4a-robot-*.json`.
OUTPUTS: upstream follow-up/reopen or local doctrine identifying which robot surface is authoritative for scheduling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Re-run `ntm --robot-activity=flywheel --panes=2,3,4`, `ntm --robot-is-working=flywheel --panes=2,3,4`, and `ntm --robot-agent-health=flywheel --panes=2,3,4 --no-caut` in a controlled active Codex pane case.
- If activity reports `THINKING` or `GENERATING`, then either is-working reports `is_working=true`/`DO_NOT_INTERRUPT`, or the CLI documentation explicitly marks `--robot-activity` as the scheduling authority.
- If agent-health uses `GetIsWorking()`, it must not report `HEALTHY` / ready-for-work for a pane where the authoritative activity surface has current positive busy evidence.
- File or update the upstream issue with a source trace if the mismatch reproduces on current main.
- Preserve machine-readable receipts for every probe.

## Testing Obligations
- JSON receipts from all three robot surfaces.
- Source trace covering `internal/robot/is_working.go`, `internal/agent/parser.go`, `internal/robot/activity.go`, and `internal/robot/agent_health.go`.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: none unless a local integration/doc patch is required. Close reason must include VALIDATED/DOCUMENTED/SURFACED and the upstream issue/comment URL or explicit no-upstream reason.

Filed upstream ntm#133. Receipt .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-p4nu-receipt.md. DIDNT: no local doctrine patch because mismatch persists upstream. title: ntm-stale-error-text-classifier-issue
description:
[ntm-stale-error-text-classifier-issue] file ntm issue: classifier reads stale ERROR/api_error text from scrollback, produces persistent ERROR state until pane is poked

## Trauma class
ntm-classifier-stale-error-poisoning — recurring 2026-05-04 (3rd observation today): skillos:p2 (failed_text), flywheel:p4 (api_error), prior: flywheel sessions earlier today.

## Symptom
- Pane content settles at codex_chevron_prompt (truly idle, awaiting input)
- BUT capture window still contains old "failed" / "api_error" / similar text from prior dispatch run output
- Classifier reads BOTH patterns: `[failed_text|api_error, codex_chevron_prompt]`
- Decision: state=ERROR (because error pattern wins over chevron)
- Reality: pane is alive and idle — sending a fresh prompt makes the agent respond instantly
- Ping clears scrollback, classifier reads only chevron, state→WAITING

## Why this matters
- Watcher v4 correctly filters on state==WAITING and skips ERROR (fail-closed, good)
- Result: pane appears persistently idle to humans ("pane 4 idle - why no dispatch?")
- Workaround required: manual ping per pane every time error text scrolls in
- Already happened 3x in <1h on this Mac — high frequency

## Proposed fix (for upstream ntm issue body)
Classifier should weight patterns by RECENCY relative to `state_since`:
- If `failed_text` / `api_error` was last NEW in capture >5min ago AND `codex_chevron_prompt` is NEWER → state=WAITING (idle at prompt after stale error scrolled past)
- If `failed_text` / `api_error` is fresh (within last minute or new since last probe) → state=ERROR (genuine recent failure)
- Use line-position / scroll-distance heuristic: error pattern at top of capture window = old, chevron at bottom = current truth

Alternative: add `--debounce-error-by-prompt-recency` flag opt-in to test this without breaking existing behavior.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: file ntm issue at Dicklesworthstone/ntm with title "classifier persistent-ERROR false-positive when stale failed_text or api_error in capture scrollback above current chevron prompt"
AG2: cite 3 observed cases (skillos:p2 03:18Z, flywheel:p4 03:26Z, earlier flywheel today) with capture-pane evidence
AG3: include proposed fix sketch (recency-weighted pattern resolution)
AG4: file flywheel-side workaround script: poll every 5min for ERROR panes, send no-op `ping` to clear scrollback, recheck — auto-recovery layer until upstream lands
AG5: AGENTS.md L## entry "L## STALE-ERROR-TEXT-AUTO-PING-RECOVERY"
AG6: receipt at /tmp/ntm-stale-error-evidence.md cites: ntm issue URL + workaround script path

## Per the new contract: callback MUST report did/didnt/gaps
- DID: each AG with citation
- DIDNT: AGs you can't meet (file gap-bead before callback)
- GAPS: any newly-discovered failure modes (file gap-beads)

## Callback envelope
/Users/josh/.local/bin/ntm send flywheel --pane=1 "DONE flywheel-stale-error task_id=<id> did=<n>/6 didnt=<bead-ids-or-none> gaps=<bead-ids-or-none> evidence=/tmp/ntm-stale-error-evidence.md tests=PASS callback_delivery_verified=true"

## Skills cited
- jeff-issue-chain (file upstream, don't patch)
- canonical-cli-scoping (auto-ping recovery flag surface)

title: [parity-probe-binary] implement flywheel-toolset-parity CLI
description:
Implement the toolset-parity-probe binary at ~/.local/bin/flywheel-toolset-parity.

Surface (canonical-cli-scoping compliant):
  flywheel-toolset-parity check --runtime={claude,codex,both} --json
  flywheel-toolset-parity doctor
  flywheel-toolset-parity --help / --info / --examples / --schema

Probe per cell (tool × runtime):
- found: which <tool> exits 0
- abs_path: resolved binary path
- version: <tool> --version output (parsed)
- hook_wired: Claude → grep ~/.claude/settings.json hooks; Codex → grep ~/.codex/config.toml + hook scripts in PATH
- smoke_ok: tool-specific smoke (e.g. dcg test, br --version, ntm --version, cm --version)

Output: ~/.local/state/flywheel/toolset-parity-<ts>.json with full matrix
Exit codes: 0 uniform, 1 drift_runtime, 2 drift_version, 3 unreachable_runtime

Codex-pane probing uses ntm send + ntm tail with 90s timeout; if unresponsive, that runtime cell is `runtime_unresponsive` not blank.

Parent: flywheel-2p25
notes: 2026-05-04T08:08:06Z: four-lens BLOCK_CLOSE. 3 open children (flywheel-75py, flywheel-m1j4, flywheel-2p25) + public_lens=no_bar_self_grade. brand+sniff+jeff PASS. did=7/7 tests=PASS. Note: flywheel-2p25 now blocking 3+ parents today — recurring hub blocker.

comments.author: ChartreuseCave
comments.text:
Constraint from `flywheel-cnep` / L69 ORCH-PROBE-AGENT-CONTEXT:

The Codex column in `flywheel-toolset-parity` MUST probe through Codex agent execution context, not an orchestrator shell or pane PATH snapshot. Required schema shape for each Codex cell:

- `agent_context={found,abs_path,realpath,version,smoke_ok,probe_transport,callback_received}`
- `orchestrator_shell_context={found,abs_path,realpath,version}` only as a companion diagnostic
- `context_drift=true` when the two contexts disagree
- `runtime_unresponsive` when the Codex agent does not callback within timeout; raw-shell fallback is forbidden

Implementation remains in q03g scope; this comment only adds the doctrinal constraint.

title: [autoloop-p1-config-tests] add config hermetic tests and 10min cadence
description:
Plan source: `/Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/autonomous-loop-2026-04-30.md`

## Scope

Add Phase 1 config, tests, and 10-minute launchd cadence after scan/queue proves
safe.

## Required Behavior

- Create `~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/config/autoloop.json` or canonical
  equivalent with scoring, cooldown, budgets, and repo allowlist settings.
- Add hermetic tests for STOP behavior, no-ready-repo, ready-repo queue, dry-run,
  and lock behavior.
- Update the LaunchAgent interval from 1800 to 600 only after tests pass.
- Preserve Phase 0 no-agent-wakeup invariant.

## Acceptance Gates

1. `jq '.schema_version and .cooldowns and .budgets' <config>` succeeds.

   dry-run, and lock cases.

   passes after interval change.
4. `grep -A1 StartInterval ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.flywheel-autoloop.plist`
   proves 600-second cadence.
5. Launchd kickstart still produces structured `last_run.json`.

## DOD

Close reason shape:
`Added Phase 1 config/tests/cadence; tests=<cmd>; config=<path>; plist=<path>`.

## Out Of Scope

Dispatching agents, queue consumption, auto-spawn.

## Three-Q

- VALIDATED: hermetic tests plus launchd lint/kickstart.
- DOCUMENTED: config schema and cadence change are explicit.
- SURFACED: later doctor/status bead reads these config and cadence values.

title: [doctrine] apply agent-ergonomics skill to our own .flywheel/scripts substrate
description:
## Source

Jeffrey Emanuel released /agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools — a 189-file, 1.5MB skill for converting CLIs from human-first to agent-first via 11-dim scoring + recommendation loop. Already in our skill registry (Joshua paid $20/mo). Joshua shared his announcement piece + GPT-5.5 review 2026-05-08T~21:50Z.

## Why apply to our substrate

Our flywheel runs on CLIs that workers + orchestrators + watchdogs invoke constantly:
- `flywheel-loop`, `dispatch-and-verify.sh`, `build-dispatch-packet.sh`
- `validate-callback.py`, `tmp-aggressive-prune.sh`, `flywheel-doctor`
- `frozen-pane-detector-fleet.sh`, `peer-orch-respawn-permit.sh`
- 50+ scripts across `.flywheel/scripts/`

If we apply the skill to even the top-10 most-invoked, we'd:
- Surface agent-friction patterns we've been hand-treating
- Generate `agent_surfaces.jsonl` baseline for time-series tracking
- Get specific 11-dim scores + ranked recommendations per script
- Catch wrong-but-legible invocation failure modes (exact LODP false-negatives we've been seeing)

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Inventory top-10 most-invoked CLIs in our substrate. Rank by dispatch-log frequency × failure-rate × blast-radius (skill's own ranking heuristic).

AG2: Run skill against the top-3 (likely: `flywheel-loop`, `dispatch-and-verify.sh`, `build-dispatch-packet.sh`). Generate `agent_surfaces.jsonl` + `recommendations.jsonl` per tool.

AG3: Apply changes auto-mode for low-blast-radius recommendations; surface high-blast-radius for Joshua review.

AG4: Re-score post-changes. Confirm fresh-agent simulation (subagent without prior context) successfully invokes each tool first-try.

AG5: Doctrine: `.flywheel/doctrine/agent-ergonomics-application-baseline-2026-05-08.md` capturing 11-dim scores + applied changes + re-score deltas.

AG6: Establish quarterly re-audit cadence — agent-ergonomics is a time-series, not one-off.

## Strategic angle

This skill IS the meta-skill for our entire flywheel substrate. Every dispatch packet, every wrapper script, every doctor tool either does or doesn't pass agent-first design. Applying systematically gives us:
- Compounding leverage: one rubric → 50+ improved tools → fleet-wide friction reduction
- Cross-orch propagation: doctrine-sync 6h cadence pushes improved scripts to alps/mobile-eats/skillos
- New-orch onboarding: future workers face cleaner tooling, climb learning curve faster

## Cross-references

- Jeffrey's piece (shared by Joshua 2026-05-08T21:50Z)
- GPT-5.5 review of skill in same context
- Memory: `feedback_canonical_cli_at_dispatch` (canonical-cli-scoping enforcement at dispatch)
- Skill: `canonical-cli-scoping` (our existing CLI discipline skill — complementary)
- Skill: `world-class-doctor-mode-for-cli-tools` (Jeffrey's prior CLI doctor skill, already applied)
- Memory: `feedback_jeffrey_emanuel_name_preference` (Jeffrey not Jeff in human-facing surfaces)
close_reason: Applied agent-ergonomics baseline to flywheel CLI substrate: top-10 inventory, top-3 artifacts, low-blast dispatch-and-verify introspection patch, doctrine cadence, and follow-up for high-blast surfaces.

title: [data-backed-deferral-receipts] implement save and override JSONL receipts
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/data-backed-deferral-guard-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-bbts
Plan predecessor: flywheel-28v

## Goal

Implement append-only save and override receipts for data-backed deferral so prevented Joshua questions become durable learning rows instead of scrollback-only wins.

## Scope

- Write prevented-deferral rows to `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-saves.jsonl` using the plan shape from lines 238-265.
- Write allow/override rows to `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-overrides.jsonl` using the plan shape from lines 193-208.
- Suppress duplicate save rows with the same `draft_question` and `suggested_action` inside a 10-minute window.
- Preserve append-only behavior; a later dispatch failure must not delete the save row.

## Acceptance Gates

1. A test fixture run creates or validates a save row with `event=="data_backed_deferral_prevented"` in `~/.local/state/flywheel/data-backed-deferral-saves.jsonl` or a temp-path equivalent.
2. `jq -e 'select(.event=="data_backed_deferral_prevented") | .ts and .session and .draft_question and .suggested_action and (.signals_aligned | length >= 3)' <receipt-file>` passes against the save receipt.
3. An allow fixture creates or validates an override row with `event=="data_backed_deferral_override"` and `reason` in `evidence_missing,cost,source_scope,reject_revert,require_confirm,live_prod,tie_between`.
4. Duplicate-suppression test runs the same prevented-deferral case twice and proves only one matching save row appears inside the 10-minute window.
5. `rg -n "data-backed-deferral-saves|data-backed-deferral-overrides|data_backed_deferral_prevented|data_backed_deferral_override" ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop .flywheel/scripts tests` finds implementation and tests.
6. Receipt writing supports temp-path injection for tests so test runs do not depend on mutating the live JSONL files.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: tests prove save, override, duplicate suppression, and append-only posture.
- DOCUMENTED: receipt field schema is described in help or adjacent docs.
- SURFACED: downstream tick/doctor surfaces can count saves and violations without parsing prose.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L56 for promotion ladder evidence linkage.
- AGENTS.md L71 and L80 for validation receipts and DID/DIDNT/GAPS evidence.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.

## Out Of Scope

- No doctor JSON field wiring; that belongs to the doctor surface bead.
- No dispatch-template lint changes.
- No deletion or compaction of existing receipt logs.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented data-backed-deferral save/override receipts; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-receipts]`.
close_reason: implemented data-backed-deferral save/override receipts; tests=bash tests/data-backed-deferral-guard.sh,bash -n ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop,bash -n tests/data-backed-deferral-guard.sh,rg acceptance grep; commit_tag=[data-backed-deferral-receipts]

title: [doctrine-prop] skillos cohort plan: 75 L-rules in 4-batch waves with cross-orch handoff
description:
mission_anchor=continuous-orchestrator-uptime-self-sustaining-fleet
plan_origin=doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07
parent=flywheel-owirq

## Why
4 fleet repos synced cleanly today (alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, zesttube, polymarket-pico-z all at doctrine_version=2026-05-07.L126). skillos is the outlier — 75 L-rules missing (L29, L35, L48-L73, L75-L107, L110-L111, L115-L126). Bulk-applying 75 L-rules to a peer orchestrator's substrate without phased review is risky; some rules may conflict with skillos's local doctrine, and the orchestrator session is in active use.

## Acceptance
1. /flywheel:plan a 4-wave cohort:
   - Wave 1 (foundation): L29 + L35 + L48-L57 (~12 rules)
   - Wave 2 (substrate): L58-L73 (~15 rules)  
   - Wave 3 (recovery+dispatch): L75-L99 (~25 rules)
   - Wave 4 (recent): L100-L126 (~23 rules including today's L125+L126)
2. Per-wave: cross-orch handoff to skillos:1 via Agent Mail asking for L-rule conflict review BEFORE doctrine-sync --apply
3. Per-wave: skillos:1 reviews + acks + flywheel:1 runs --apply with separate idempotency key
4. Per-wave: skillos:1 verifies post-apply on their next tick + confirms no doctrine conflicts
5. Final: skillos doctrine_version stamps at 2026-05-07.L126
6. compliance pack on close per post-x6ok8 contract

## Hard rules
- NO bulk apply (75 rules at once) — would overwhelm skillos:1's review capacity + risk silent doctrine conflicts
- Cross-orch handoff REQUIRED at every wave boundary
- L107 + canonical-meta-rules-sync three-surface watchdog must pass after each wave
- skillos:1 has veto on any individual L-rule that conflicts with their local doctrine — flywheel:1 documents the deferral, never overrides
- Compliance pack per wave (4 packs total, not 1 bulk)

## Cross-refs
- flywheel-owirq (parent: doctrine-sync infrastructure)
- /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/doctrine-propagation-2026-05-07/01-VERIFY-PASS.json (skillos missing list)
- L107 shared-surface reservation
- canonical-meta-rules-sync three-surface watchdog
- Cross-orch protocol per Agent Mail

## Acceptance gates
AG1: The artifact, command, or doctrine surface named in `[doctrine-prop] skillos cohort plan: 75 L-rules in 4-batch waves with cross-orch handoff` is updated with close evidence.
AG2: A targeted test, dry-run, or validator command passes and is named in the close receipt.
AG3: `br show flywheel-rdqc7` remains open or in_progress until the evidence artifact exists.

title: [skill-bug] agent-ergonomics-... skill name 76 chars exceeds codex 64-char limit
description:
## Source

Joshua's purchased skill from jeffreys-skills.md fails to load on every codex 0.125 / 0.129 startup with:

```
⚠ /Users/josh/.codex/skills/agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools/SKILL.md:
  invalid name: exceeds maximum length of 64 characters
⚠ /Users/josh/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools/SKILL.md:
  invalid name: exceeds maximum length of 64 characters
```

Skill name from SKILL.md frontmatter:
`agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools` = 76 chars

## Workaround

Symlink with shorter name:
```bash
ln -s agent-ergonomics-and-agent-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools \
  ~/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics
```

This may or may not work depending on whether codex resolves symlinks before validating name. If frontmatter `name:` is what's checked, need to also patch SKILL.md frontmatter to a shorter alias.

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Verify whether codex's 64-char limit applies to:
- Directory name
- SKILL.md frontmatter `name:` field
- Both
Determines workaround shape.

AG2: Apply workaround locally — shorten name to fit. Suggested: `agent-ergonomics-cli` (already exists at ~/.claude/skills/agent-ergonomics-cli/ — different/older skill).
Or: `agent-ergonomics-max` (20 chars).

AG3: File issue at jeffreys-skills.md / Dicklesworthstone/jeffreys-skills (or wherever the skill ships from) noting the name length collides with codex 64-char limit.

AG4: Document the codex 64-char limit as a SKILL-NAMING-CONSTRAINT canonical L-rule for our own skills (avoid future authoring trip).

## Cross-references

- Jeffrey announcement piece (Joshua shared 2026-05-08T~21:50Z)
- Memory: `feedback_jeffrey_emanuel_name_preference` (Jeffrey not Jeff)
- canonical-cli-scoping skill (audit subject of meta-application — being audited via this skill)
close_reason: Fixed: verified Codex limit applies to SKILL.md frontmatter name, confirmed local short-name workaround, filed upstream issue https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/jeffreysprompts.com/issues/5, documented L150 SKILL-NAMING-CONSTRAINT on canonical doctrine surfaces and README; evidence .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-rzgqc/skill-name-findings.md

title: [skillos-discovery-coordinator] implement fleet skill discovery promotion ladder
description:
Plan source: /Users/josh/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/PLANS/fleet-skill-reporting-2026-05-01.md
Parent decomposition bead: flywheel-xbuh

## Goal

Implement the skillos coordinator reader that groups `skill-discoveries.jsonl` rows and applies the 1/2/3/5-sighting promotion ladder.

## Scope

- Add skillos-owned coordinator tick logic or a reusable flywheel script that skillos invokes.
- Read the last 24h of `~/.local/state/flywheel/skill-discoveries.jsonl`.
- Group by `candidate_skill_name`, with exact match first and fuzzy-match only when explicitly safe.
- Ladder:
  - 1 sighting: log only.
  - 2 sightings: notify/create Agent Mail thread candidate.
  - 3 sightings: create skill-builder bead if one does not exist.
  - 5 sightings: dispatch skillos worker when bead is open and capacity exists.
- Emit a `fleet-skill-pulse` row to `~/.local/state/flywheel/team-pulse.jsonl`.

## Acceptance Gates

1. Coordinator command supports `--dry-run --json` and reports grouped candidates without mutating Beads or Agent Mail.
2. Fixture with one sighting returns action `log_only`.
3. Fixture with two sightings returns action `notify_skillos` or `agent_mail_thread_needed`.
4. Fixture with three sightings runs `br create --dry-run` in dry-run mode and reports a planned skill-builder bead with consolidated evidence.
5. Apply mode at three sightings requires an idempotency key and creates at most one skill-builder bead for the same candidate on repeated runs.
6. Fixture with five sightings reports `dispatch_worker_needed` and includes the target skill-builder bead id if one exists.
7. Coordinator writes a valid `fleet-skill-pulse` row with candidate counts and pending action count.

## Three-Q Audit

- VALIDATED: dry-run/apply/idempotency fixtures cover all ladder thresholds.
- DOCUMENTED: coordinator help describes thresholds and ownership by skillos.
- SURFACED: pulse rows and planned bead ids make skill promotion visible outside one pane.

## Doctrine References

- AGENTS.md L52, L55, L56, L61, L70, L71.
- Plan lines 75-101 for ladder and skillos HQ role.
- Memory: `feedback_orchestrator_must_finish_p0_before_filing_more`.

## Out Of Scope

- No Agent Mail thread implementation beyond emitting planned action.
- No live NTM dispatch send; dispatch is a separate notification/dispatch bead.
- No supervised quality scoring for discoveries.

## DOD

Close with reason shape: `implemented skillos skill-discovery coordinator ladder; tests=<commands>; commit_tag=[skillos-discovery-coordinator]`.
